ref: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
text: A ba n nɩ: nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ k'a n yar dɔ bɩ jɩsɩ sim, an nyɩ yɩ yar, n y'a tɔ karɛ Emanʊwɛl, a jɩ lɛ taa k'a hɩ, a ʊ: Woso ɩ kan wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
text: Zozɩyas n Zekonɩyas kan a dannɔ kɩ yɩ. A n a ga kʊ ʊ kan *Babɩlɔnnɩ lɔmbaa tarɛ dɔmɩm bɩ kɩ.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Azɔɔr n Sadɔk yɩ, Sadɔk n Akim yɩ, Akim n Eliyud yɩ,

ref: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
text: Yeezuu yɩrɛ bɩ zaa ba n nɩ: A da Maarii bɩ, nyɩnta a ʊ Zozɛf jaan, bɩ, an lɛɛ ka kʊ Maarii n a ba Zozɛf lʊ bɩ, *Sɛnt-Ɛspri a ka Maarii n jɩsɩ sa.

ref: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
text: A jaan Zozɛf bɩ kʊ gʊaa mɩŋŋa m bɩ, a n a ŋʊa k'a a da nyannɩ ʊ y. A n a hɔɔn da k'a wɔ saa kʊ gɔsɩ b'a dɔ y.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: K'a n'a hɔɔn darɛ ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, Zuuba Malɛɛka bʊr zi nɩnnarɛ nɔ ʊ, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Zozɛf, Davɩd do gʊaa, ɩ bɩ nyi ba k'ɩ Maarii sa ɩ ba ɩ lʊ y, bala, Sɛnt-Ɛspri paŋŋa y'a ka, an nɩ jɩsɩ bɩ m,

ref: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
text: a nyɩ yɩm yar, k'ɩ tɔ ka Yeezuu, bala, a m n'a do bɩ bʊmbɔrɛ a mimbʊnyaarɔ nɔ ʊ.»

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Naa do woo… naa, ba ʊ kʊ meerbaa kʊ Woso a ba kan a lɛsinnɩsorazaa lɛ kɩ bɩ n kʊ yɩ.

ref: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
text: Zozɛf k'a bɩ gu hinceem ma bɩ, a hɔ kʊ Zuuba malɛɛka b'a hɩ m bɩ ba: A Maarii gaa, an a ba a lʊ,

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: bɩ, a n a dɔ lʊ y, an ta an aa dɔmɩm k'a nyɩyar bɩ yɩ ʊ bɩ ku, an a tɔ ka Yeezuu.

ref: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
text: Yeezuu bɩ, yɩ a ʊ Betelehɛm ʊ Zidee kʊ ʊ, cir Hɩrɔɔdɩ dɔmɩm ʊ, bɩ hɔdonno a to dɔjɩr ʊ, ŋnɩ bʊr Zerizalɛm ʊ,

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: ŋnɩ laaka da: «Zɩɩfʊrɔ cir k'a yɩ b'ɩ ka? Wɔɔ monyaa yɩ dɔjɩr ʊ bɩ y'a ka, wɔɔ n bʊr k'ʊ b'a da ʊ.»

ref: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a naa ma bɩ, a da bɔ ʊ kan Zerizalɛm zannɔ kɩ haay.

ref: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
text: A n wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ wɔɔ so kʊ ma kan wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ, an laaka da ŋ han kʊ niŋŋə kʊ Krista bɩ n nɩ yɩŋ ʊ bɩ ma.

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Brɔɔ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: Betelehɛm ʊ Zidee kʊ ʊ, bala, hɔ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa b'a hɩ bɩ n naa do.

ref: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
text: B'ɩbɩɩ Betelehɛm, Zidee kʊ, ɩbɩɩ bɛɛ m poore Zidee kʊrɔ gʊtɔɔ wɔɔ barla y. Bala, cir bɔrɛ ɩbɩɩ bireyaa ʊ, an ka Israyɛl mɔɔ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ lɛɛ.

ref: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
text: A m bɩ, Hɩrɔɔdɩ n hɔdonno wɔɔ bir sɛɛrɛ, an laaka da ŋ han dɔmɩm kʊ monyaa bɩ bɔ ʊ bɩ ma.

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: A n ŋ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ, an n'a hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a ta a laaka da nyɩ bɩ mim ma mɩŋŋa, k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩ, k'a a wusigə a b'a hɩ mɔɔ m, kʊ m ta m aa da ʊ sɔ.»

ref: The wise men listened to what the king said and then left. And the star they had seen in the east went on ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
text: Cir bɩ k'a naa hɩ bɩ, ŋ y'a hʊr, ta kʊ ŋ nawʊm bɩ, monyaa kʊ ŋ lɛɛ ka ŋn'a yɩ dɔjɩr b'ɩ tarɛ ŋ lɛɛ, an ta an aa jɩm lɛɛ kʊ nyɩ bɩ n ta ʊ bɩ burə ʊ.

ref: They were thrilled and excited to see the star.
text: Monyaa bɩ kʊ ŋ y'a yɩ bɩ, ŋ heer yɩ nyɩnta gʊta.

ref: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
text: Ŋ gasʊ cɛ b'ʊ, ŋn'aa nyɩ bɩ yɩ kan a da Maarii kɩ, ŋnɩ kukurə zɛ a taa, ŋnɩ yaa da m, bɩ jɛ ʊ, ŋn'a mɔdrɔ wɔɔ hʊr, ŋnɩ gan wʊ ma: Sarma m kan tidəər nyisi kɩ, *mɩɩr kɩ.

ref: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
text: Bɩ Woso a hɩ ŋ nɩ nɩnnarɛ nɔ ʊ, a ʊ: ŋ b'a ya, ŋnɩ bɔ Hɩrɔɔdɩ zi y. Naa minto bɩ, brɔɔ zaa vanta sa ŋnɩ doo a kʊ ʊ. Eziptɩ kʊ tarɛ

ref: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
text: A hʊr kʊ ŋ y'a ʊ bɩ, Zuuba malɛɛka a hɩnka Zozɛf m nɩnnarɛ nɔ ʊ, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Wuti ɩ nyɩ bɩ gaa kan a da bɩ kɩ, ɩ ɩ si, ɩ ta Eziptɩ ʊ, nyɩnta b'ʊ k'an ta an aa dɔmɩm kʊ mɔɔ n nɩ m yarɛ m meer ba kan ɩbɩɩ kɩ bɩ ku, bala, Hɩrɔɔdɩ y'a kam nyɩ bɩ ma, k'a zɛ.»

ref: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
text: Zozɛf n wuti, an nyɩ bɩ kan a da bɩ kɩ gaa gunuu ʊ, an a hʊr an ta Eziptɩ ʊ.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: A gɔɔta b'ʊ an ta, an Hɩrɔɔdɩ garɛ ku, kʊ Woso meerbaa k'a ba kan a lɛsinnɩsorazaa lɛ kɩ bɩ n kʊ yɩ. Mɔɔ m Nyɩ bɩ bir, k'an bɔ Eziptɩ ʊ. Betelehɛm nyɩnɔ poorero zɩnzɛrɛ

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a b'a dɔ a ʊ, hɔdonno wɔɔ a yamba a m bɩ, a wuti nɔmaa gandaara m. A n gʊɔɔ nyɔɔ Betelehɛm ʊ kan kʊrɔ vantɔɔ k'a a so ŋ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'aa nyɩnɔ poorero yaarɔ kʊ ŋ dɔɔraa hɩɩya gɛɛ ŋ n a ku rɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ. Amba hɔdonno wɔɔ a hɩ m, nyɩnta k'a ʊ, an laaka da k'a nyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ dɔmɩm dɔ bɩ ma.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Naa ma bɩ, hɔ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa Zeremii a hɩ bɩ kʊ yɩ, a ʊ:

ref: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
text: N zeedarɛ ma Ramaa ʊ, nyikəre kan heerzarlɛ gʊta kɩ: Rasɛl nɩ nyi karɛ a nyɩnɔ minto, a lɛ baa kʊ n n'a wɔ zɩnzɛ a nɩ y, nyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ gɩnga bɩ tɔ ma. Zozɛf bɔ Eziptɩ ʊ

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ garɛ jɛ ʊ bɩ, Zuuba malɛɛka a hɩnka Zozɛf m nɩnnarɛ nɔ ʊ Eziptɩ ʊ.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: A n meer ba kan kɩ, a ʊ: «Wuti, ɩ nyɩ bɩ kan a da bɩ kɩ gaa ɩ ɩ wusigə, ɩ ta Israyɛl tara ra. Bala, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ n'a kam ma k'a nyɩ bɩ meeyaa bɔ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ gɩnga.»

ref: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
text: Zozɛf n wuti, an nyɩ bɩ kan a da bɩ kɩ gaa, an ta Israyɛl ʊ.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Bɩ, Zozɛf k'a a ma, n ʊ, Arkelayus nɩ cirbəə m Zidee ʊ a zɩ Hɩrɔɔdɩ bɩncɛ ʊ bɩ, nyibəə a nyasʊ k'an ta b'ʊ bɩ kɩ, bɩ Woso meer ba kan kɩ nɩnnarɛ nɔ ʊ, an ta Galilee kʊ ʊ.

ref: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
text: A n aa nyɩnta kʊ kʊ n n'a hɩm ma Nazarɛtɩ ʊ b'ʊ, k'an bɩr yɩrɛ ɩ ʊ hɔ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ a hɩ bɩ n kʊ yɩ, ŋ ʊ: N y'a birm Nazarɛtɩ gʊaa.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: K'a zibəə k'a n'a hɩnkarɛ a ʊ, awɔɔ heerlʊrlɛ yɩ bɩ ba.

ref: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
text: A n n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «K'a a bɔ a taam, k'a sugur yɛ, bala, Woso cirbəə b'a zɔ.»

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Zaan bɩ, yoonde ka huu a a du a ma, an kʊmbar sa an a so a ʊ, bɩsɩnɔ kan zɔɔn kɩ n a hɔbɩrɛ.

ref: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
text: A m naa, Zerizalɛm, Zidee kʊ bɩ haay, kɔ k'a tɔ m Zʊrdɛn kʊrɔ wɔɔ haay nɩ tam zi,

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: ŋ n'a mimbʊnyaarɔ hɩrɛ, Zaan nɩ batɛm karɛ ŋ ʊ Zʊrdɛn kɔ ʊ.

ref: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
text: K'a farizɛɛnrɔ kan sadisɛɛnrɔ kɩ yɩ ŋn'ɩ zɛm gʊta batɛm sire bɩncɛ ʊ bɩ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Mɩnnɔ kʊsɩrɔ do, nka y'a hɩ awɔɔ m, a ʊ, a a si Woso nɔmaa gʊta k'a nɩ zɛm bɩ lɛɛ?

ref: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
text: Bɩ dɩɩtaa naa, n sɩga banka k'an gɔrɔ zɩnzɛ ra ŋ məsi ma, gɔ niŋŋə k'a bɩr nyɩ mɩŋŋa darɛ y, n y'a zɛm la, n n a da sɛ ʊ.» Hi batɛm kan sɛ batɛm kɩ

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: A y'a gʊr m a wɔ ʊ, a y'a tʊyaa waarɛ an a hɔyaa gʊr an a zar sɩ ʊ, bɩ, a sɛ k'a bɩr lihim bɩ nyam puur b'ʊ.» Yeezuu batɛm si (Markɩ

ref: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
text: A m bɩ, Yeezuu n a to Galilee ʊ, an bʊr Zʊrdɛn ʊ Zaan zi, k'a bɩ batɛm si.

ref: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
text: Bɩ, Zaan a gu an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ y'ɩ ga m, ɩ batɛm ka mɔɔ ʊ, b'ɩbɩɩ ɩ ya, ɩ nɩ zɛm mɔɔ zi!»

ref: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «A to k'an yɩ ncɩnaaʊ naa ma, bala, naa n'a karɛ, wɔɔ n hɔ haay k'an tɩrga Woso ma bɩ ba.» Zaan n yɛ kɛɛrɛ.

ref: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
text: Yeezuu k'a batɛm bɩ si bɩ, a bɔ hi b'ʊ. A m bɩ, brama n a gʊ, an Woso Ɛspri yɩ an nɩ zerle, am cirpʊʊrɛ hɔ bɩ m an bɩ zer la.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Leer n a to mim ʊ an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «Ba mɔɔ Nyɩ m na. Mɔɔ ŋʊa gʊta. A mɔɔ nɔ yɩ paan….»

ref: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
text: Yeezuu k'a a ma, n ʊ, n Zaan du lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ bɩ, a a hʊr, an ta Galilee ʊ.

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: A n gɔɔta Nazarɛtɩ ʊ y. A hʊr a ʊ, an aa nyɩnta Kapɛrnawum ʊ. Kʊ m bɩ, an nɩ Galilee higʊta lɛ ra Zabɩlɔn kan Nɛftalii kɩ kʊrɔ wɔɔ barla.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Sʊtaana n a zɔ ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Kʊ Woso Nyɩ n'ɩbɩɩ m, k'ɩ ka kʊ jaarɔ nɔɔn duro n a ba hɔbɩrɛ.»

ref: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «N y'a gʊrsɩra ba sɔ, n ʊ: Ɩ bɩ da Zuuba ɩ Woso lɛ ʊ y.»

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Sʊtaana n a ya, an ta m ci gʊta mim ʊ, an durnya cirbəəro kʊrɔ hɩnka m kan ŋ paŋŋa kɩ,

ref: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
text: an a hɩ, a ʊ: «K'ɩbɩɩ kukurə zɛ mɔɔ taa, ɩ mɔɔ da ʊ, mɔɔ naa haay karɛ ɩbɩɩ ʊ.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Yeezuu a nyɩ bɔ nʊ, a ʊ: «Ɩ jɛ bɔ mɔɔ jɩr ʊ, Sʊtaana! Bala, n y'a gʊrsɩra ba, n ʊ: Zuuba Woso y'a deem ɩbɩɩ nɩ yaa dam nɩ, ɩ da ʊ.»

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
text: Yɩ a ʊ ncɩnaaʊ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa Ezayii meerbaarɔ nɔɔn duro n kʊ yɩ.

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: Zabɩlɔn tara, Nɛftalii tara, higʊta zaa, kʊrɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ Zʊrdɛn kɔ gəm la kan doro vantɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ Galilee ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, ŋ y'a zaŋŋarɔ wɔɔ to, ŋn'ɩ zum Yeezuu ʊ.

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Yeezuu k'a nɩ Galilee higʊta lɛ ra an nɩ tam bɩ, a gʊɔɔ yɩ hɩɩya: Sɩmɔɔn kʊ n n'a birm Pɩyɛɛr kan a danzaa Andɩr kɩ, ŋn'ɩ zaŋŋa zom higʊta b'ʊ, bala, zɔbənno m.

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: K'a ta lɛɛ bɩ, a Zakkɩ yɩ kan a danzaa Zaan kɩ, Zebedee nyɩnɔ m ŋn'ɩ kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ kan a zɩ Zebedee bɩ kɩ, ŋn'a zaŋŋarɔ bankam. A n ŋ bir.

ref: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
text: Ŋ n a bɔ a zɩ b'ʊ kan kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ kɩ lɛɛm, ŋn'ɩ zure a jɛ ʊ. Yeezuu kan gʊɔɔ ciiro kɩ

ref: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yeezuu gasʊ Galilee kʊ b'ʊ haay, an nɩ bɩmbɩrm, an nɩ hɔ dɩndam gʊɔɔ m zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ, an nɩ Woso cirbəə lemim nyɩnta bɩ pa dam, an nɩ yaaba do haay waam kan kʊsɩ do haay kɩ zamaa bɩ bire ʊ.

ref: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
text: A tɔ bɔ lɛɛ haay ʊ, an ta kan Siirii kʊ kɩ haay, a m naa, n nɩ zɛm gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ cicir bam yaaba do haay kan kʊsɩ do haay nɔ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ m: Gʊɔɔ kʊ zinəro n ta ŋ nɩ, parparlɔzannɔ, mɛgarɛzannɔ kɩ an ŋ waa.

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Zamaa cir gʊta nɩ zure a jɛ ʊ, a to ŋ y'a ʊ Galilee ʊ, Taraburo ʊ, Zerizalɛm kan Zidee kɩ, kan Zʊrdɛn kɔ gəm la kɩ.

ref: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
text: Yeezuu k'a zamaa bɩ yɩ bɩ, a der ci burə ʊ, an nyɩnta. A karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ ma.

ref: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
text: Kʊ m sɩra hɩ awɔɔ m. K'awɔɔ tɩrgabaa n cem wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ tɩrgabaa kɩ han, awɔɔ bɩ gasʊm Woso cirbəə ʊ y.» Heerbusu mim

ref: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
text: Naa minto bɩ, k'ɩbɩɩ ta Wosocɛ ʊ k'ɩ yaa ganwʊrɛ ka Woso ʊ, ɩ bɔkarɛ ɩ tɔ da ɩ nɔ ʊ, ɩ ʊ, ɩ ɩ zar ɩ mim ma,

ref: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
text: «Mimbirenyɩntazannɔ m mɩŋŋazerlezannɔ m: Ŋ hɔ m Woso cirbəə bɩ m.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Mimbirenyɩntazannɔ m gʊɔɔ bʊgʊ-bʊgʊdɔɔ wɔɔ m: Ŋ y'a hɔjirle yɩm durnya ʊ.

ref: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
text: Mimbirenyɩntazannɔ m nyikərezannɔ m: Woso bra barɛ ŋ nɩ.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Mimbirenyɩntazannɔ m cicirzɛrɛzannɔ m: Woso yɩ ŋ cicir zɛrɛ.

ref: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
text: Mimbirenyɩntazannɔ m heerhuzannɔ m: Ŋ Woso yɩrɛ.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: «Durnya lɛɛgure n'awɔɔ m, kʊ k'a nɩ zigi burə ʊ bɩ b'a dam ma, an a nyaakʊm da y.

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, a b'a wer si fɩɩga y: A b'a wer si brama tɔ nɩ y, bala, Woso cirbəəduu m bɩ.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: A b'a wer si tara tɔ nɩ y, bala, a gam cɩntarahɔ m bɩ, a b'a wer si Zerizalɛm tɔ nɩ y, bala, cir gʊta bɩ kʊ m bɩ.

ref: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
text: «Durnya yɛ n'awɔɔ m. Bɩ, kʊ yɛ nyɩntabaa bɔ, n y'a bam lɔ, k'a a ya, a nyɩntabaa ka ʊ? A zibəə ba ʊ dɔ y, a lo n nawʊm garga ra, gʊɔɔ nɩ tʊntɔm la.»

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Kʊ gʊaa sɛ nya fɩtɩrɛ ʊ a bɩr a cɩntam kasɩ taa y, a cɩnta a tɩr a ʊ hɔ burə ʊ an cɛ bɩ gu gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ b'ʊ haay rɔ wɔɔ daahan minto.

ref: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
text: «A b'a hɔɔn da a ʊ, bʊr mɔɔ ʊ kʊ m bɩ Moyiisi lɛ, gɛɛ, wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ n a hɩ rɔ wɔɔ zar y. Mɔɔ n bʊr kʊ m bɩ ŋ zar y, bʊr mɔɔ ʊ kʊ ŋ nɩ kʊ yɩ.

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
text: «Awɔɔ ma n n a hɩ wɔɔ yaabɔɔ m, n ʊ: ‹Ɩ bɩ gər zɛ y›, kʊ gʊaa a mim meeyaa bɔ ʊ, a a ga m, kʊ n n ta m dʊdɔkənno taa.

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: Kʊ gʊaa nɔmaa ba kan a mim kɩ, a a ga m, kʊ n n ta a zaa m dʊdɔkənno taa. Kʊ gʊaa a hɩ a mim m, a ʊ: ‹Hɔɔnwarzaa›, a a ga m, kʊ n n ta a zaa m dʊdɔkənno jɩtannɔ taa. Kʊ gʊaa a hɩ a mim m, a ʊ: ‹hɔɔzaa›, a ga a ʊ m k'an ta sɛhar ʊ.

ref: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
text: k'ɩ ganwʊrɛ bɩ to Wosocɛ ʊ b'ʊ, ɩ ta ɩ yaa n'ɩ zɛ kʊ ra kan ɩ mim bɩ kɩ, k'ɩ bɔkarɛ ɩ ɩ wusigə ɩ bʊr, ɩ b'ɩ ganwʊrɛ bɩ ka Woso ʊ.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: K'ɩbɩɩ jɩm ɩ tarɛ ɩ m dʊdɔkarɛ bɩncɛ ʊ, k'ɩ tɔ ʊ, a kʊ ma zaa ra. K'a n yɩ ncɩnaaʊ y, a y'ɩ kam dʊdɔkər ʊ, dʊdɔkər bɩ n ɩ ka lɔnnɔdɔmazannɔ ʊ, ŋn'ɩ du lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ ɩbɩɩ m: Ɩbɩɩ bɩ bɔm b'ʊ k'ɩbɩɩ n ɩ zɩm bɩ ka haay y, bɩ tanka deem taan… bɩ gɔɔtarɛ y.» Jaandarɛ mim

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: «N y'a ya, n n a hɩ sɔ, n ʊ: ‹Kʊ gʊaa la a lʊ ra k'an wɔsaalʊmaci ka ʊ.›

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: «Awɔɔ ma, n n a hɩ wɔɔ yaabɔɔ m, n ʊ: ‹Ɩ bɩ ɩ lerbɔrɛ to y, b'a ka kʊ hɔ k'ɩbɩɩ ler bɔ Zuuba m ɩ ɩ wer si bɩ n kʊ yɩ.›

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: «Awɔɔ ma, n n a hɩ, n ʊ: ‹Mɩnyaa nɩ sompim mɩnyaa bɩncɛ ʊ, sɔ nɩ sompim sɔ bɩncɛ ʊ.›

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, kʊ gʊaa a ba awɔɔ m bʊnyaa, a b'a bʊnyaa ba m sɔ y. Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa a wɔ ta ɩ bɩsɩ paasɩ rɛ, k'ɩ ɩ jɩsa paasɩ bɩ ka ʊ k'an a ta rɛ.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: Kʊ gʊaa ʊ, k'a dʊdɔ ka kan ɩ kɩ, k'a ɩ huudaara yɔ ɩ m, k'ɩ to k'an ɩ huukuurə sa sɔ.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Kʊ gʊaa paŋŋa ba ɩ ma, k'a ɩ da tarɛ ma ganjɩbɔʊrɛ tisi kan a kɩ, k'ɩ ta ganjɩbɔʊrɛ tisi hɩɩya kan kɩ.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Kʊ gʊaa hɔ yɛ ɩ han, k'ɩ ka ʊ. Kʊ gʊaa ʊ, k'a zɩm sa ɩ zi, ɩ bɩ ɩ gu han y.» Jɩnnɔ ŋʊarɛ (Likki

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: «Awɔɔ ma, n n a hɩ, n ʊ: ‹K'ɩ ɩ gʊaa ŋʊa, k'ɩ sɔ ɩ jɩm m.›

ref: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: K'a a jɩnnɔ ŋʊa, k'a yaa da Woso m gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ fɩr karɛ awɔɔ ma rɔ wɔɔ tɔ ma.

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, awɔɔ yɩm awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ nyɩnɔ paan…rɔ. Bala, a a ka woso nɩ gum gʊɔɔ bʊnyaarɔ wɔɔ ra kan gʊɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ. A a ka sɔ, la nɩ bam gʊɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ ra kan gʊɔɔ bʊnyaarɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Bɩ, k'awɔɔ ɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ n'awɔɔ ŋʊam nɔ wɔɔ ŋʊarɛ, a nyɔɔdʊ ɩ ka? Bala, yawʊrsinno wɔɔ y'a barɛ ncɩnaaʊ sɔ.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Bɩ, k'awɔɔ ɩ yaa darɛ a danyɩnɔ m bala, bɔ awɔɔ n'a bam, an nɩ yɩrɛ a hɔ? Kɩfɩɩrɔ wɔɔ bɩr a barɛ ncɩnaaʊ sɔ ra?

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: K'a maam, k'a yɩ mɩŋŋa paan… amba awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ n mɩŋŋa paan… bɩ m.»

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: «A b'a ka k'a a zibəəro mɩŋŋɔɔ ba, kʊ gʊɔɔ n dɩga a ma y. K'awɔɔ ŋ ba kʊ ŋ nɩ dɩga a ma, awɔɔ bɩ ganwʊrɛ yɩm awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ zi y.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: B'ɩbɩɩ, k'ɩ ɩ gan wʊrɛ, k'ɩ jɩsa wɔ bɩ bɩ hɔ k'ɩ bɩsɩ wɔ n'a bam bɩ dɔ y.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: «K'awɔɔ ɩ yaa darɛ, a bɩ yɩ am zambʊzannɔ hɔ bɩ nɩ y. Kʊ ŋ lɛ n taa kʊ gʊɔɔ n a yɩ bɩ, jɩm ŋ tɩr a ʊ sokʊmacɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ, kan zaarɔ zɛkʊrajɩrɔ kɩ ʊ, ŋn'ɩ bɩr yaa darɛ. Mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, sɩra m, ŋ y'a ganwʊrɛ yɩ.

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: B'ɩbɩɩ, k'ɩ lɛ taa kɩ yaa da, gasʊ ɩ cɛ ʊ ɩ lɛ ta, ɩ yaa da ɩ Zɩ Woso k'a nɩ b'ʊ bɩ m, b'ɩbɩɩ Zɩ k'a nɩ lɛɛ k'a nyaakʊm da bɩ yɩm bɩ, a y'ɩbɩɩ zibəə k'ɩbɩɩ nyaakʊm da bɩ ganwʊrɛ karɛ ɩ ʊ.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: K'awɔɔ ɩ yaa darɛ, a bɩ meer ba gʊta am kɩfɩɩrɔ hɔ bɩ nɩ y. A hɔɔn ŋn'a darɛ, ŋ ʊ, k'a meer ba gʊta, Woso y'a yaadarɛ mam.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: «K'awɔɔ k'a yaa da, k'a da ncɩnaaʊ: Wɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ, k'ɩ tɔ n lɛbɔʊrɛ yɩ,

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: k'ɩ cirbəə n bʊr, k'ɩ hɔɔnhɔ n ba tara burə ʊ, amba an nɩ bam arzana ʊ bɩ m.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: «Kʊ sɩra nɩ, k'awɔɔ gʊɔɔ kʊrɔmarɔ sugur ka ŋ ʊ, awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ sugur karɛ awɔɔ ʊ sɔ.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Bɩ, k'awɔɔ bɩr sugur kam gʊɔɔ ʊ, awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ b'awɔɔ kʊrɔmarɔ sugur karɛ awɔɔ ʊ sɔ y.» Lɛkʊsɩrɛ

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: K'ɩbɩɩ y'ɩ lɛ kʊsɩrɛ, k'ɩ hi da ɩ meer ʊ, ɩ tidəər zɛ ɩ mim ma,

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: kʊ gʊɔɔ b'a dɔ, ŋ ʊ, ɩ lɛ ɩ kʊsɩ y. Bɩ, k'ɩ Zɩ k'a nɩ lɛɛ k'a nyaakʊm da ʊ bɩ n a dɔ a deem. B'a y'ɩ ganwʊrɛ karɛ ɩ ʊ.» Arzaka k'a nɩ arzana ʊ (Likki

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: K'a lɛɛ ka a ka Woso cirbəə bɩ ma, kan a tɩrgabaa kɩ. Maam bɩ, Woso hɔ k'a gɔɔta bɩ karɛ awɔɔ ʊ.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Naa minto bɩ, a b'a heer kɔ booti mim ma y. Booti dɩgarɛ a mɩŋŋa mim ma. Dɔmɩm biyəə y'a mɩŋŋa mim m.»

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: «A b'a ka arzaka ma durnya burə ʊ naa ʊ y. Bala, bɛɛ kan muku kɩ y'a zarm, konno ɩ cɛlɛ yarm, ŋn'a sarɛ.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Bɩ k'ɩ ka arzana arzaka ma. Bala, muku kan bɛɛ kɩ ba b'ʊ k'a zar y, konno ba b'ʊ k'a cɛlɛ yar, k'a sa y.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Bala, lɛɛ k'ɩ arzaka n ta ʊ b'ɩ heer n ta ʊ sɔ.» (Likki

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Bɩ k'ɩ mɩnyaa bɩ mɩŋŋa y, ɩbɩɩ mɛ bɩ haay nyɩntam monsigə ʊ. K'a ɩ yɩrɛ ɩ ʊ, lɛɛgure k'ɩbɩɩ n ta m bɩ monsigə nɩ, monsigə bɩ do yɩm gʊta bidə.» Woso gɛɛ busoo (Likki

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: «Gʊaa ba ʊ an b'a dam ma, an zuubɔɔ hɩɩyɔɔ zi ba y. A deem ŋʊam, an sɔ deem m, gɛɛ, an a zɛ kʊ ra kan deem kɩ, an kaanm deem m. Awɔɔ b'a dam ma, a zu Woso ʊ, k'a a ya, a zu busoo ʊ y.» (Likki

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: K'a maam, a b'a heer kɔ a bɩr a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: Bɔ wɔɔ n'a bɩrɛ? Bɔ wɔɔ n'a mire? Bɔ wɔɔ n'a dure ʊ ma?

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: «Bɩ y'a ka, mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: a b'a heer kɔ a mɩsɩrbaa bɩ hɔbɩrɛ mim ma y. B'a b'a heer kɔ huu mim ma sɔ y. Mɩsɩrbaa n lɛ da hɔbɩrɛ m na? Mɛsim bɩ n lɛ da huu m na?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: K'a dɩga bɛnnɔ ma: ŋ bɩr hɔ duure y, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔ darɛ y, ŋ bɩr hɔ zarm sɩ ʊ y, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ yɩ ŋ tʊrm! Awɔɔ n lɛ da ŋ nɩ gallɛm hɔka ra?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: Bɔ y'a ka, awɔɔ n'a heer kɔm huu mim ma? K'a dɩga pooyaa hɔvuro ma, ŋ yɩ mam, bɩ ŋ bɩr zi barɛ, bɩ ŋ bɩr huu barɛ y.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Bɩ mɔɔ ʊ kʊ m a hɩ awɔɔ m, Salomɔɔn mɩŋŋa, b'a kan a cirbəə bɩ kɩ, a n huu du a ma, am ŋ deem hɔ bɩ nɩ y.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Bɩ, kʊ Woso ɩ huu dum pooyaa bur ma ncɩnaaʊ, bɩ ta an a ʊ her kʊ sɛ n b'a bɩ booti m, a bɩ huu dure awɔɔ ma an lɛ da brɔɔ m na? Sɩrakarɛ poore zannɔ!

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Naa do haay kɩfɩɩrɔ wɔɔ n'a karɛ ŋ ma kʊsɩ-kʊsɩ, b'awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ b'a dɔ, a ʊ, awɔɔ lɛ taa ŋ ma.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: «A bɩ gʊaa dʊdɔ yar y, kʊ Woso b'a dʊdɔ yar y.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: K'awɔɔ gʊaa dʊdɔ yar lʊnlɔ, awɔɔ dʊdɔ yar n nawʊm maam sɔ, hɔganhɔ k'awɔɔ n ta m gʊɔɔ minto bɩ, bɩ nɩ nyɩntam hɔganhɔ awɔɔ minto sɔ.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: A lɔ ɩbɩɩ nɩ bur k'a n'ɩ mim mɩm ʊ bɩ yɩrɛ, an bɔkarɛ kusə n'ɩbɩɩ mɩm ʊ, b'ɩ bɩr a yɩrɛ?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Ɩbɩɩ y'a bam lɔ, ɩ hɩ ɩ danyɩ m, ɩ ʊ, jɩm kʊ m bur bɔ ɩ mɩm ʊ, an bɔkarɛ kusə n'ɩbɩɩ mɩm ʊ!

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Zambʊzaa, kusə bɩ bɔ ɩ mɩm ʊ cɩna, k'ɩ dɔ ɩ lɛɛ yɩ mɩŋŋa, ɩ bur bɩ bɔ ɩ danyɩ bɩ mɩm ʊ.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Awɔɔ k'an bʊnyaa ncɩnaaʊ a bɔkarɛ a hɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ ka a nyɩnɔ ʊ dɔ, awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ bɛɛ lɛ da ra? A hɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ kam gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ n'a yɛm han rɔ wɔɔ ʊ.

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Gʊaa y'awɔɔ bire ʊ a nyɩ nɩ bur yɛm han, an jaa ka ʊ ra?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: K'a hɔ yɛ, n y'a karɛ awɔɔ ʊ. K'awɔɔ a ka ma, awɔɔ y'a yɩm. K'awɔɔ cɛlɛ zɛ, n y'a gʊrɛ awɔɔ m.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Bala, kʊ gʊaa hɔ yɛ, a y'a yɩm. K'a a ka ma, a y'a yɩm. Kʊ gʊaa cɛlɛ zɛ, n y'a gʊm nɩ.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Gɛɛ, k'a zɔ yɛ han, a mɩm kaŋ ʊ ra?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, hɔ k'awɔɔ lɛ n taa haay kʊ gʊɔɔ n a ba awɔɔ m bɩ, k'a ba ŋ nɩ sɔ. Moyiisi lɛrɔ wɔɔ haay kan wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ ciro wɔɔ kɩ a hɩ. Zaa poore (Likki

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, gʊaa k'a nɩ mɔɔ meerbaa kʊ mɔɔ ba naa mam bɩ, an nɩ zi bam nɩ bɩ, bɔ a ʊ kʊ ʊ kan gʊaa k'a hɔɔn n ta ʊ, an a cɛ dɔ ci burə ʊ kɩ.

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Bɩ, cɛlɛ fɔɔrɛ bɩ kan zaa kʊsɩ bɩ kɩ bɩ, ta ŋ nawʊm mɩsɩrbaa bɩncɛ ʊ. Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ ŋ yɩm nɔ wɔɔ bɩ gʊta y. Gɔ dɔ n nawʊrɛ a nyɩ ma (Likki

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
text: K'a dundo a zi kan ŋʊaarlɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ kɩ, bʊr ŋ tɩr a ʊ awɔɔ zi, am seero hɔ bɩ m, an bɔkarɛ poojino kʊsɩrɔ m.

ref: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
text: Ŋ zibəəro awɔɔ nɩ ŋ dɔm ma. N bɩr tʊgɔ nyɩ nɔm laa gɔ ra y, n bɩr sɔɔn nyɩ nɔm laazankʊrɛ ra y.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
text: Gɔ mɩŋŋa bɩ, nyɩ mɩŋŋa an n'a dam, gɔ bʊnyaa bɩ, nyɩ bʊnyaa an n'a dam sɔ.

ref: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
text: Gɔ mɩŋŋa b'a dam ma an nyɩ bʊnyaa da y, gɔ bʊnyaa b'a dam ma, an nyɩ mɩŋŋa da sɔ y.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Gɔ haay k'a bɩr nyɩ mɩŋŋa dam bɩ, a zɛ n nawʊm la, n n'a dam sɛ ʊ.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
text: Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ ŋ zibəəro awɔɔ nɩ ŋ dɔm ma. Karɩndanyɩnɔ paan…rɔ (Likki

ref: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
text: Bɩ gʊaa k'a nɩ mɔɔ meerbaa kʊ mɔɔ ba naa mam b'a bɩr zi bam nɩ bɩ, gʊaa bɩ do bɔ a ʊ kʊ ʊ kan gʊaa yarma k'a cɛ dɔ nyɩntaa burə ʊ kɩ.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: La n ba, hi n yar, hɩnhɛr n hɔn kan paŋŋa kɩ, an a mom cɛ b'ʊ, cɛ bɩ n wurgə, a hɔsɩ n gɔɔta y.»

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: Gʊaa haay k'a n'a hɩm mɔɔ ma: ‹Zuuba, Zuuba› bɩ bɛɛ nɩ gasʊm Woso cirbəə ʊ y. Bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ hɔɔnhɔ bam nɔ wɔɔ m.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Dʊdɔkarɛ hinni ʊ, gʊɔɔ gʊta y'a hɩrɛ: ‹Zuuba, Zuuba, ɩbɩɩ tɔ minto, wɔɔ Woso lɛ si ʊ so ra. Ɩbɩɩ tɔ ma wɔɔ la zinəro ra. Wɔɔ hoserlo ba gʊta ɩbɩɩ tɔ minto.›

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ, mɔɔ ʊ: ‹Mɔɔ n awɔɔ dɔ fɩɩga y. K'a a hʊr mɔɔ jɩr ʊ, mimbʊnyaabənno!› Cɛ jɩr k'an mɩŋŋa (Likki

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
text: La n ba, hi n yar, hɩnhɛr n hɔn kan paŋŋa kɩ, an a mom cɛ b'ʊ, a n a da y. Bala, a jɩr bɩ da n y'a ʊ ci burə ʊ.

ref: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Bala, Yeezuu bɩr hɔ dɩndarɛ am ŋ wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ hɔ bɩ nɩ y. Woso paŋŋa an nɩ hɔ bɩ dɩndam nɩ.

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Gʊɔɔ kʊ n lɛɛ ka n ŋ bɔ ʊ Woso cirbəə bɩ minto rɔ wɔɔ, n brɔɔ nyɔɔm garga ra monsigə ʊ. Nyikəre kan sɔsʊrɛ kɩ nyɩntam b'ʊ.»

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Yirbəə k'a da bɩ, n bʊr gʊɔɔ m gʊta zinəro n ta ŋ nɩ. Yeezuu meer ba bala an la zinəro wɔɔ ra, an yaabazannɔ wɔɔ waa haay.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Kusimə n b'a zɔ ma, an kukurə zɛ a taa, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Zuuba, k'ɩ lɛ taa, ɩ ɩ da ma, ɩ mɔɔ waa, ɩ dintim naa do gʊ mɔɔ ma.»

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
text: Yeezuu n a wɔ hʊr, an a da ma, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ lɛ taa, ɩ waa. Kʊ dintim bɩ n gʊ ɩ ma.» Lɛɛm bɩ, a kusiməbaa bɩ nya ma.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ tam, m aa a waa.»

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Yeezuu k'a naa ma bɩ, a dɩga gʊaa bɩ ma, an zɛ kədə, an a hɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ zum kʊ ʊ kan kɩ rɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, mɔɔ n sɩrakarɛ naa do lɔɔ yɩ Israyɛl gʊaa zi y.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: «Zuuba, mɔɔ zibəənyɩ nɩ wʊtam har ʊ, a mɛ n ga, an nɩ fɩr yɩm gʊta.»

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
text: A hɩ mɔɔ n nawʊrɛ awɔɔ m, gʊɔɔ y'a tom gʊta dɔjɩr ʊ kan meer kɩ ŋnɩ bɩ nyɩnta kan Abraham kɩ Izakkɩ kɩ, Zakɔɔb kɩ, ŋnɩ hɔ bɩ Woso cirbəə b'ʊ.

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Meerbaa naa do jɛ ʊ, Yeezuu a hɩ sʊrdaarɔ cir bɩ m, a ʊ: «Doo har ʊ, k'an ba ɩbɩɩ m, amba ɩbɩɩ sɩra ka mɔɔ ʊ bɩ m.» Wakatɩ bɩ do ʊ lɛɛm bɩ, zibəənyɩ b'a waa. Pɩyɛɛr dində waarɛ (Markɩ

ref: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
text: Ŋ n a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'ɩ zee dam, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ ɩbɩɩ lɛ n taa k'ɩ ba wɔɔ nɩ Woso Nyɩ? Bʊr ɩbɩɩ ʊ k'ɩ bɩ fɩr ka wɔɔ ma, bɩ wɔɔ fɩryɩrɛ dɔmɩm bɩ n a ku cɩna ra?»

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: A m bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ kʊnɔ ʊ b'ʊ haay rɔ wɔɔ n bɔ, ŋn'ɩ tam k'a yaa Yeezuu zɛ ʊ. A yɩ kʊ ŋ y'a ʊ bɩ, ŋ y'a yɛ han gʊta k'an bɔ a kʊ b'ʊ.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Yeezuu k'a gasʊ Pɩyɛɛr har ʊ bɩ, a Pɩyɛɛr dində yɩ an nɩ wʊtam mɛmarɛ m.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: A n a wɔ da a wɔ ma mɛmarɛ bɩ n bɔ a mɛ ʊ, an wuti an nyasʊ Yeezuu m. Yaabazannɔ waarɛ

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Yɩ a ʊ ncɩnaaʊ, kʊ hɔ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa Ezayii a hɩ bɩ n kʊ yɩ. A a hɩ, a ʊ: A ɩ hɔ haay k'a nɩ fɩr kam wɔɔ mɛsim ma bɩ sa, an wɔɔ yaabarɔ gʊ wɔɔ ma. Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ n'a ŋʊam k'a zu Yeezuu ʊ (Likki

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Wosocikarɩnsaamba deem n a zɔ ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, mɔɔ zure ɩbɩɩ jɛ ʊ lɛɛ haay k'ɩbɩɩ nɩ taŋ ʊ b'ʊ.»

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Karɩndanyɩ vanta n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Zuuba, zaa ka m ʊ, kʊ m aa nɩ m zɩ gər bir, kʊ m a dɔ m bʊr.»

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ ma, ŋn'a gu, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Zuuba, wɔɔ yɔ! Kʊ m bɛɛ m wɔɔ gɩngam!»

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yeezuu gasʊ kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ, a karɩndanyɩnɔ n zu a jɛ ʊ.

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, hɩnhɛr gʊta wuti hi bɩ burə ʊ, an a ŋʊa k'a ka kʊ hi m kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ mom. Yeezuu n bɔkarɛ, an nɩ hinceem bam.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Ŋ n zɛ kədə, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Bɩ gʊaa bɩ kara n naa, baa hɩnhɛr kan hi kɩ n n'a lɛ sire?» Gʊɔɔ kʊ zinəro n ta ŋ nɩ waarɛ (Markɩ

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Yeezuu k'a lee ku higʊta bɩ tʊr deem ʊ Gadara zannɔ kʊ ʊ b'ʊ bɩ, a m bɩ, gʊɔɔ hɩɩyɔɔ kʊ zinəro n ta ŋ nɩ n bɔ gəənoyaarɔ ʊ, ŋnɩ b'a zɛ ʊ. Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ duro nyɩnta bʊnyaa gʊta. Naa minto bɩ, gɔsɩ b'a dam ma, an cem lɛɛ bɩ do ʊ y.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: A n bɔkarɛ, kurkuuro cir gʊta ɩ nyɩnta laatʊ b'ʊ, ŋn'ɩ hɔ bɩrɛ.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Zinəro wɔɔ n'a yɛm Yeezuu han gʊta, ŋ ʊ: «K'ɩ la wɔɔ ra, k'ɩ wɔɔ nyɔɔ kurkuuro wɔɔ mɛ ʊ.»

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a ta!» Ŋ nɩ bɔ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ mɛ ʊ, ŋnɩ gasʊ kurkuuro wɔɔ mɛ ʊ. A m bɩ, kurkuuro cir gʊta bɩ n bra si, ŋnɩ zer, ŋn'a zar higʊta b'ʊ, ŋnɩ gɩnga.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Kurkuuro dundonno wɔɔ n bra si, ŋn'aa hɔ k'a ba bɩ hɩ kʊnɔ ʊ b'ʊ, kan hɔ k'a ba gʊɔɔ kʊ zinəro n ta ŋ nɩ rɔ wɔɔ n bɩ kɩ.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
text: Naa jɛ ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu gasʊ kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ, an a wusigə, an higʊta bɩ zɛ ra an ta a kʊ ʊ.

ref: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
text: Yeezuu k'a naa ma bɩ, a a hɩ, a ʊ: «Laafɩɩzannɔ lɛ baa lɔɔtɔr ma y, yaabazannɔ lɛ n taa lɔɔtɔr ma.

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
text: Gʊaa ba ʊ, an nɩ dɩvɛn daa sam k'a tɔ hibɔɔnrɔ gusinno ʊ y. Kʊ m bɛɛ m, hibɔɔnrɔ wɔɔ hɩnhaanm, dɩvɛn bɩ n a lo hibɔɔnrɔ wɔɔ n a zaar ba. Bɩ dɩvɛn daa bɩ tɔ n tɩr a ʊ hibɔɔnrɔ daarɔ ʊ, dɩvɛn bɩ kan hibɔɔnrɔ wɔɔ kɩ b'a zaarbarɛ y.» Zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ cir nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ waarɛ, kan lʊ k'a wɔ da Yeezuu huu ma kɩ (Markɩ

ref: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
text: Mim naa do n ta lɛ haay ʊ kʊ bɩ do ʊ. Bʊʊrɔ hɩɩyɔɔ waarɛ

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
text: Wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ gɔsɩnnɔ nyɩnta b'ʊ, ŋn'a hɩrɛ a heer ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Gʊaa naa do ɩ Woso sʊnsɔ wʊrɛ!»

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Yeezuu k'a hɔ k'a nɩ ŋ heer ʊ bɩ dɔ bɩ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Bɔ minto, awɔɔ n'a hɔɔn darɛ ncɩnaaʊ?

ref: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: Bɩ kɛɛrɛ bɩ, awɔɔ y'a dɔrɛ, a ʊ: Gʊaanyɩ b'ɩ lɛ m tara burə ʊ, k'a gʊɔɔ mimbʊnyaarɔ sugur ka ŋ ʊ.» A n a hɩ lʊgɔ bɩ m, a ʊ: «Wuti, ɩ ɩ wʊtarahɔ bɩ gʊr ɩ doo!»

ref: The man got up and went home.
text: Gʊaa bɩ n wuti an doo.

ref: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
text: K'a ta, a yaa a ka meerbaa naa do jɩ ma: ‹Cicirzɛrɛ mɔɔ lɛ n taa, mannɩ barɛ bɛɛ y›. Mɔɔ n bʊr kʊ m bɩ gʊɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ bir y. Bʊr mɔɔ ʊ kʊ m bɩ mimbʊnyaabənno bir.» Hɔ daa kan hɔ gusire kɩ bɩr zum kʊ ʊ y (Markɩ

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Yeezuu k'a hʊr b'ʊ bɩ, bʊʊrɔ hɩɩyɔɔ ɩ b'a zɛ ʊ, ŋnɩ zu a jɛ ʊ, ŋn'ɩ baa dam, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Wɔɔ cicir zɛ *Davɩd Nyɩ!»

ref: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
text: Yeezuu k'a n'a hʊrm bɩ, n bʊr gʊaa m zinə n ta m, an a ba bəətir.

ref: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
text: Yeezuu k'a nɩ tam bɩ, a a to, an gʊaa yɩ an nɩ nyɩntam yawʊrsire bɩncɛ b'ʊ, a tɔ m Matiyee, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Zu mɔɔ ʊ!» A n wuti an zu a jɛ ʊ.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Kʊ Yeezuu nɩ nyɩntam Matiyee har ʊ b'ʊ k'a hɔ bɩ bɩ, yawʊrsinno kan mimbʊnyaabənno kɩ nyɩnta b'ʊ gʊta kan kɩ, kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ naa yɩ bɩ, ŋ y'a hɩrɛ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, ŋ ʊ: «A lɔ, awɔɔ karɩnsaamba bɩ nɩ hɔbɩrɛ bɩm kan yawʊrsinno kɩ, mimbʊnyaabənno kɩ?»

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
text: K'a nɩ meer barɛ ncɩnaaʊ kan gʊɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ bɩ, zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ cir deem n a zɔ ma, an sʊnta a taa, an n'a hɩm nɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ ɩ ga, a n maasɩrɛ ba cɩna y. Bɩ, bʊr ɩ wɔ ka a mim la, k'an a mɩm bɔ ʊ.»

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu a bɔ an dɩga a jɛ ʊ, an lʊ bɩ yɩ an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ nyɩnnɩ, ɩ heer ba kookoo, ɩ sɩrakarɛ y'ɩ bʊmbɔ». Lɛɛm bɩ, lʊ b'a waa wakatɩ bɩ do ʊ.

ref: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
text: Yeezuu k'a la zinə bɩ ra bɩ, bəətir b'a sɩŋŋɩda sa, an nɩ meer bam. Gʊɔɔ cir gʊta bɩ zɛ kədə. Ŋ n'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Hɔ naa do lɔɔ n ba cɩna Israyɛl kʊ naa ʊ y!»

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Zaan karɩndanyɩnɔ a zɔ Yeezuu ma, ŋnɩ laaka da han, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ minto m, wɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kɩ, wɔɔ n'ʊ lɛ kʊsɩm, b'ɩbɩɩ hɔɔ wɔɔ bɩr a kʊsɩrɛ?»

ref: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «Kʊ gʊaa ɩ lʊsarɛ cibsa barɛ, lʊ bɩ zim bɩ nɩ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ bireyaa ʊ, gʊɔɔ wɔɔ y'a dam ma, ŋnɩ heerzarlɛ ba ra? Bɩ dɔmɩm niŋŋə ʊ, n zɛm bɩ lʊ bɩ zim bɩ gʊ ŋ ma, ŋ y'a lɛ kʊsɩm dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ.

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Gʊaa ba ʊ, an nɩ pɛɛra daa kər sam, an n'a tam huu gusire rɛ y. Bala, pɛɛra daa kər bɩ huu gusire bɩ gaam an a kaansʊ, an lɛ da biisi ra.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Bala, lʊ bɩ y'a hɩrɛ a heer ʊ, a ʊ: «Kʊ mɔɔ m wɔ da a huu lɛ ma tɔkkɛ, mɔɔ yɩ m waam.»

ref: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
text: Yeezuu n wuti kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ kɩ, ŋnɩ zu a jɛ ʊ.

ref: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
text: Bɩ, lʊ n ta ʊ, ma n'a dam zi a dɔɔraa bueerehɩɩya hɔ m naa, an a zɔ Yeezuu ma jɛ ʊ, an a wɔ da Yeezuu huu lɛ ma.

ref: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
text: Yeezuu k'a lee ku zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ cir bɩ har ʊ bɩ, k'a hirpenno wɔɔ yɩ kan gʊaa cir bɩ bʊgʊr barɛ kɩ bɩ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ:

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
text: «K'a bɔ, nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ n ga y. Hinceem a a ba.» Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ nɩ yaa wʊm ma.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Kʊ n bɔ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ m bɩ, a gasʊ zi, an nyɩ bɩ sa a wɔ ma, a m bɩ, nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ n wuti.

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Yeezuu k'a lee ku har ʊ bɩ, bʊʊrɔ wɔɔ a zɔ ma, an ŋ lar, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ sɩra ka a ʊ, mɔɔ yɩ m dam ma, m naa ba ra?» Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩɩn…, Zuuba.»

ref: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
text: A m naa, Yeezuu n a wɔ da ŋ mɩnyaarɔ wɔɔ ma, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «Hɔ k'awɔɔ sɩra ka k'a yɩ bɩ, k'a yɩ maam.»

ref: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Ŋ mɩnyaarɔ wɔɔ n gʊ. Yeezuu n lɛ ka ŋ ʊ paŋŋa m, a ʊ: «K'a dundo a zi! A b'a ka gʊaa n a dɔ y!»

ref: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
text: Bɩ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ bɔ bɩ, ŋ y'a sɩŋŋɩda sa lɛɛm, ŋn'a mim dɩndam kʊ b'ʊ haay. Yeezuu bəətir waa

ref: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
text: Bɩ farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Zinəro jɩtaarɛ bɩ nɩ paŋŋa kaŋ ʊ, an nɩ lam zinəro wɔɔ ra.» Yeezuu ɩ gʊɔɔ cir gʊta cicir zɛm

ref: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
text: Yeezuu ɩ bɩrm kʊnɔrɔ wɔɔ ʊ kan bɔlɛɛmarɔ kʊrɔ wɔɔ kɩ, an nɩ hɔ dɩndarɛ ŋ sokʊmacɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ, an nɩ Woso cirbəə lemim nyɩnta pa dam sɔ, an nɩ yaaba do haay waam kan kʊsɩ do haay kɩ.

ref: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
text: K'a gʊɔɔ cir gʊta bɩ yɩ bɩ, ŋ cicir a nyasʊ, bala, gʊɔɔ wɔɔ ɩ heerzarlɛ nɔ ʊ kan kɔrɛ kɩ, amba hɔbɩsɩrɔ kʊ ŋ ba dundor m bɩ m.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: A n a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Mɔsa zibəə bɩ gʊta, bɩ zibəənyɩnɔ wɔɔ bɩ gʊta y.

ref: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
text: Naa minto bɩ, k'a yɛ Wutuzaa bɩ han k'an zibəənyɩnɔ nyɔɔ a mɔsa zibəə bɩ tɔ ma.»

ref: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
text: Filipi kan Bartelemii kɩ, Toma kan yawʊrsirezaa Matiyee kɩ, Alfee nyɩ Zakkɩ kan Tade kɩ.

ref: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
text: Kʊ ŋ bɩ ta awɔɔ m brɔɔ lɛɛ, a b'a hɔɔn da k'awɔɔ meer bam lʊnlɔ, gɛɛ, bɔ awɔɔ n n'a hɩrɛ y. Hɔ k'awɔɔ n'a hɩm bɩ, n y'a karɛ awɔɔ ʊ dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ.

ref: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
text: Bɩ k'a ta Israyɛl do zi, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ bɔ kʊ ʊ kan hɔbɩsɩrɔ kʊ ŋ nyaam nɔ wɔɔ kɩ zi.

ref: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
text: «K'a tʊr ka mɩŋŋa, awɔɔ nyɔɔ mɔɔ nawʊm am seero hɔ bɩ m poojino bireyaa ʊ. Bɩ minto bɩ, k'a hɔɔn ka mɩŋŋa, am mɩnnɔ hɔ bɩ m. K'a yɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ mim ba ʊ y, am cirpʊnnɔ hɔ bɩ m.

ref: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
text: Sɩmɔɔn *Zelɔtɩ, kan Zidas Iskarɩyɔtɩ kɩ, b'ɩ Yeezuu da jɩnnɔ wɔ ʊ bɩ. (Markɩ

ref: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
text: Yeezuu bueerehɩɩyɔɔ nɔɔn duro nyɔɔ, an lɛ ka ŋ ʊ, a ʊ: «A bɩ kɩfɩɩrɔ zaa sa y, b'a bɩ gasʊ Samarii zannɔ kʊ ʊ y.

ref: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
text: A bɩ hɩra sa zaa bɩ sarɛ minto, b'a bɩ huu sa hɩɩya y. A bɩ kʊsɔ kan bara kɩ sa y, bala, zibəəzaa b'a ga m, an hɔbɩrɛ yɩ.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: K'awɔɔ gasʊ kʊ poore ʊ gɛɛ kʊ gʊta ʊ, k'a laaka da, kʊ gʊaa ta ʊ a lɛ n taa k'awɔɔ si a har ʊ, k'a gɔɔta b'ʊ k'an ta an awɔɔ hʊrlɛ ku.

ref: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: dʊdɔkarɛ hinni ʊ, kʊ naa do fɩryɩrɛ yɩm gʊta an lɛ da Sodɔm kan Gomɔɔr kɩ kʊ ra.» Fɩryɩrɛ k'a nɩ zɛm bɩ mim (Markɩ

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Danyɩnɔ kʊ kam zɛ ma. Zɩ y'a nyɩ karɛ, n n a zɛ. Nyɩnɔ wutire ŋnɩ jɩm kan a zɩrɔ kɩ, ŋnɩ ŋ ka, ŋnɩ ŋ zɩnzɛ.

ref: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
text: K'a dundo a zi kan gʊɔɔ kɩ, bala, ŋ y'awɔɔ gʊrm, ŋnɩ ta awɔɔ m dʊdɔkənno zi. Ŋ y'awɔɔ zɛm madɩɩrɔ m a sokʊmacɛrɔ ʊ.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Ŋ tarɛ awɔɔ m kʊrɔ lɛɛzannɔ kan cinno kɩ taa mɔɔ tɔ ma. Naa nɩ nyɩntam kasɛtɩ ŋ mɩŋŋɔɔ kan kɩfɩɩrɔ wɔɔ kɩ lɛɛ.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Bala, awɔɔ bɛɛ n nɩ meer barɛ a zim y, awɔɔ Zɩ Sɛnt-Ɛspri n nɩ meer k'awɔɔ n n'a bam bɩ darɛ awɔɔ lɛ ʊ.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Gʊaa haay sɔrɛ awɔɔ m mɔɔ tɔ ma. Bɩ gʊaa k'a jɩm kɔɔkɔɔ an ta an fɩryɩrɛ bɩ nyarɛ ku bɩ bʊmbɔrɛ yɩm.

ref: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
text: Kʊ ŋ fɩr ka awɔɔ ma kʊ niŋŋə ʊ, k'a a si a ta kʊ vanta ʊ. Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, awɔɔ bɩ Israyɛl kʊnɔrɔ wɔɔ nyarɛ Gʊaanyɩ bɩ m bɔkarɛ, an bʊr y.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: Karɩndanyɩ bɩ n lɛ da a karɩnsaamba ra y, zibəənyɩ bɩ n lɛ da a zuuba ra sɔ y.

ref: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
text: Karɩndanyɩ a ga m, an yɩ am a karɩnsaamba m. Zibəənyɩ a ga m, an yɩ am a zuuba m. Kʊ n har bɩ zaa bɩ ba zinəro jɩtaarɛ, awɔɔ hɔɔn ʊ naa, n b'a bam har bɩ nyɩnɔ wɔɔ m ncɩnaaʊ ra? (Likki

ref: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
text: A bɩ nyi ba gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ mɛsim zɛm gər, bɩ ŋ b'a dam ma, ŋnɩ gʊaa nyi zɛ rɔ wɔɔ nɩ y. K'a nyi ba Woso m. Bɩ y'a da ma, an mɛsim kan nyi kɩ zaar ba sɛhar ʊ.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: N bɩr bɛnnɔ hɛɛnm hɩɩya wacɩr la? A n bɔkarɛ deem taan… bɩr gam k'awɔɔ Zɩ bɛɛ zaa ka y.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: B'awɔɔ, ŋ y'awɔɔ minkə bɩ lɛ dɔ.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: K'a maam, a bɩ nyi ba y. Awɔɔ gore ta ʊ, an lɛ da bɛnnɔ haay ra. (Likki

ref: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
text: Kʊ gʊaa a hɩ gʊɔɔ lɛɛ, a ʊ, a mɔɔ dɔ, mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ m Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ m, mɔɔ ʊ, mɔɔ zaa dɔ sɔ.

ref: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
text: Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa bɩsɩ ka mɔɔ ma gʊɔɔ lɛɛ, mɔɔ bɩsɩ karɛ a zaa ma sɔ mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ lɛɛ. Laafɩɩ bɛɛ bʊr y, yarbarɛ m (Likki

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: A b'a hɔɔn da, a ʊ, bʊr mɔɔ ʊ kʊ laafɩɩ n nyɩnta ʊ tara burə ʊ y. Mɔɔ n bʊr kʊ laafɩɩ n nyɩnta ʊ y. Bʊr mɔɔ ʊ zoobarɛ m.

ref: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
text: Bʊr mɔɔ ʊ, kʊ m gʊaa gʊ a zɩ ma, kʊ m nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ gʊ a da ma, kʊ m lʊ gʊ a nyar ma.

ref: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
text: Gʊaa har gʊɔɔ n nɩ yɩrɛ a jɩnnɔ.

ref: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
text: Kʊ gʊaa n a gɔdarkʊra busu an zu mɔɔ ʊ, a n a ga m, an yɩ mɔɔ gʊaa y.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Kʊ gʊaa lɛ taa k'a a meeyaa bʊmbɔ, a b'a meeyaa bʊmbɔrɛ yɩrɛ y. Bɩ gʊaa k'a yɛ zɛ ma mɔɔ tɔ ma bɩ bʊmbɔrɛ yɩm.» Ganwʊrɛ yɩrɛ (Markɩ

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Kʊ gʊaa wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa si k'an wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa bɩ minto, a wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa ganwʊrɛ yɩm. Kʊ gʊaa gʊaa tɩrga si a tɩrgabaa tɔ ma, a gʊaa tɩrga ganwʊrɛ yɩm.

ref: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
text: «Kʊ gʊaa awɔɔ si, mɔɔ a m si. Mɔɔ si k'a ʊ bɩ, bɔzaa ɩ mɔɔ nyɔɔ b'a a si.

ref: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, kʊ gʊaa hi ka hɩnnɩ ʊ tɔkkɛ nyɩnɔ poorero nɔɔn duro deem ʊ, kʊ mɔɔ karɩndanyɩ m bɩ tɔ ma, gʊaa bɩ do bɩ kʊrɔm ganwʊrɛ ma y.»

ref: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
text: Zaan karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a hʊr bɩ, Yeezuu a sɩŋŋɩda ba, an n'a hɩm zamaa bɩ m, a ʊ: «Nka awɔɔ ta a yaa dɩga a poohɔɔn ʊ? Hɩnhɛr awɔɔ yɩ, an nɩ niigə gɔ yəŋŋəm gɛ?

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Bala, Zaan k'a bʊr bɩ, a bɩr hɔ bɩrɛ y, a bɩr dɩvɛn mim sɔ y. N n a hɩrɛ, n ʊ: zinə n ta m.

ref: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
text: Zaan k'a nɩ lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ b'a to an Krista zibəəro wɔɔ mim ma, an a karɩndanyɩnɔ gɔsɩnnɔ nyɔɔ,

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: kʊ ŋ n aa laaka da han, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ ŋn'ɩ dɔrɛ ɩ zɛrɛ ma bɩ gɛɛ, gʊaa vanta nɩ?»

ref: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «K'a ta a yaa hɔ k'awɔɔ n'a mam, an n'a yɩm naa dɩnda Zaan m:

ref: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
text: Bʊʊrɔ mɩm ɩ lɛɛ yɩm, lʊgɔrɔ ɩ tam, kusimoo y'a waam, dintim nɩ gʊrɛ ŋ ma, tikkiro ɩ meerbaa mam, gəəno y'a mɩm bɔŋ ʊ, lemim nyɩnta ɩ hɩm ŋʊazannɔ m.

ref: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
text: Mimbirenyɩntazaa m gʊaa k'a n a sɩrakarɛ to mɔɔ minto bɩ m.»

ref: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
text: Bɔ awɔɔ ta a yaa dɩga a paan…? Gʊaa m, an huu wɩsɩ da a ra gɛ? Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ huu wɩsɩrɔ darɛ a ra rɔ wɔɔ ɩ cinno harlɔ ʊ.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Nka awɔɔ ta a yaa dɩga a? Wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa m gɛ? Ɩɩn…, mɔɔ y'a hɩ awɔɔ m, a lɛ da wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa ra.

ref: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, lannɔ nyɩnɔ barla gʊaa ba ʊ an lɛ da Zaan-Batiisi ra y. A n bɔkarɛ gʊaa k'an poore Woso cirbəə nɔ ʊ bɩ lɛ da m.

ref: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
text: A n a sɩŋŋɩda sa Zaan-Batiisi Woso lemim padarɛ dɔmɩnnɔ ʊ, an lee ku dɩɩtaa naa ma bɩ, arzana bɩ gasʊ n nawʊŋ ʊ paŋŋa m, tʊntɔʊrɛzannɔ n'a soŋ ʊ.

ref: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
text: K'a dɔ mɩŋŋa a ʊ, *Elii niŋŋə kʊ n ʊ a zɛm bɩ m Zaan m.

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: Wɔɔ hir hɛ awɔɔ m, b'awɔɔ n sar ba y! Wɔɔ zɛlaa lɔ awɔɔ m, b'awɔɔ n nyi ka y!

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Gʊaanyɩ bɩ bʊr, an nɩ hɔ bɩm, an nɩ hɔ mim, n n'a hɩm ma, n ʊ: Naa do naa, nɔzaa m, bɩ, bɛɛmir m sɔ. Yawʊrsinno kan mimbʊnyaabənno kɩ hannyɩ m. Bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ yɛm Woso Mindɔrɛ bɩ ma rɔ wɔɔ a dɔ ŋ ʊ, Woso Mindɔrɛ bɩ tɩrga.» (Likki

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: A m naa, Yeezuu n a sɩŋŋɩda sa, an nɩ nyaa bam kan kʊrɔ k'a hoserlo ba ʊ gʊta bɩ ŋ n a bɔ a taam nɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
text: B'ɩbɩɩ, Kapɛrnawum, ɩbɩɩ y'ɩ busure ɩ lee ku brama ra? Ɩbɩɩ zerm ɩ gasʊ gəənokʊ ʊ! Bala, kʊ hoserlo kʊ ŋ ba ɩbɩɩ zi rɔ wɔɔ, kʊ ba ŋ y'a ʊ Sodɔm kʊ ʊ, k'an naa a gɔɔta an ta ʊ her dɔmɩm ʊ.

ref: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
text: Naa y'a ka, mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: dʊdɔkarɛ dɔmɩm ʊ, ɩbɩɩ fɩryɩrɛ yɩm kʊsɩ an lɛ da Sodɔm hɔ ra.» Zɩ kan Nyɩ kɩ (Likki

ref: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.

text: Hɔsɩra bɩ, durnya m. Hɔdo mɩŋŋa bɩ, Woso cirbəə nyɩnɔ wɔɔ m. Sʊr bɩ, Sʊtaana nyɩnɔ m.

ref: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
text: Jɩm k'a sʊr bɩ duu bɩ, Sʊtaana m. Hɔdarɛ bɩ, durnya nyarɛ m. Hɔdənno wɔɔ, malɛɛkarɔ m.

ref: Weeds are gathered and burned. That's how it will be at the end of time.
text: Amba kʊ ŋ nɩ sʊr gʊrm ŋn'a dam sɛ ʊ bɩ, yɩ a nawʊrɛ ncɩnaaʊ durnya nyarɛ hinni ʊ:

ref: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather from his kingdom everyone who does wrong or causes others to sin.
text: Gʊaanyɩ bɩ y'a malɛɛkarɔ nyɔɔrɛ, ŋnɩ hɔ haay k'a nɩ gʊɔɔ gʊm Woso ma rɔ wɔɔ gʊr Woso cirbəə bɩ nɔ ʊ. Ŋ mimbʊnyaabənno wɔɔ gʊrm sɔ, ŋnɩ bɔ ŋ nɩ,

ref: Then he will throw them into a flaming furnace, where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: ŋn'aa ŋ zar sɛhar ʊ. Ŋ nyi kam b'ʊ, ŋn'a sɔ sʊ.

ref: But everyone who has done right will shine like the sun in their Father's kingdom. If you have ears, pay attention! A Hidden Treasure
text: Bɩ, gʊɔɔ tɩrgarɔ wɔɔ gure am wosogure hɔ bɩ m, ŋ Zɩ bɩ cirbəə nɔ ʊ b'ʊ. Gʊaa k'a nɩ tʊr m, k'a n'a tʊr ka ma.» Arzaka k'a a nyaakʊm da

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when someone finds a treasure hidden in a field and buries it again. Such a person is happy and goes and sells everything in order to buy that field. A Valuable Pearl
text: «Woso cirbəə bɩ bɔ kʊ rɛ kan arzaka gʊta kʊ n y'a nyaakʊm da hɔsɩra ʊ bɩ kɩ, gʊaa n sʊr la: a n so ra, an a nyaakʊm da. K'a heer nyɔɔ bɩ, an a hʊr, an aa hɔ haay k'an ta m nɔ wɔɔ hɛɛ, an hɔsɩra bɩ do si m.»

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a shop owner is looking for fine pearls.
text: «Woso cirbəə b'a ya an bɔ kʊ rɛ kan terbarɛzaa k'a n'a kam nyɛɛnyaarɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ ma kɩ.

ref: After finding a very valuable one, the owner goes and sells everything in order to buy that pearl. A Fish Net
text: K'a nyɛɛnyaa wɩsɩ yɩ, a busoo nɩ yɩm gʊta bɩ, an ta, an aa hɔ haay k'an ta m nɔ wɔɔ hɛɛ, an b'a si m.» Zaŋŋa hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happens when a net is thrown into a lake and catches all kinds of fish.
text: «Woso cirbəə b'a ya, an bɔ kʊ rɛ kan zaŋŋa kʊ ŋ n'a zom hi ʊ, an nɩ zɔ do haay gʊrlɛ an nɩ bɔm nɩ bɩ kɩ.

ref: When the net is full, it is dragged to the shore, and the fishermen sit down to separate the fish. They keep the good ones, but throw the bad ones away.
text: Kʊ zɔnɔ a han bɩ n y'a gaa, n n bɔ m hi bɩ lɛ ra. N n bɔkarɛ, ŋnɩ nyɩnta, ŋnɩ zɔnɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ wɔɔ nyʊkɔ ʊ, ŋnɩ ŋ zar mɔr ʊ, ŋnɩ zɔnɔ bʊnyaarɔ wɔɔ lo.

ref: That's how it will be at the end of time. Angels will come and separate the evil people from the ones who have done right.
text: Yɩ a nawʊrɛ ncɩnaaʊ durnya nyarɛ hinni ʊ. Malɛɛkarɔ zɛm bɩ gʊɔɔ bʊnyaarɔ wɔɔ gʊ gʊɔɔ tɩrgarɔ wɔɔ ma,

ref: Jesus asked his disciples if they understood all these things. They said, “Yes, we do.”
text: Yeezuu n a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ lar, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ naa haay woo… ma mɩŋŋa ra?» Ŋ ʊ: «Ɩɩn…»

ref: So he told them, “Every student of the Scriptures who becomes a disciple in the kingdom of heaven is like someone who brings out new and old treasures from the storeroom.” The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Mark
text: A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, wosocikarɩnsaamba biyəə k'a n'a bam Woso cirbəə kʊ bɩ karɩndanyɩ bɩ, bɔ a ʊ kʊ rɛ kan harzaa k'a nɩ hɔ gusire kan hɔ daa kɩ bɔrɛ a arzaka ʊ bɩ kɩ.» Nazarɛtɩ gʊɔɔ n sɩra ka Yeezuu ʊ y (Markɩ

ref: When Jesus had finished telling these stories, he left
text: Yeezuu k'a hɔbɔkʊrɛnoonno nɔɔn duro da an ŋ nya bɩ, a a hʊr lɛɛ bɩ do ʊ,

ref: and went to his hometown. He taught in their synagogue, and the people were so amazed that they asked, “Where does he get all this wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: an doo kʊ k'a tʊr ʊ b'ʊ. A n a sɩŋŋɩda sa, an nɩ hɔ dɩndam kʊ bɩ do sokʊmacɛ b'ʊ, zamaa bɩ n zɛ kədə a hɔdɩndarɛ bɩ tɔ ma. Ŋ n'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Awɔɔ a to mindɔrɛ naa do m ka? A a ba lɔ, an nɩ hoserlo barɛ?

ref: Isn't he the son of the carpenter? Isn't Mary his mother, and aren't James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas his brothers?
text: Gɔwurezaa bɩ nyɩ bɛɛ n'awɔɔ m na? Maarii nyɩ bɛɛ n'awɔɔ m na? Zakkɩ, Zozɛf, Sɩmɔɔn, Ziidi, a danyɩnɔ bɛɛ m nawɔnnɔ ra?

ref: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
text: Awɔɔ danlʊrɔ ba wɔɔ bire ʊ naa ʊ ra? A a ba lɔ kɛɛrɛ, an n'a dam naa do haay barɛ ma?»

ref: So the people were upset because of what he was doing. But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their own family.”
text: Naa do a ka, ŋ n kaanm Yeezuu m. A m naa, Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa ɩ daʊrɛ yɩm bɩncɛ haay ʊ, b'a bɩr a yɩm a kʊ ʊ, a bɩr a yɩm a har ʊ sɔ y.»

ref: And because the people did not have any faith, Jesus did not work many miracles there.
text: Yeezuu n hoser ba b'ʊ gʊta y, ŋ sɩrakarɛwar bɩ tɔ ma.

ref: When Jesus got out of the boat, he saw the large crowd. He felt sorry for them and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Yeezuu k'a bɔ kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ bɩ, a gʊaa cir yɩ gʊta, gʊɔɔ wɔɔ cicir n a nyasʊ, an a sɩŋŋɩda sa, an nɩ ŋ yaabazannɔ waarɛ.

ref: “Come on!” Jesus said. Peter then got out of the boat and started walking on the water toward him.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Bʊr.» Pɩyɛɛr n bɔ kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ, an nɩ tɔm hi bɩ ra k'a ta Yeezuu zi.

ref: so much he swore to give her whatever she wanted.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ n a wer si, a ʊ: hɔ k'a lɛ n taa haay bɩ, a y'a kaŋ ʊ.

ref: and told his officials, “This is John the Baptist! He has come back from death, and that's why he has the power to work these miracles.”
text: A n n'a hɩrɛ a zibəənyɩnɔ m, a ʊ: «Gʊaa naa do, Zaan-Batiisi m! K'a a mɩm bɔ ʊ gəəno bire ʊ bɩ y'a ka, an paŋŋa yɩ, an nɩ hoserlo barɛ.»

ref: Herod had earlier arrested John and had him chained and put in prison. He did this because John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take Herodias, the wife of your brother Philip.”
text: Bala, Zaan a hɩ Hɩrɔɔdɩ m, a ʊ: «A n a ga m k'ɩbɩɩ n a ba ɩ lʊ y!»

ref: But the girl's mother told her to say, “Here on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ da bɩ n a nyʊnnyɔɔta, a ʊ: «A n Zaan-Batiisi mim yɛ laaga burə ʊ.» Nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ n a yɛ maam sɔ.

ref: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.
text: an bʊr m laaga ʊ, an b'a ka nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ b'ʊ, an a si, an aa a ka a da ʊ.

ref: John's followers took his body and buried it. Then they told Jesus what had happened. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Mark
text: Zaan karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n bʊr, ŋnɩ bɩ Zaan gər bɩ sa, ŋn'aa bir, ŋnɩ bɔkarɛ ŋn'aa hɔ k'a a ba bɩ hɩ Yeezuu m. Yeezuu gʊɔɔyaarɔ tʊr tisi soor (Markɩ

ref: But when Peter saw how strong the wind was, he was afraid and started sinking. “Save me, Lord!” he shouted.
text: K'a hɩnhɛr bɩ yɩ an nɩ hɔnm kan paŋŋa kɩ bɩ, a da bɔ ʊ, hi bɩ n'a monɩm, an zee da, a ʊ: «Zuuba, m bɔ lɛ m!»

ref: But they said, “We have only five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: A m bɩ, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Bur soor kan zɔnɔ kɩ hɩɩya wɔɔ n ta m naa ʊ.»

ref: After Jesus heard about John, he crossed Lake Galilee to go to some place where he could be alone. But the crowds found out and followed him on foot from the towns.
text: Yeezuu k'a mim naa do ma bɩ, a a hʊr lɛɛ bɩ do ʊ, an da kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ k'a ta toore ma a deem. Gʊɔɔ cir kʊ ŋ naa yɩ bɩ, ŋ y'a hʊr a kʊrɔ ʊ a gam ma, k'a zu a jɛ ʊ.

ref: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «A n a ga m, ŋn'a hʊr y. K'a hɔbɩrɛ ka ŋ ʊ a zim.»

ref: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a ŋ ka m ʊ naa ʊ».

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: A n lɛ ka, kʊ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ n nyɩnta bur bɩ ra, an bur soorlɔ wɔɔ si kan zɔ hɩɩya bɩ kɩ a wɔ ʊ, an dɩga brama, an barka da Woso m. A n buuro wɔɔ zɩnzɛ ra, an a ka a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'a ka zamaa b'ʊ.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted, Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftovers.
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ haay a bɩ, an ŋ ka. Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ bur kan zɔ kɩ kuuro kʊ ŋ gɔɔta rɔ wɔɔ gʊr kasɩ lɛ ma bueerehɩɩya.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into a boat and start back across the lake. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, Yeezuu a ka karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n da kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ, kʊ ŋ nɩ bra higʊta bɩ gəm deem la, k'a gɔɔta a zamaa bɩ da zaa ra.

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: K'a zamaa bɩ da zaa ra an a nya bɩ, a a hʊr a deem, an ta ci ra, k'a yaa da. Yirbəə k'a da bɩ, a gɔɔta b'ʊ a deem.

ref: By this time the boat was a long way from the shore. It was going against the wind and was being tossed around by the waves.
text: Kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ n a gʊ hi bɩ lɛ bɩ ma, an ta laatʊ hi bɩ bire ʊ. Bala, hɩnhɛr y'a nyam kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ zi, hikurero wɔɔ n'a monɩm kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ

ref: A little while before morning, Jesus came walking on the water toward his disciples.
text: Dɔ k'a bʊr an nɩ mɛm bɩ, a wuti an tɔ hi bɩ ra, an nɩ tarɛ ŋ zi.

ref: When they saw him, they thought he was a ghost. They were terrified and started screaming.
text: Kʊ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ a yɩ an nɩ tam hi bɩ ra bɩ, nyibəə gasʊ ŋ ʊ, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Toor m!» Ŋ n'ɩ zee dam.

ref: At once, Jesus said to them, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, Yeezuu meer ba kan ŋ kɩ, a ʊ: «K'a sɩra ka, mɔɔ m, a da bɩ bɔ ʊ y!»

ref: Peter replied, “Lord, if it really is you, tell me to come to you on the water.”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Zuuba, k'ɩbɩɩ m paan… k'ɩ lɛ ka kʊ m bʊr ɩ zi hi bɩ burə ʊ.»

ref: At once, Jesus reached out his hand. He helped Peter up and said, “You surely don't have much faith. Why do you doubt?”
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, Yeezuu a wɔ nyɔɔ an a nyasʊ, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ sɩrakarɛ poore! A lɔ ɩbɩɩ n sɩnsɩm ka?»

ref: When Jesus and Peter got into the boat, the wind died down.
text: Ŋ n'a da kʊ ʊ, ŋnɩ gasʊ kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ hɩnhɛr bɩ n jɩm.

ref: The men in the boat worshiped Jesus and said, “You really are the Son of God!” Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Mark
text: Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kukurə zɛ a taa, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Woso Nyɩ n'ɩbɩɩ m paan….» Zenezarɛtɩ yaabazannɔ waarɛ (Markɩ

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and came to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Kʊ ŋ higʊta bɩ zɛ ra bɩ, ŋ yaa lee ku Zenezarɛtɩ ʊ.

ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: Kʊ bɩ do gʊɔɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ Yeezuu yɩ, ŋn'a dɔ bɩ, ŋ yaa a hɩ kʊ b'ʊ haay kan kʊrɔ jɩnjɛɛnnɔ wɔɔ kɩ, ŋ ʊ, a bʊr. Ŋ n bʊr yaabazannɔ wɔɔ m haay Yeezuu zi.

ref: They begged him just to let them touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Ŋ n a yɛ han, k'a a wɔ da a huu lɛ tɔkkɛ ma. Bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a wɔ da ma rɔ wɔɔ waarɛ yɩ haay.

ref: Jesus answered, “Every plant that my Father in heaven did not plant will be pulled up by the roots.
text: A n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Hɔ haay kʊ mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ n a sɔɔn bɩ, n y'a wʊrm.

ref: “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? They don't even wash their hands before they eat.”
text: «Bɔ m, ɩbɩɩ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ bɩr zum wɔɔ yaabɔɔ yɩnnɩbayɩrɔ wɔɔ ʊ? Ŋ n'ɩ hɔ bɩm, bɩ ŋ n a wɔ hir y. A n a ga nɩ y.»

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: B'awɔɔ ʊ, kʊ gʊaa a hɩ a zɩ m, gɛɛ a da m, a ʊ: ‹Daahan kʊ mɔɔ m ga m, m a ka ɩbɩɩ ʊ bɩ, Woso hɔ nɩ.›

ref: Is this any way to show respect to your parents? You ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching.
text: Pakra bɛɛ m kʊ gʊaa bɩ do m a da a zɩ kan a da kɩ han y. Woso lemim bɩ zar awɔɔ nawʊrɛ ncɩnaaʊ a yɩnnɩbayɩrɔ wɔɔ minto.

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.” What Really Makes People Unclean (Mark
text: Daʊrɛ kʊ ŋ n'a kam mɔɔ ʊ bɩ, vam m. Bala, durnya mim ŋn'a dɩndam gʊɔɔ m, ɩ ʊ Woso hɔ m.» Hɔ k'a nɩ dintim kam gʊaa ma (Markɩ

ref: Stay away from those Pharisees! They are like blind people leading other blind people, and all of them will fall into a ditch.”
text: K'a ŋ to, bʊʊrɔ nɩ bʊʊrɔ gaam. Bɩ, kʊ bʊʊ bʊr, an nɩ bʊʊ gaarɛ, ŋ gʊaa hɩɩya haay y'a zarm yaa ʊ!»

ref: Jesus did not say a word. But the woman kept following along and shouting, so his disciples came up and asked him to send her away.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ nʊ y. A karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ ma, ŋn'a yɛ han, ŋ ʊ: «La lʊ naa do ra, bala, a ɩ wɔɔ kɔm kan a zeedarɛ kɩ.»

ref: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
text: Hɔ k'a nɩ gasʊm gʊaa lɛ ʊ an ta a nɔ ʊ bɩ bɛɛ nɩ dintim kam ma y, bɩ hɔ k'a nɩ bɔm gʊaa lɛ ʊ bɩ nɩ dintim kam ma.»

ref: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
text: A m naa, karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ ma, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ naa ma bɩ, ɩbɩɩ dɔ ɩ ʊ, a busu ŋ ma ra?»

ref: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom naa do jɩ hɩ ʊ m.»

ref: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ gɔɔta, awɔɔ n a jɩ dɔ ra?

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Awɔɔ n a dɔ, a ʊ, hɔ haay k'a nɩ gasʊm gʊaa lɛ ʊ, an nɩ tarɛ a nɔ ʊ bɩ, ta an nawʊm, an nɩ bɔm garga ra ra?

ref: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
text: Bɩ, hɔ k'a nɩ bɔm gʊaa lɛ ʊ bɩ, a to a nawʊm gʊaa heer ʊ, bɩ nɩ dintim kam ma.

ref: Out of your heart come evil thoughts, murder, unfaithfulness in marriage, vulgar deeds, stealing, telling lies, and insulting others.
text: Bala, gʊaa heer hɔɔndarɛ bʊnyaarɔ wɔɔ nɩ bɔŋ ʊ: gərzɛrɛ, jaandarɛ, koorbarɛ, gʊɔɔ tɔ zarlɛ, kan sʊnsɔwʊrɛ kɩ.

ref: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
text: Nawɔnnɔ nɩ dintim kam gʊaa ma, bɩ hɔbɩrɛ bɩ, ɩ n ɩ wɔ hir bɩ bɩr dintim kam gʊaa ma y.» Yeezuu bɔ Zidee kʊ ʊ (Markɩ

ref: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ nyɔɔ n y'a ʊ Israyɛl gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ bɔ kʊ ʊ kan seero kʊ ŋ nyaam nɔ wɔɔ kɩ zi.»

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Gʊɔɔ cir gʊta bʊr zi, lʊgɔrɔ m, bʊʊrɔ m, gandeenzannɔ m, bəətinno kan yaabazannɔ vantɔɔ kɩ m, ŋnɩ bɩ ŋ nyɩnta Yeezuu taa, an ŋ waa.

ref: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
text: Yeezuu k'a cem b'ʊ bɩ, a ta Tiir kan Sidɔn kɩ kʊrɔ wɔɔ ʊ.

ref: Suddenly a Canaanite woman from there came out shouting, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on me! My daughter is full of demons.”
text: Lʊ nɩ b'ʊ an ta Yeezuu zi, zɩɩfʊ bɛɛ nɩ y, Kanaan do nyɩ m, an a hɩ Yeezuu m, a ʊ: «M cicir zɛ Zuuba, *Davɩd Nyɩ, mɔɔ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ zinə n ta m, an nɩ fɩr kam ma gʊta.»

ref: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
text: Bɩ, lʊ b'a gu an bɩ kukurə zɛ a taa, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Zuuba, bʊr ɩ da mɔɔ han!»

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «A n a ga m, n nɩ nyɩnɔ hɔbɩrɛ sa, n n a ka jinnyɩnɔ ʊ y.»

ref: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
text: Lʊ bɩ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Sɩra m, Zuuba, baa jinnyɩnɔ wɔɔ ɩ hɔbɩrɛ bʊtʊ k'a a lo ŋ zuubarɔ wɔɔ taa bɩ gʊrlɛ, ŋn'a bɩrɛ.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
text: A m naa Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Lʊ, ɩ sɩrakarɛ gandaa ʊ! Ɩ hɔ k'ɩ lɛ n taa bɩ yɩm!» Lɛɛm bɩ, a nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ b'a waa wakatɩ bɩ do ʊ. Yeezuu yaabazannɔ waa gʊta

ref: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
text: Yeezuu a hʊr b'ʊ, an ta Galilee higʊta lɛ ra, an aa der ci ra, an nyɩnta.

ref: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ n zɛ kədə, kʊ bəətinno nɩ meer barɛ, gandeenzannɔ n a gannɔ yɩ, lʊgɔrɔ nɩ tam mɩŋŋa, bʊʊrɔ nɩ lɛɛ yɩm bɩ minto. Ŋ y'a lɛ bɔ Israyɛl Woso ʊ. Yeezuu gʊɔɔyaarɔ tʊr tisi si (Markɩ

ref: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
text: Yeezuu a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ bir an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Zamaa bɩ cicir ɩ mɔɔ zɛm, bala, a dɔmɩm kaakʊ hɔ m naa, ŋ nɩ kan mɔɔ kɩ, hɔsɩ ba ŋ ʊ, ŋn'a bɩrɛ y. Mɔɔ lɛ baa kʊ m ŋ da zaa ra kan nɔ kɩ y. Kʊ n bɛɛ nɩ, ŋ y'a dundom zaa ra.»

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «K'ʊ bur yɩ ka pooyaa ʊ naa ʊ, ʊ a ka gʊɔɔ cir naa ʊ, k'an ŋ ka?»
