ref: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
text: Yeezuu n ŋ lar, a ʊ: «Bur karɔ n ta awɔɔ ʊ?» Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Saanhɩna kan zɔ poorenyɩnɔ kɩ.»

ref: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
text: A n lɛ ka kʊ zamaa bɩ n nyɩnta tara ʊ.

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: A n bur saanhɩna bɩ sa kan zɔnɔ wɔɔ kɩ an yaa da Woso m barka, an n'a zɩnzɛm la, an n'a kam a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ ʊ, ŋn'a kam, zamaa b'ʊ.

ref: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
text: Ŋ n a bɩ, an ŋ ka, an a yɔ, ŋn'a kuuro gʊr kasɩ lɛ ma saanhɩna.

ref: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
text: Bɩ jɛ ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu ŋ da zaa ra, an da kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ an ta Magadaan ʊ.

ref: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
text: A a ba lɔ awɔɔ n a jɩ ma, a ʊ, bur bɛɛ mɔɔ n'a hɩm ma? K'a dundo a zi kan farizɛɛnrɔ, sadisɛɛnrɔ nyʊnyɔɔ bɩ kɩ.»

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
text: Ŋ yaa lee ku kʊ kʊ n n'a hɩm ma, ŋ ʊ: Filipi-Sezaaree kʊrɔ wɔɔ ʊ. Yeezuu laaka da a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ han, a ʊ: «Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: nka m Gʊaanyɩ bɩ nɩ?»

ref: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
text: A m naa, karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n bɔkarɛ, ŋn'a jɩ ma, ŋn'a dɔ ŋ ʊ, bur barɛ nyʊnyɔɔ bɛɛ an n'a hɩm ma y. Farizɛɛnrɔ kan sadisɛɛnrɔ kɩ hɔdɩndarɛ an n'a hɩm ma. Pɩyɛɛr kasɛtɩ karɛ (Markɩ

ref: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «Kʊ woso lɛ a zo, b'awɔɔ ʊ, lɛɛ yɩm mɩŋŋa, bala, brama bɩ piin….

ref: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
text: Dɔgta m, b'awɔɔ ʊ la bam her, bala, brama bɩ piin… b'a kundə sɔ. Awɔɔ brama mim dɔ, b'awɔɔ b'a dam ma a hɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ bam dɩɩtaa rɔ wɔɔ jɩ dɔ y.

ref: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
text: Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Gɔsɩnnɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: Zaan-Batiisi m, nawɔnnɔ ʊ: *Elii m, gɔsɩnnɔ a ya, ŋ ʊ: Zeremii m, gɛɛ, wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ deem m.»

ref: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
text: A n a sa wakatɩ bɩ do ma, Yeezuu a sɩŋŋɩda sa, an nɩ hɔ dɩndarɛ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Pakra m, kʊ m ta Zerizalɛm ʊ kʊ m aa fɩr yɩ gʊta gʊɔɔgusinno kan wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kɩ wɔ ʊ. Ŋ mɔɔ zɛm gər. B'a dɔmɩm kaakʊ hinni ʊ, Woso mɔɔ mɩm bɔŋ ʊ.»

ref: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a dundo a zi, k'a a ba laatʊ farizɛɛnrɔ kan sadisɛɛnrɔ kɩ nyʊnyɔɔ bɩ ma.»

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Ŋ n bɔkarɛ, ŋn'a hɩm kʊ m, ŋ ʊ: «Kʊ wɔɔ n bur sa bɩ y'a ka an naa hɩ.»

ref: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
text: Bala, Gʊaanyɩ bɩ zɛrɛ a Zɩ lɛbɔʊrɛ nɔ ʊ kan a malɛɛkarɔ kɩ. Dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ bɩ, a gʊaa biyəə zibəə nyɔɔdʊ kaŋ ʊ.»

ref: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
text: Bɩ, mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ ɩbɩɩ m, Pɩyɛɛr n'ɩbɩɩ m, bɩ ci naa do mɔɔ n nɩ m sokʊma bɩ dɔrɛ a burə ʊ. Zɛ kan a paŋŋa kɩ bɩ bʊrm la y.

ref: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
text: A n ŋ lar, a ʊ: «B'awɔɔ, awɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: nka m mɔɔ nɩ?»

ref: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n meerbaa bɩ si, an a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Krista bɩ n'ɩbɩɩ m, Woso k'an mɩsɩr bɩ nyɩ.»

ref: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Mimbirenyɩntazaa n'ɩbɩɩ m, Sɩmɔɔn, Zonas nyɩnnɩ, bala, gʊaa bɛɛ sɩra naa do hɩnka ɩbɩɩ nɩ y. Mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ m.

ref: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Mɔɔ Woso cirbəə cɛnyɩrɔ karɛ ɩbɩɩ ʊ, hɔ haay k'ɩbɩɩ n n'a kʊsɩm tara burə ʊ bɩ, n y'a kʊsɩm arzana ʊ, hɔ haay k'ɩbɩɩ n n'a hʊrm tara burə ʊ bɩ, n y'a hʊrm arzana ʊ.»

ref: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
text: Naa do jɛ ʊ, a lɛ ka ŋ ʊ mɩŋŋa, a ʊ: ŋ b'a hɩ gɔsɩ m, ŋ ʊ, Krista n'awɔɔ nɩ y. Yeezuu fɩryɩrɛ mim (Markɩ

ref: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n Yeezuu gaa, an bɔ m toore ma, an nɩ nyaa bam kan kɩ, a ʊ. «Woso dɔrɛ ɩ ma kan naa kɩ Zuuba, mim naa do b'ɩ b'ɩ yɩrɛ y.»

ref: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu a bɔ, an a hɩ Pɩyɛɛr m, a ʊ: «Ɩ jɛ bɔ mɔɔ jɩr ʊ! Sʊtaana, mɔɔ makra ɩbɩɩ n'a bam, ɩbɩɩ hɔɔndarɛ bɩ n bɔ kʊ ʊ kan Woso hɔ kɩ y. Gʊɔɔ hɔɔndarɛ m.» Yeezuu zaa zure

ref: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Bɩ kur bɩ, Yeezuu a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Kʊ gʊaa lɛ taa k'a zu mɔɔ ʊ, k'an sɔ a mɩŋŋa m, k'an a gɔdarkʊra busu, an zu mɔɔ ʊ.

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
text: Bala, kʊ gʊaa lɛ taa k'a a meeyaa bʊmbɔ, a b'a meeyaa bʊmbɔrɛ yɩrɛ y. Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa ga mɔɔ tɔ ma a y'a meeyaa bʊmbɔrɛ yɩrɛ.

ref: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
text: Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa a so durnya hɔ haay ʊ, an bɔkarɛ an kʊrɔ mɩsɩrbaa k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ ma, a nyɔɔdʊ ɩ ka? Gɛɛ gʊaa y'a mɩsɩrbaa lʊrm kan bɔ kɩ?

ref: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
text: Yeezuu a ya, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ naa ʊ naa, gɔsɩnnɔ zɛ b'a kure, kʊ ŋ n Gʊaanyɩ bɩ yɩ an bʊr a cirbəə nɔ ʊ y.»

ref: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
text: A n a lʊr ŋ mɩm ʊ. A meer nɩ yɔm am wosogure hɔ bɩ m, a huuro wɔɔ nɩ nyakɩ bɔm, ŋn'a ba fu fɩrr….

ref: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
text: A m naa, karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a dɔ, ŋ ʊ, Zaan-Batiisi an n'a hɩm ma. Yeezuu zinə bɔ nyɩ mɛ ʊ

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Dɔmɩm sɔrdɩ kur bɩ, Yeezuu Pɩyɛɛr gaa, kan Zakkɩ kɩ a danzaa Zaan kɩ, an bɔ ŋ nɩ toore ma a minto, ci jintə mim ʊ.

ref: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
text: *Elii n a hɩnka ŋ nɩ kan Moyiisi kɩ, ŋn'ɩ meer bam kan Yeezuu kɩ.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a hɩ Yeezuu m, a ʊ: «Zuuba, kʊ wɔɔ ɩ naa ʊ, a mɩŋŋa. K'ɩ lɛ taa, kʊ m bur ba kaakʊ: ɩbɩɩ hɔ deem, Moyiisi hɔ deem, Elii hɔ deem.»

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: K'a nɩ meer barɛ ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, warkʊ k'a nɩ yɔm bʊr an b'a ku ŋ la. Meerbaa n a to warkʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Ba mɔɔ Nyɩ m na. Mɔɔ ŋʊa gʊta. A mɔɔ nɔ yɩ paan…. K'a a tʊr ka ma.»

ref: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ naa ma bɩ, ŋ y'a dundo, ŋn'a meer kunku tara ma, dabɔʊ gʊta k'a ŋ nyasʊ bɩ minto.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Yeezuu n a zɔ ŋ ma, an a wɔ da ŋ ma, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «K'a wuti, a da bɩ bɔ ʊ y!»

ref: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
text: Kʊ ŋ y'a bɔ ŋnɩ dɩga bɩ, ŋ n gɔsɩ yɩ dɔ, kʊ Yeezuu y'a deem bɛɛ y.

ref: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
text: A karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a lar, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ m wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ ʊ, Elii lɛɛkarɛ an bʊr?»

ref: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Sɩra m, Elii nɩ lɛɛkarɛ an bʊr k'a bɩ hɔ haay banka.

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, Elii bʊr kara, bɩ ŋ n a dɔ y, a hɔɔnhɔ ŋ y'a ba m. Gʊaanyɩ bɩ fɩr yɩrɛ ŋ wɔ ʊ maam sɔ.» (Markɩ

ref: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
text: Kʊ ŋ lee ku zamaa cir gʊta bɩ zi bɩ, gʊaa a zɔ Yeezuu ma, an kukurə zɛ a taa, an a hɩ m, a ʊ:

ref: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
text: «Karɩnsaamba, m nyɩnnɩ cicir zɛ, hɔɔ n'a zɛm, an nɩ fɩr yɩm gʊta, a tɩr a da sɛ ʊ, gɛɛ hi ʊ.

ref: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
text: Mɔɔ n bʊr m ɩ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ zi, bɩ, ŋ n a da ma, ŋn'a waa y.»

ref: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
text: Yeezuu n meerbaa bɩ sa, a ʊ: «Zamaana sɩrakarɛwar zannɔ, gʊɔɔ bʊnyaarɔ, mɔɔ dɔmɩm bam karɔ kan awɔɔ kɩ? Dɔmɩm karɔ mɔɔ nɩ m yarɛ, m yɛ kɔrɛ ma awɔɔ minto? K'a bʊr m mɔɔ zi.»

ref: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
text: Yeezuu n həən si zinə bɩ ma, an bɔ nyɩ bɩ mɛ ʊ, nyɩ bɩ n laafɩɩ yɩ wakatɩ bɩ do ʊ.

ref: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ Yeezuu ma, ŋn'a lar a deem, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ y'a ka wɔɔ n ʊ da ma, ʊ la zinə bɩ ra?»

ref: Jesus replied: It is because you don't have enough faith! But I can promise you this. If you had faith no larger than a mustard seed, you could tell this mountain to move from here to there. And it would. Everything would be possible for you. Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Mark
text: Bɩ, zinə ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, hɔ ba ʊ an b'a dam ma an a ka, an bɔ, kʊ yaadarɛ bɛɛ kan lɛkʊsɩrɛ kɩ y.]» Yeezuu a ya, an n'a zɛ mim dɩndam (Markɩ

ref: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
text: Hindeem ʊ, karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a so kʊ ma haay Galilee ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «N Gʊaanyɩ bɩ dam gʊɔɔ wɔ ʊ.

ref: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
text: Ŋ y'a zɛm, b'a dɔmɩm kaakʊ hinni ʊ, Woso y'a mɩm bɔŋ ʊ.» Meerbaa naa do ŋ heer zar gʊta. Yawʊr karɛ

ref: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
text: Kʊ Yeezuu kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ lee ku Kapɛrnawum ʊ bɩ, Wosocɛ yawʊrsinno a zɔ Pɩyɛɛr ma, ŋn'a lar, ŋ ʊ: «Awɔɔ karɩnsaamba bɩ bɩr Wosocɛ yawʊr kam na?»

ref: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «A y'a kam.» Pɩyɛɛr k'a lee ku har ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu lɛɛ ka m an a lar, a ʊ: «Sɩmɔɔn, kan ɩbɩɩ kɩ, nkɔɔ durnya cinno nɔɔm nɩ yawʊr sire ŋ han? Ŋ nyɩnɔ m gɛɛ, saanɔɔ nɩ?»

ref: Peter answered, “From foreigners.” Jesus replied, “Then their own people don't have to pay.
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Saanɔɔ m.» Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «K'a maam ŋ nyɩnɔ wɔɔ ba ŋ barla y.

ref: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
text: Bɩ k'ʊ lɛ baa kʊ ŋ n'a hɩ, ŋ ʊ, ʊ ʊ zar a ma bɩ, ta higʊta b'ʊ, ɩ yaa ka zo hi b'ʊ. Zɔ niŋŋə k'a nɩ lɛɛ kam an ka bɩ mom bɩ, bɔ zɔ bɩ do m, ɩ lɛ gʊ, ɩbɩɩ busoo yɩrɛ a lɛ b'ʊ, an nɩ mɔɔ kan ɩbɩɩ kɩ yawʊr karɛ bɔm. K'ɩ bɔ ʊ, ɩ b'a ka yawʊrsinno wɔɔ ʊ.»

ref: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
text: K'ɩ mɩnyaa y'ɩ dam mimbʊnyaa barɛ ma, k'ɩ bɔ ʊ ɩ zo laatʊ ɩ ma, k'ɩ gasʊ ɩ mɩnyaa deem m mɩsɩrbaa k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ, bɩ jɩra, gasʊ k'ɩbɩɩ nawʊrɛ ɩ mɩm hɩɩya m sɛhar ʊ bɩ m. Ser k'a nyaam hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom darɛ (Likki

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Ɩbɩɩ n ɩ da ma sɔ, ɩ ɩ mim bɩ cicir zɛ, amba mɔɔ ɩbɩɩ cicir zɛ bɩ n na?›

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Gʊaa k'a n'a mɩŋŋa zerm am nyɩ hɔ bɩ m bɩ, bɩ zaa n jɩtaarɛ Woso cirbəə b'ʊ.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: Gʊaa k'a nyɩ poore si mɔɔ tɔ ma am nyɩ naa do m bɩ, mɔɔ mɩŋŋa a yɩ m si.» Dundoazi mim (Markɩ

ref: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
text: Kʊ gʊaa bɩ lɛ baa k'a tʊr ka brɔɔ ma sɔ, k'ɩ hɩ kristagʊɔɔ sokʊma bɩ m. K'a ʊ a b'a tʊr kam kristagʊɔɔ sokʊma bɩ ma, k'ɩ dɩga ma kɩfɩr gɛɛ, yawʊrsirezaa.

ref: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
text: «Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa a ka nyɩnɔ kʊ ŋ sɩra ka mɔɔ rɔ nɔɔn duro gʊaa deem n a do mimbʊnyaa nɔ ʊ, kʊ n jɛnlajaa gʊta kʊsɩ a wer ma, n n a da higʊta ʊ, a jɩra.

ref: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
text: K'ɩ wɔ gɛɛ, ɩ gam y'ɩ dam mimbʊnyaa barɛ ma, k'ɩ zɛ ra, ɩ zo laatʊ ɩ ma k'ɩ gasʊ mɩsɩrbaa k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ b'ʊ, ɩ wɔ deem m, gɛɛ ɩ gam deem m, bɩ jɩra, gasʊ k'ɩbɩɩ nawʊrɛ ɩ wɔ hɩɩya m, gɛɛ ɩ gam hɩɩya m sɛhar k'a lihire ba ʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ bɩ m.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: K'ɩ danyɩ a zar ɩ ma, k'ɩ ta zi, ɩ yaa hɔ k'a ba ɩbɩɩ m bɩ hɩ m, ɩbɩɩ kan kɩ a hɩɩya. K'a ɩbɩɩ mim bɩ si, k'ɩ dɔ ɩ ʊ, awɔɔ a zɛ kʊ ra.

ref: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
text: Bala Gʊaanyɩ bɩ, bʊr a ʊ, k'a gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nyaam nɔ wɔɔ bʊmbɔ.]

ref: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
text: K'a ser bɩ yɩ, a heer yɩm nyɩnta kan kɩ an lɛ da bunɛɛhʊ a yaa nɛɛhʊ rɔ kʊ ŋ n nyaam nɔ wɔɔ ra.

ref: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
text: Awɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ lɛ baa sɔ kʊ nyɩnɔ nɔɔn duro gʊaa deem n nyaam y. Kʊ bɔ zaa ʊ mim

ref: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
text: Bɩ, k'a n a tʊr ka ɩ ma, k'ɩ gʊaa na ɩ ma deem gɛɛ hɩɩya, amba Wosoci a hɩ, a ʊ: ‹Gʊaa hɩɩya gɛɛ kaakʊ y'a ga mim kasɛtɩ barɛ m.›

ref: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: hɔ haay, k'awɔɔ n'a gum ma tara burə ʊ naa ʊ, n y'a gum ma sɔ Woso cirbəə nɔ ʊ. Hɔ haay k'awɔɔ nɩ yɛm ma tara burə ʊ, n yɛm ma sɔ Woso cirbəə nɔ ʊ.

ref: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
text: Mɔɔ m ya, m n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m sɔ: K'awɔɔ gʊaa hɩɩya a zɛ kʊ ra tara burə ʊ naa ʊ, k'a hɔ niŋŋə haay yɛ yaadarɛ nɔ ʊ, mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ hɔ bɩ do karɛ ŋ ʊ.

ref: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
text: Bala, lɛɛ kʊ gʊɔɔ a so kʊ ma ʊ hɩɩya gɛɛ kaakʊ mɔɔ tɔ ma bɩ, mɔɔ yɩ ŋ bireyaa ʊ.» Sugur ka kʊ ʊ mim

ref: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a zɔ Yeezuu ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Zuuba, kʊ mɔɔ danyɩ a zar mɔɔ ma, gallɛm karɔ mɔɔ nɩ sugur kaŋ ʊ? Gallɛm saanhɩna gɛɛ?»

ref: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
text: «Naa do minto bɩ, Woso cirbəə bɩ bɔ kʊ rɛ kan cir k'a wuti k'a a zibəənyɩnɔ lar ŋ zibəə ma.

ref: As he was doing this, one official was brought in who owed him
text: A sɩŋŋɩda k'a k'a ba bɩ, bʊr n y'a ʊ zibəənyɩ deem m, an ta talan waa bu zɩm m.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Bɩ, gʊaa bɩ ba hɔsɩ m an b'a zɩm bɩ karɛ y. A m bɩ, cir bɩ n lɛ ka, kʊ n nɩ gʊaa bɩ hɛɛ kan a lʊ kɩ, a nyɩnɔ kɩ, kan hɔ niŋŋə k'an ta m haay kɩ, k'a cɩnta zɩm bɩ bɩncɛ ʊ.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: A m naa, zibəənyɩ bɩ n kukurə zɛ cir bɩ taa, an cicir ba a lɛ ra, a ʊ: ‹M cicir zɛ, mɔɔ yɩ m zɩm bɩ kam haay!›

ref: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
text: Gʊaa bɩ cicir n cir bɩ nyasʊ, an zɩm bɩ to m, an a da zaa ra.

ref: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
text: A mim bɩ n kukurə zɛ a taa, an cicir ba a lɛ ra, a ʊ: ‹M cicir zɛ, mɔɔ yɩ m zɩm bɩ kam.›

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: Bɩ, gʊaa b'a gu, an ta, an aa a ka, n n a du lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ, k'an tolle ba, an zɩm bɩ ka, an a nya.

ref: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
text: Zibəənyɩnɔ mɩsɩrɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ naa yɩ bɩ, ŋ heer a zar gʊta, ŋnɩ ta, ŋn'aa hɔ haay woo… k'a ba bɩ hɩ cir bɩ m.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Cir bɩ n zibəənyɩ bɩ bir an a hɩ m, a ʊ: ‹Zibəənyɩ bʊnyaa, mɔɔ ɩ zɩm bɩ to ɩbɩɩ m haay, k'ɩbɩɩ cicir ba mɔɔ lɛ ra bɩ minto.

ref: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
text: Cir bɩ nɔ n ma ʊ gʊta, an gʊaa bɩ du lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ k'an fɩr yɩ, k'an zɩm bɩ ka haay.»

ref: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
text: Yeezuu n a ya, an a na ma, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ Zɩ k'a nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ y'a barɛ awɔɔ m ncɩnaaʊ, k'awɔɔ bɩr sugur kam kʊ ʊ kan a heer kɩ haay y.»

ref: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
text: Yeezuu k'a naa do dɩnda haay bɩ, a a hʊr Galilee ʊ, an ta Zidee ʊ, Zʊrdɛn kur.

ref: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
text: Minno y'a hʊnhɔ y'a karɛ gʊɔɔ bɩr lʊ sire y: Gʊɔɔ ta ʊ, yɩ kʊ ŋ y'a ʊ bɩ, ŋ b'a dam ma ŋnɩ lʊ si y, gɔsɩnnɔ ta ʊ, gʊɔɔ y'a ba ŋ nɩ, gɔsɩnnɔ n ta ʊ, ŋ bɩr lʊ sire y Woso cirbəə bɩ minto. Gʊaa k'a n'a dam ma, an meerbaa naa do si bɩ, k'an a si.» Nyɩnɔ poorero barkadaʊrɛ (Markɩ

ref: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
text: Zamaa cir gʊta zu a jɛ ʊ, an ŋ yaabazannɔ waa b'ʊ.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ n a zɔ ma, k'a nyasʊ meerbaa nɔ ʊ, ŋnɩ laaka da han, ŋ ʊ: «Gʊaa a da ma, an la a lʊ ra, baa kʊ mim kara m na?»

ref: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
text: A n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ n a karɩnda ba Wosoci b'ʊ ra? A ʊ: ‹A sɩŋŋɩda ʊ bɩ, Woso gʊaa da kan lʊ kɩ.›

ref: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
text: A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: ‹Bɩ y'a ka: gʊaa y'a gʊrɛ a zɩ kan a da kɩ ma, an a zɛ kʊ ra kan a lʊ kɩ, ŋ gʊaa hɩɩya bɩ n a ba deem.›

ref: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
text: Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, ŋ bɩ hɩɩya dɔ y, ŋ deem. Bɩ, gʊaa bɩ hɔ kʊ Woso a zɛ kʊ ra bɩ gʊngʊm kʊ ma y.»

ref: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ n a lar, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ m Moyiisi ʊ, n nɩ harcɩntarɛ kaansʊrɛ ci ba, kʊ n nɩ bɔkarɛ n nɩ la lʊ ra?»

ref: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ heerkarɛ minto y'a ka Moyiisi n lɛ ka k'awɔɔ n la a lannɔ ra. Bɩ, durnya sɩŋŋɩda ʊ bɩ, ncɩnaaʊ bɛɛ y.

ref: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: kʊ gʊaa la a lʊ ra, bɩ lʊ bɩ n jaan da y, an lʊ vanta sa, gʊaa bɩ do, jaandarɛzaa m.»

ref: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «Gʊaa haay bɛɛ nɩ hɔdɩndarɛ naa do sire y, bɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ Woso paŋŋa ka ŋ ʊ kʊ ŋ n'a si rɔ wɔɔ, brɔɔ y'a sim.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Gʊɔɔ bʊr nyɩnɔ poorero m Yeezuu zi, k'an a wɔ ka ŋ mim la, an yaa da ŋ tɔ ma, bɩ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n yɛ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ nɩ y, ŋn'a bam kan ŋ kɩ.

ref: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
text: Bɩ kur bɩ, gʊaa a zɔ Yeezuu ma, an a lar, a ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, bɔ mɩŋŋa mɔɔ n'a barɛ m mɩsɩrbaa k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ yɩ?»

ref: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
text: A n a wɔ ka nyɩnɔ wɔɔ mim la, an a hʊr lɛɛ bɩ do ʊ. Yeezuu kan arzakazaa kɩ (Markɩ

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Bɔ m, ɩbɩɩ nɩ mɔɔ larm hɔ mɩŋŋa mim ma? Gʊaa mɩŋŋa ba ʊ kʊ Woso deem bɛɛ y. K'ɩbɩɩ lɛ taa k'ɩ gasʊ mɩsɩrbaa k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ, k'ɩ zu Woso lɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ.»

ref: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
text: Yeezuu k'a naa yɩ bɩ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a nyɩnɔ poorero wɔɔ to, ŋnɩ bʊr mɔɔ zi, a b'a ta ŋ nɩ tolle y. Bala, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ bɔ kʊ ʊ kan nawɔnnɔ kɩ rɔ wɔɔ, ŋ hɔ m Woso cirbəə bɩ m.»

ref: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
text: Bɩ, lɛɛkarɛzannɔ gʊta nyɩntam jɛzannɔ, jɛzannɔ gʊta n nyɩnta lɛɛkarɛzannɔ.»

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: ɩ zɩ kan ɩ da kɩ da ʊ, ɩ ɩ mim ŋʊa amba ɩ ɩ mɩŋŋa ŋʊa bɩ m.»

ref: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
text: Nyukumbueere bɩ n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ zu lɛrɔ nɔɔn duro haay ʊ, bɔ ɩ gɔɔta mɔɔ n'a barɛ?»

ref: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «K'ɩbɩɩ lɛ taa k'ɩ yɩ mɩŋŋa paan… k'ɩ ta ɩ yaa hɔ k'a n'ɩbɩɩ ʊ haay bɩ hɛɛ, ɩ busoo bɩ ka ŋʊazannɔ ʊ. Maam bɩ, ɩbɩɩ arzaka yɩm arzana ʊ. K'ɩ bʊr, ɩ bɩr zum mɔɔ ʊ.»

ref: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
text: Bɩ, nyukumbueere bɩ k'a naa ma bɩ, a heer a zar gʊta, an a hʊr kan heerzar kɩ, bala, arzakazaa gʊta m.

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: A m naa, Yeezuu n a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: a wer ta ʊ kʊ arzakazaa n gasʊ Woso cirbəə nɔ ʊ.

ref: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
text: Mɔɔ m ya, m n'a hɩrɛ: Yoonde gasʊ huuka yaa ʊ zanta, an lɛ da arzakazaa gasʊ Woso cirbəə bɩ nɔ ʊ m.»

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n zɛ kədə meerbaa naa do minto, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Bɩ nka n'a dam ma kɛɛrɛ an bʊmbɔrɛ yɩ?»

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Yeezuu n dɩga ŋ ma, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Gʊɔɔ b'a dam ma y, bɩ, Woso a da hɔ haay ma.»

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n meerbaa bɩ sa, a ʊ: «Dɩga, wɔɔ hɔ haay to k'ʊ zu ɩbɩɩ ʊ, a nyɩntam wɔɔ ma lɔ?»

ref: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: kʊ Gʊaanyɩ bɩ bʊr an nɩ nyɩntam a cirbəəduu paŋŋazaa bɩ ra durnya daa b'ʊ, awɔɔ bueerehɩɩyɔɔ k'a zu mɔɔ ʊ naa, awɔɔ nyɩntam sɔ duuro ra kan mɔɔ kɩ k'ʊ Israyɛl do bueerehɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ dʊdɔ ka.

ref: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
text: «Woso cirbəə bɩ bɔ kʊ rɛ kan gʊaa k'a wuti dɔgta mɩŋŋa m, k'a gʊɔɔ sa a dɩvɛnsɩra zibəə ma bɩ kɩ.

ref: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
text: A n lɛ sa kan zibəəzannɔ wɔɔ kɩ k'a *deniyee deem ka ŋ ʊ hindeem zibəə busoo. A n ŋ nyɔɔ a hɔsɩra b'ʊ.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: A n a ya, an a bɔ dɔgta wakatɩ nɛɛhʊ ma an gʊɔɔ gɔsɩnnɔ yɩ, ŋn'ɩ jɩnɩm daasɩyaa ʊ, bɩ ŋ ba zibəə nɩ y.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: ‹K'a ta mɔɔ hɔsɩra b'ʊ sɔ, mɔɔ y'awɔɔ zibəə busoo k'a a ga m karɛ awɔɔ ʊ.›

ref: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
text: Ŋ n ta hɔsɩra b'ʊ. A n a ya, an bɔ sinsebir ʊ kan yirbəə wakatɩ kaakʊ kɩ ma, an a ya, an a ba maam sɔ.

ref: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
text: Yirbəə wakatɩ soor k'a a ku bɩ, a a ya, an bɔ, an gʊɔɔ vantɔɔ yɩ ŋn'ɩ jɩnɩm daasɩyaa b'ʊ, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: ‹Bɔ m, awɔɔ n gɔɔta naa ʊ dɔgta m saa…, b'awɔɔ ba zibəə nɩ y?›

ref: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ n a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: ‹Gɔsɩ n wɔɔ sa zibəə ma y.› A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: ‹K'a ta mɔɔ hɔsɩra b'ʊ sɔ a yaa zi ba.›

ref: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
text: Lɛɛ k'a bʊr k'a lihi bɩ, dɩvɛnsɩra zaa b'a hɩ a zibəədɩgamazaa bɩ m, a ʊ: ‹Zibəəzannɔ wɔɔ bir, ɩ ŋ busoo ka ŋ ʊ. A sɩŋŋɩda ba gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ bʊr jɛ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ ma, k'ɩ nyambʊr lɛɛkarɛzannɔ wɔɔ ma.›

ref: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
text: Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ bʊr yirbəə wakatɩ soor ma rɔ wɔɔ a zɔ, n nɩ deniyee deem-deem ka ŋ ʊ.

ref: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
text: Busoo bɩ kʊ ŋ n'a sim bɩ, ŋn'ɩ kukur bam hɔsɩrazaa bɩ ma.

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: Ŋ ʊ: ‹Gʊɔɔ nɔɔn duro, bʊr ŋ y'a ʊ jɛ ʊ, ŋ n zi ba wakatɩ deem taan…, ɩbɩɩ n bɔkarɛ, ɩ busoo ka ŋ ʊ hɔdeem kan wɔɔ kɩ, bɩ zi wɔɔ ba, ʊ kɔ dɔgta m tee… woso n wɔ wɔɔ ʊ, am sɛ hɔ bɩ m!›

ref: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
text: Hɔsɩrazaa bɩ n a hɩ ŋ gʊaa deem m, a ʊ: ‹M gʊaa, mɔɔ n tu ta ɩbɩɩ rɛ y, ɩbɩɩ n lɛ sa kan mɔɔ kɩ kʊ m deniyee deem ka ɩ ʊ hindeem zibəə busoo ra?

ref: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
text: Ɩ zibəə busoo si k'ɩ hʊr. Mɔɔ lɛ taa kʊ m busoo ka gʊaa k'a bʊr jɛ ʊ naa ʊ, amba mɔɔ lɛ n taa kʊ m a ka ɩbɩɩ ʊ naa m.

ref: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
text: Mɔɔ ba lɛ m kʊ m m hɔɔnhɔ ba m busoo m na? Gɛɛ nɔntaama n ta ɩbɩɩ m kʊ mɔɔ m mɩŋŋa bɩ minto ra!› »

ref: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
text: Yeezuu n a ya, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, jɛzannɔ yɩm lɛɛkarɛzannɔ, lɛɛkarɛzannɔ n yɩ jɛzannɔ.» Yeezuu a ya, an n'a zɛ kan a mɩmbɔʊrɛ kɩ mim dɩndam (Markɩ

ref: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
text: Yeezuu n jɩm, an ŋ bir, an ŋ lar, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ lɛ n taa kʊ m bɔ ba awɔɔ minto?»

ref: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
text: Yeezuu n ŋ cicir zɛ, an a wɔ da ŋ mɩnyaarɔ wɔɔ ma. Lɛɛm bɩ, gʊɔɔ hɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ ɩ lɛɛ yɩrɛ, ŋn'ɩ zum Yeezuu ʊ.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ nɩ bʊgʊr barɛ ŋ ma, ŋ ʊ, ŋn'a tʊr ka, bɩ ŋ y'a banka ŋn'a da ra: «Karɩnsaamba, Davɩd Nyɩ, wɔɔ cicir zɛ!»

ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Yeezuu a hʊr, an nɩ tam Zerizalɛm ʊ. A n karɩndanyɩnɔ bueerehɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ gaa a hɔ, ŋn'ɩ tarɛ, an n'a hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ:

ref: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
text: Ŋ y'a kam kɩfɩɩrɔ ʊ, brɔɔ n yaa wʊ ma, ŋn'a zɛ madɩr m, ŋn'a wɔ gɔdarkʊra ma, b'a dɔmɩm kaakʊ hinni ʊ, Woso y'a mɩm bɔŋ ʊ gəəno bire ʊ.» Zakkɩ kan Zaan kɩ da hɔ yɛrɛ (Markɩ

ref: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
text: Zebedee nyɩnɔ da a zɔ Yeezuu ma kan a nyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ, an kukurə zɛ a taa, k'a hɔ yɛ han.

ref: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
text: Yeezuu n a lar, a ʊ: «Bɔ ɩ lɛ n taa?» A n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Lɛ ka kʊ mɔɔ nyɩnɔ hɩɩyɔɔ nɔɔm n bɩ nyɩnta ɩbɩɩ cirbəə bɩ nɔ ʊ. Deem ɩ bɩsɩ, deem ɩ jɩsa.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ n hɔ k'a n'a yɛm bɩ dɔ y. Awɔɔ y'a dam ma, a fɩryɩrɛ kʊ mɔɔ n n'a yɩm bɩ yɩ ra?» Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Wɔɔ ʊ da ma.»

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Fɩryɩrɛ kʊ mɔɔ n n'a yɩm bɩ, awɔɔ y'a yɩrɛ. Bɩ, k'a gɔɔta mɔɔ jɩsa kan mɔɔ bɩsɩ bɩ kɩ nyɩntarɛ nɩ, mɔɔ n m da ma, m zaa ka y, bala, mɔɔ n m so ŋ ʊ y. N zɛm bɩ ŋ ka gʊɔɔ kʊ mɔɔ Zɩ bɩncɛrɔ wɔɔ banka ŋ minto rɔ wɔɔ ʊ.»

ref: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ buro kʊ ŋ gɔɔta rɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ naa ma bɩ, ŋ nɔ ma ʊ kan danyɩnɔ hɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
text: Yeezuu n ŋ bir, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ dɔ, a ʊ: gʊɔɔ k'awɔɔ nɩ dɩgarɛ ŋ ma kɩfɩɩrɔ wɔɔ, ŋ cinno ɩ paŋŋa barɛ ŋ ma, paŋŋazannɔ n n'a hɔɔnhɔ bam gʊɔɔ wɔɔ ma sɔ.

ref: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
text: B'a n a ga m, an yɩ ncɩnaaʊ awɔɔ bire ʊ y. Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa lɛ taa k'a yɩ jɩtaarɛ awɔɔ bire ʊ, k'an a ba awɔɔ zibəənyɩ.

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
text: Bɩ, kʊ gʊaa lɛ taa k'a yɩ lɛɛkarɛzaa awɔɔ bire ʊ, k'an a ba awɔɔ lɔ.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: Bala, Gʊaanyɩ bɩ n bʊr kʊ gʊɔɔ n'a hɔɔnhɔ ba y, bʊr a ʊ k'a b'a ba gʊɔɔ zibəənyɩ, k'a ga, kʊ gʊɔɔ gʊta n bʊmbɔrɛ yɩ.» Zeriko bʊʊrɔ hɩɩyɔɔ waarɛ (Markɩ

ref: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
text: Bɔ kʊ ŋ y'a ʊ Zeriko ʊ bɩ, gʊaa cir gʊta zu Yeezuu jɛ ʊ.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Bʊʊrɔ nyɩnta hɩɩya zaa bɩ lezi. Kʊ ŋ y'a ma, ŋ ʊ: Yeezuu n nɩ cenim bɩ, ŋn'a sɩŋŋɩda ba ŋn'ɩ zee dam, ŋ ʊ. «Karɩnsaamba, *Davɩd Nyɩ, wɔɔ cicir zɛ!»

ref: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
text: «Awɔɔ y'a hɔɔn dam mim naa do ma lɔ? Gʊaa ɩ nyɩnta nyɩnɔ m hɩɩya. A n a hɩ lɛɛkarɛzaa bɩ m, a ʊ: ‹M nyɩnnɩ, ta her ɩ yaa zi ba mɔɔ *dɩvɛnsɩra ʊ.›

ref: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
text: Bɩ, zibənno wɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a to ŋnɩ nyɩ bɩ yɩ bɩ, ŋn'a dɩndam kan kʊ kɩ, ŋ ʊ: ‹Naa n nɩ zɛm b'a so a zɩ arzaka b'ʊ. K'a bʊr, k'ʊ a zɛ, k'ʊ ʊ so arzaka b'ʊ!›

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
text: Kʊ gʊaa awɔɔ lar a hʊrlɛ zaa ma, k'a hɩ m a ʊ: Zuuba lɛ n taa. B'a yɩ ŋ tore ŋnɩ bʊr.»

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Ba mim naa do ʊ kʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa bɩ hɔ k'a hɩ naa n kʊ yɩ. A ʊ:

ref: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
text: K'a hɩ *Siyɔn zannɔ m, a ʊ: K'a dɩga a cir k'a nɩ zɛrɛ a zi mɩŋŋazerle m, an da bra ʊ, brapoo m, brada nyɩnnɩ m.

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n ta, ŋnɩ hɔ kʊ Yeezuu a hɩ ŋ nɩ bɩ ba.

ref: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
text: Ŋ n bʊr brada bɩ m kan a nyɩnnɩ bɩ kɩ, ŋn'a huuro ka ŋ la, Yeezuu n da ʊ.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Yeezuu k'a gasʊ Zerizalɛm ʊ bɩ, kʊ bɩ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ haay ŋn'ɩ bɩrlɛ a mim ma, ŋn'ɩ laaka dam, ŋ ʊ: «Gʊaa naa do nka nɩ?»

ref: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
text: Wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ kan wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ heer n yɩ nyɩnta kan Yeezuu kɩ y, kʊ ŋ y'a yɩ an nɩ zi gʊta barɛ, kan nyɩnɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ zee dam Wosocɛ ʊ b'ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Hozaana, Davɩd Nyɩ!»

ref: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
text: Wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kɩ kʊ ŋ Yeezuu hɔbɔkʊrɛnoonno nɔɔn duro ma bɩ, ŋ y'a dɔ ŋ ʊ, a hɩ a nawʊrɛ a ma.

ref: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
text: A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «N n a gʊrsɩra ba Wosoci ʊ, n ʊ: ‹N mɔɔ cɛ bɩ birm yaadarɛcɛ› ra? Awɔɔ n bɔkarɛ a sa, a ba konno nyaakʊndajɩ!»

ref: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
text: K'awɔɔ sɩra ka, hɔ haay k'awɔɔ n n'a yɛm yaadarɛ nɔ ʊ haay bɩ, Woso yɩ ŋ karɛ awɔɔ ʊ.» Yeezuu paŋŋa bɩ, a to a ʊ ka? (Markɩ

ref: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
text: Ŋ n a hɩ Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ ɩ hɔ kʊ ŋ n'a hɩm bɩ mam na?» Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Ɩɩn…, awɔɔ n a karɩnda ba fɩɩga Wosoci b'ʊ, n ʊ: ‹Ɩbɩɩ ka nyɩnɔ kan banfinnirɔ kɩ n n'a lɛ bɔrɛ ɩbɩɩ ʊ› ra?»

ref: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
text: A m bɩ, Yeezuu n ŋ to b'ʊ, an a hʊr an bɔ kʊnɔ ʊ b'ʊ, an ta Betanii ʊ dɔ n mɛ m b'ʊ. Yeezuu lɛ kʊsɩ da sɔɔn ʊ (Markɩ

ref: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Kʊ m awɔɔ lar mim deem ma, deem bala, k'awɔɔ nyɩ bɔ ʊ, kʊ m bɔkarɛ, m lɛ kʊ mɔɔ n ta m bɩ jɩ hɩ awɔɔ m.

ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Yeezuu k'a wusigə dɔgta mɩŋŋa m, an nɩ tam kʊnɔ ʊ b'ʊ bɩ, nɔ a nyasʊ.

ref: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: k'awɔɔ sɩra ka, bɩ bɩsɩkarɛ b'awɔɔ heer ʊ, awɔɔ mɔɔ zi kʊ mɔɔ ba sɔɔn naa do m bɩ bam. B'awɔɔ a da ma sɔ, a hɩ ci naa do m, a ʊ, ɩ bɔɔta naa ʊ, ɩ ɩ da higʊta ʊ. A bam maam.

ref: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
text: A m naa, ŋn'a nyɩ bɔ Yeezuu nʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Wɔɔ n a dɔ y.» Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ bɩ lɛ kʊ mɔɔ n ta m, m nɩ naa bam bɩ jɩ hɩrɛ awɔɔ m sɔ y.» Gʊaa nyɩnɔ hɩɩyɔɔ hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom

ref: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
text: «K'a tʊr ka hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom vanta ma: Gʊaa ɩ nyɩnta, an dɩvɛn gɔ sɔɔn, an lambaar da ma, an yaa hɔn, k'a bɩr dɩvɛn bɩ hi bɔŋ ʊ, an bɛŋŋʊ dɔ sɔ k'a tɩr dɔ hɔsɩra bɩ ma. A n a sa, an a da gʊɔɔ wɔ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n'a zi ba, k'a b'a nyɔɔdʊ bɩ jir. A n a hʊr, an ta kʊ ʊ.

ref: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
text: Yeezuu k'a gasʊ Wosocɛ ʊ b'ʊ bɩ, a a sɩŋŋɩda ba, an nɩ hɔ dɩndam. Wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ kan gʊɔɔgusinno kɩ a zɔ Yeezuu ma, ŋn'a lar, ŋ ʊ: «Lɛ kara ɩbɩɩ n ta m k'ɩ naa ba? Nka ɩ lɛ bɩ do ka ɩ b'ʊ?»

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: Batɛm kʊ Zaan n n'a kam bɩ, a to a ʊ Woso zi gɛɛ, gʊɔɔ zi?» Ŋ nɩ bɩsɩ kam kan kʊ kɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Kʊ wɔɔ ʊ a to a ʊ Woso zi, a y'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: Bɔ minto m, wɔɔ n sɩra ka ʊ?

ref: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
text: Bɩ kʊ wɔɔ ʊ gʊɔɔ zi, wɔɔ ɩ nyi bam zamaa bɩ kʊ ŋ Zaan dɔ wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa paan… bɩ, ŋ bɩ yɛm kan ʊ kɩ y.»

ref: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
text: Nyɩ bɩ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: ‹Mɔɔ bɩ tarɛ y.› Maasɩrɛ k'a ba bɩ, a a hɔɔndarɛ lʊr an ta hɔsɩra b'ʊ.

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: Gʊaa bɩ n a ya, an a hɩ nyɩ vanta bɩ m maam sɔ. Nyɩ bɩ do ʊ: ‹Ɩɩn…, baaba,› b'a n ta y.

ref: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
text: Nyɩnɔ hɩɩyɔɔ nɔɔn duro bire ʊ bɩ, nka y'a zɩ bɩ hɔɔnhɔ ba?» Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Lɛɛkarɛzaa bɩ m». Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, yawʊrsinno kan lannɔ kʊ ŋ n'a mɩŋŋa hɛɛnm nɔ kɩ lɛɛkarɛ awɔɔ m Woso cirbəə bɩ nɔ ʊ.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Bala, Zaan-Batiisi bʊr awɔɔ zi, an zaa mɩŋŋa hɩnka awɔɔ m, b'awɔɔ n a mim si y. Bɩ, yawʊrsinno kan lannɔ kʊ ŋ n'a mɩŋŋa hɛɛnm nɔ kɩ a mim si. Awɔɔ naa yɩ, b'awɔɔ gɔɔta awɔɔ n a bɔ a taam k'a mim si y.» Wutu dɔmazannɔ bʊnyaarɔ hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom (Markɩ

ref: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
text: Dɩvɛn mɔsa k'a ku bɩ, an a zibəənyɩnɔ nyɔɔ hɔsɩra bɩ zibənno wɔɔ zi, kʊ ŋ n'aa a hɔ si, ŋnɩ b'a ka a ʊ.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: Zibəənyɩnɔ wɔɔ n ŋ nyasʊ, ŋnɩ deem dundu, ŋnɩ deem zɛ gər, ŋnɩ deem bɩmba jaarɔ m, ŋn'a zɛ, an ga.

ref: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
text: Hɔsɩrazaa bɩ n a ya, an zibəənyɩnɔ nyɔɔ gʊta an lɛ da biisi bɩ m. Bɩ, zibənno wɔɔ a ya ŋnɩ hɔ kʊ ŋ y'a ba lɛɛkarɛzannɔ wɔɔ m bɩ ba nawɔnnɔ m sɔ.

ref: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
text: A nyarɛ ma bɩ, a nyɩyar a nyɔɔ, an n'a hɔɔn darɛ, a ʊ, kʊ mɔɔ nyɩ m naa, ŋ y'a daŋ ʊ.

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Ŋ n a nyasʊ, ŋnɩ bɔ m hɔsɩra b'ʊ, ŋn'a zɛ gər.»

ref: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
text: Yeezuu n laaka da ŋ han, a ʊ: «Kʊ dɩvɛnsɩrazaa bɩ bʊr, bɔ an n'a bam zibənno wɔɔ nɩ?»

ref: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
text: Ŋ n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «A gʊɔɔ bʊnyaarɔ nɔɔn duro zɩnzɛm, a bɩ ŋ cicir zɛrɛ y, an hɔsɩra bɩ ka gʊɔɔ vantɔɔ ʊ ŋn'a zi ba, ŋn'a hɔ ka ʊ a dɔmɩm ʊ.»

ref: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ n hɔ kʊ Wosoci b'a hɩ bɩ karɩnda ba ra? A a hɩ, a ʊ: Jaa kʊ cɛdonno wɔɔ a zo bɩ y'a ba cɛ bɩ jɩr jaa paŋŋazaa. Hɔ kʊ Zuuba a ba bɩ n naa do, wɔɔ nɩ dɩgam ma hɔ mɩŋŋa gʊta.

ref: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
text: Bɩ y'a ka, mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, mɔɔ ʊ, n Woso cirbəə bɩ yɔrɛ awɔɔ m, n n a ka do vanta ʊ an cirbəə bɩ zibəə k'a a ga m bɩ ba. [

ref: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
text: Kʊ gʊaa a do jaa naa do ra, a hɩsɩga dam, bɩ kʊ jaa b'a da gʊaa ra a gʊaa bɩ do hɔɔnsɩrɛ.]»

ref: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
text: Ŋ n'a kam ma k'a nyasʊ, bɩ, ŋ yɩ nyi bam zamaa bɩ m, bala, zamaa b'ɩ dɩgam Yeezuu ma wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Naa jɛ ʊ bɩ, farizɛɛnrɔ ta, ŋn'aa a so kʊ ma, k'a a ka zaa k'a nɩ Yeezuu nyasʊm ma a meerbaa nɔ ʊ bɩ dɔ.

ref: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
text: «Karɩnsaamba, Moyiisi a gʊrsɩra ba an a ka wɔɔ ʊ, a ʊ, kʊ gʊaa cɛmba ta ʊ, an ga an a lʊ to, b'a nyɩ ba ʊ y, k'a dam n lʊ bɩ si, k'an nyɩ yɩ kan kɩ a cɛmba bɩ minto.

ref: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
text: Yeezuu a ya, an hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom da ŋ nɩ, a ʊ:

ref: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
text: «Woso cirbəə bɔ kʊ rɛ kan cir k'a a nyɩnnɩ lʊsarɛ cibsa ba kɩ.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: A n a zibəənyɩnɔ nyɔɔ kʊ ŋ n'aa gʊɔɔ k'a hɩ ŋ nɩ kʊ ŋ nɩ bɩ cibsa bɩ rɔ wɔɔ bir, ŋnɩ bɔkarɛ ŋ ʊ, a bɩ zɛrɛ y.

ref: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
text: A n a ya, an zibəənyɩnɔ vantɔɔ nyɔɔ an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: ‹K'a yaa hɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ mɔɔ ŋ bir cibsa bɩ ma rɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: mɔɔ cibsa bɩ banka m a nya, mɔɔ m dono zɩnzɛ kan m hɔbɩsɩrɔ gʊtɔɔ kɩ, hɔ haay banka, k'a bʊr cibsa bɩ ma kɛɛrɛ.›

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: Bɩ, gʊɔɔ wɔɔ n dɩga cibsa birle bɩ ma hɔsɩ y, ŋn'a hʊr ŋnɩ ta a zibəəjɩrɔ ʊ, naa n ta hɔsɩra ʊ, naa n ta daasɩ ʊ mɔdrɔ hɛɛjɩ ʊ.

ref: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
text: Ŋ n gɔɔta rɔ wɔɔ n zibəənyɩnɔ wɔɔ nyasʊ, ŋnɩ nyannɩ da ŋ ma, ŋnɩ bɔkarɛ, ŋnɩ ŋ zɩnzɛ.

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: Cir bɩ nɔ n ma ʊ, an sʊrdaarɔ nyɔɔ ŋn'aa gərzenno nɔɔn duro zɩnzɛ, ŋnɩ sɛ da ŋ kʊ bɩ ma.

ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: A m naa, an a hɩ a zibəənyɩnɔ m, a ʊ: ‹Cibsa bɩ hɔbɩrɛ bɩ bankarɛ nya, bɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ mɔɔ hɩ ŋ nɩ rɔ wɔɔ n a ga kʊ ʊ ba y.

ref: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
text: K'a ta zaazɛkʊrarɔ wɔɔ ra, kʊ nka awɔɔ yɩ, k'a hɩ m k'an bʊr cibsa bɩ ma.›

ref: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
text: Cir bɩ gasʊ k'a dɩga gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ bʊr cibsa bɩ ma rɔ wɔɔ ma, an a to, an gʊaa yɩ, gʊaa bɩ do n harcɩntarɛ cibsa huu du a ma y.

ref: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
text: Bɩ minto bɩ, ɩ hɔɔnhɔ hɩ wɔɔ m, wɔɔ lɛ bɩ zaa ka k'ʊ Rɔm cir yawʊr ka ʊ gɛɛ, ʊ b'a ka ʊ gɛ?»

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Ŋ n a karɩndanyɩnɔ kan Hɩrɔɔdɩ jɛzannɔ kɩ nyɔɔ Yeezuu zi, kʊ ŋ n'aa hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ dɔ wɔɔ ʊ sɩra ɩbɩɩ n'a hɩm, Woso zaa ɩbɩɩ n'a dɩndam sɩra bɩncɛ ʊ, ɩbɩɩ bɩr nyi bam gʊɔɔ nɩ y, b'ɩbɩɩ bɩr gʊɔɔ nyʊkɔŋ ʊ sɔ y.

ref: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
text: K'a busoo kʊ n nɩ Rɔm cir yawʊr kam nɩ bɩ hɩnka m m.» Ŋ n deniyee deem hɩnka m.

ref: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
text: Ŋ ʊ: «Rɔm cir hɔɔ m.» A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a maam k'a hɔ kʊ Rɔm cir hɔ m bɩ ka ʊ, k'a hɔ kʊ Woso hɔ m bɩ ka ʊ sɔ.»

ref: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
text: Meerbaa naa do a ka ŋ n zɛ kədə, ŋn'a hʊr, ŋnɩ Yeezuu to. Gəəno mɩmbɔʊrɛ mim (Markɩ

ref: At last the woman died.
text: Ŋ haay kur bɩ, lʊ bɩ n ga sɔ.

ref: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
text: Moyiisi lɛrɔ wɔɔ haay kan wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ meerbaarɔ kɩ haay məsi m lɛrɔ hɩɩyɔɔ nɔɔn duro m.» Krista bɩ, nka nyɩ nɩ? (Markɩ

ref: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
text: Hinni bɩ do ʊ bɩ, sadisɛɛnrɔ gɔsɩnnɔ bʊr Yeezuu zi. Bala, sadisɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ ʊ gəəno mɩmbɔʊrɛ b'a ʊ y. Ŋ n laaka da Yeezuu han, ŋ ʊ:

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «K'awɔɔ n gʊrsɩra mɩŋŋɔɔ wɔɔ dɔ kan Woso paŋŋa kɩ bɩ y'a ka awɔɔ n'a nyʊnnyɔɔm.

ref: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
text: Bala, gəəno mɩmbɔʊrɛ dɔmɩm ʊ, yar bɩr lʊ sarɛ y, lʊ bɩr yar sarɛ y, bɔ ŋ y'a ʊ kʊ ʊ kan malɛɛkarɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ brama rɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
text: Bɩ, kʊ gəəno mɩmbɔʊrɛ mim nɩ, awɔɔ n hɔ kʊ Woso a hɩ awɔɔ m bɩ karɩnda ba ra? A a hɩ, a ʊ:

ref: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
text: ‹Abraham kan Izakkɩ kɩ, Zakɔɔb kɩ Woso m mɔɔ m.› Gəəno Woso bɛɛ y, gʊɔɔ mɩsɩɩrɔ Woso m.»

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Yeezuu hɔdɩndarɛ naa do cem zamaa bɩ hɔɔndarɛ han. Lɛ k'a lɛ da lɛrɔ wɔɔ m haay (Markɩ

ref: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a ma, ŋ ʊ, Yeezuu a ka sadisɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ b'a dam ma ŋn'a ya ŋnɩ meer ba bɩ, ŋ y'a so kʊ ma.

ref: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
text: A ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, lɛrɔ wɔɔ haay bire ʊ, kara ɩ lɛ da?»

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Lɛ k'a lɛ da, a m lɛ gʊta bɩ m naa do.

ref: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Lɛ hɩɩya hɔ k'a gʊtabaa lɛɛkarɛ hɔ bɩ ku bɩ m naa do: ‹K'ɩ mim ŋʊa amba ɩ mɩŋŋa m.›

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a so kʊ ma bɩ, Yeezuu laaka da ŋ han, a ʊ:

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: Zuuba Woso a hɩ mɔɔ zuuba m, a ʊ: nyɩnta mɔɔ bɩsɩ ʊ, kʊ m tolle ba m a ka, k'ɩ jɩnnɔ wɔɔ n nyɩnta ɩ gam taa.

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: «Awɔɔ y'a hɔɔn dam Krista ma lʊnlɔ? Nka nyɩ nɩ?» Ŋ n a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «*Davɩd Nyɩ m.»

ref: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «B'a yɩ lɔ, Sɛnt-Ɛspri n a ka Davɩd n a bir a Zuuba? Bala, Davɩd b'a hɩ, a ʊ:

ref: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
text: K'a ɩ yɩrɛ ɩ ʊ Davɩd y'a birm a Zuuba, yɩ an nawʊm lɔ kɛɛrɛ kʊ Krista n yɩ *Davɩd Nyɩ?»

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Gɔsɩ n a da ma, an a nyɩ bɔ nʊ y. Hinni bɩ do tɛkka ma, gʊaa ba ʊ dɔ, a heer n kookoo k'a lar mim ma y.

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Bɩ kɛɛrɛ naa, ‹N y'a bɔrɛ awɔɔ cɛ b'ʊ.›

ref: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
text: «Zerizalɛm, Zerizalɛm, ɩbɩɩ k'ɩ nɩ wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ zɩnzɛm, ɩ nɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ Woso ŋ nyɔɔ ɩ ma rɔ wɔɔ bɩmbam jaarɔ m bɩ! Mɔɔ gallɛm karɔ m a ŋʊa kʊ m ɩ ɩ zamaa so kʊ ma m taa amba kʊrda tɩr a nyɩnɔ so kʊ ma a zarɔ taa bɩ m, b'awɔɔ n yɛ!

ref: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
text: «Wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kɩ, Moyiisi lɛrɔ wɔɔ ŋn'a hɩnkam gʊɔɔ m.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Bɩ minto bɩ, awɔɔ a ga m, a sɩra ka ŋ ʊ, a hɔ kʊ ŋ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m haay bɩ ba, b'a bɩ zu ŋ zibəə ʊ y. Bala, ŋ bɩr hɔ kʊ ŋ n'a dɩndam gʊɔɔ m bɩ barɛ y.

ref: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
text: Ŋ tɩr mɔr gorezaa kʊsɩ ŋn'a ka gʊaa mee, bɩ ŋ bɩr a wɔ dam ma k'a a da a zaa han, an a busu y.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: Ziro kʊ ŋ nɩ ŋ bam haay rɔ wɔɔ ba ŋ nawʊm kʊ gʊɔɔ m dɩga a ma, bɩ y'a ka, Wosoci lemim kʊ ŋ tɩr a kʊsɩ a tusə ma gɛɛ a za ma bɩ ba ŋ tɩr a ʊ jintə, ŋ tɩr pɛɛra lensəzannɔ dɔ a huuro rɛ, k'an bɩr yɩm.

ref: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
text: Lɛɛ mɩŋŋɔɔ ŋ tɩr a ka ma cibsarɔ bɩncɛ ʊ, sokʊma bɩncɛ ʊ, lɛɛ wɩsɩ ŋ tɩr a kam ma.

ref: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
text: A ŋʊa ŋ tɩr a ʊ kʊ ŋ nɩ bɩr yaa darɛ a m mɩŋŋa gʊɔɔ gaa ʊ, kʊ gʊɔɔ n bɩr a birm karɩnsaamba.

ref: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
text: B'awɔɔ, a bɩ yɛ kʊ n n'a bir karɩnsaamba y, bala, danyɩnɔ n'awɔɔ m haay, awɔɔ karɩnsaamba bɩ deem.

ref: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
text: A bɩ gɔsɩ bir tara burə ʊ a ʊ, a Zɩ y, bala, awɔɔ Zɩ bɩ deem, an nɩ arzana ʊ bɩ.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: A b'a ka ma kʊ n n'a bir jɩtaarɛ y. Bala, awɔɔ jɩtaarɛ bɩ deem, Krista m.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ awɔɔ wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, awɔɔ ɩ zonno hɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ ta m bɩ yɔrɛ ŋ nɩ, a a ya, a gaa, an nɩ yaa jintoo dam kʊ n n'a hɩ a ma gʊɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ. Naa minto m, awɔɔ hʊnhʊrlɛ n nɩ yɩm maam-maam.]

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, awɔɔ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, awɔɔ ɩ tam kʊrɔ ʊ tara burə ʊ kan kɔɔlʊʊ nɔ ʊ kɩ an n'a kam gʊaa deem taan… ma k'an zu awɔɔ lɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ, bɩ k'awɔɔ ku b'awɔɔ ka an yɩ sɛhar gʊaa gallɛm hɩɩya an lɛ da awɔɔ m.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, awɔɔ k'an bʊʊrɔ, an nɩ zaa hɩnkam gʊɔɔ m, awɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: ‹Kʊ gʊaa ler bɔ Wosocɛ bɩ tɔ ma hɔsɩ bɛɛ nɩ y, bɩ k'a ler bɔ Wosocɛ bɩ sarma bɩ tɔ ma pakra m k'an hɔ k'a hɩ bɩ ba.›

ref: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
text: Hɔɔnwarzannɔ, bʊʊrɔ n'awɔɔ m! Kara ɩ lɛ da a mim nɩ? Sarma bɩ m gɛɛ, Wosocɛ k'a ka sarma bɩ nɩ yɩm Woso hɔ bɩ nɩ?

ref: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
text: Awɔɔ a ya, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: ‹Kʊ gʊaa ler bɔ mannɩduu ma hɔsɩ bɛɛ y, bɩ k'a ler bɔ mannɩhɔ k'a n'a burə ʊ bɩ ma, pakra m k'an hɔ k'a hɩ bɩ ba.›

ref: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
text: Bʊʊrɔ n'awɔɔ m! Kara ɩ lɛ da? Mannɩhɔ bɩ n gɛɛ, mannɩduu k'a nɩ mannɩhɔ bɩ kam Woso ʊ bɩ nɩ?

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: Gʊaa k'a nɩ ler bɔm Wosocɛ bɩ ma bɩ, ler bɔ a nawʊm Wosocɛ bɩ ma, kan Woso k'a nɩ cɛ b'ʊ bɩ kɩ.

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Gʊaa k'a nɩ ler bɔm arzana ma, ler bɔ a nawʊm Woso duu bɩ ma kan Woso mɩŋŋa k'a nyɩnta ra bɩ kɩ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, awɔɔ ɩ dʊlɛɛrɔ bu hɔ ganwʊrɛ bɔŋ ʊ an n'a kam Woso ʊ, a bɔkarɛ, a hɔ k'an mɩŋŋa Woso lɛrɔ wɔɔ nɔ ʊ bɩ to, amba: Tɩrgabaa, mɩŋŋabaa, kan sɩrabarɛ kɩ, nawɔnnɔ awɔɔ a ga m, a ŋ ba, a ŋ gɔɔtarɔ wɔɔ ba sɔ.

ref: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
text: Awɔɔ k'an bʊʊrɔ, an nɩ zaa hɩnkam bɩ, awɔɔ y'a hɔ mire sʊrm k'a mɛɛm bɔ ʊ, b'awɔɔ ɩ yoonde monɩm.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, awɔɔ ɩ hɔmihɔ kan hɔbɩrɛ mɔr burə kɩ sʊrgɔm, k'a dintim gʊ ŋ ma. Bɩ, ŋ nɔ rɔ wɔɔ, awɔɔ koorbaa zibəə kan a hɔɔndarɛ bʊnyaarɔ kɩ yɩ ŋ han.

ref: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
text: Farizɛn, bʊʊ n'ɩbɩɩ m, lɛɛ ka ɩ hɔmihɔ bɩ nɔ sʊrgɔ, a burə bɩ hu bɔm sɔ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, bɔ awɔɔ ʊ kʊ rɛ kan gəənoyaarɔ huro kɩ, ŋ burə gʊɛrɛ n ta ʊ, an bɔkarɛ, ŋ nɔ ʊ b'ʊ bɩ, yaarɔ kan hɔɔ vənno do haay kɩ yɩ ŋ han.

ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Awɔɔ do m bɩ, gʊɔɔ ɩ dɩgarɛ a ma gʊɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ, an bɔkarɛ zambʊ kan bʊnyaabaa kɩ y'awɔɔ heer han.» (Likki

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
text: «Kʊsɩ ɩ kan awɔɔ kɩ, wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ zambʊzannɔ kɩ, gəənoyaarɔ awɔɔ nɩ ŋ bankam mɩŋŋa wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ minto, gʊɔɔ tɩrgɔɔ wɔɔ awɔɔ nɩ ŋ yaarɔ gʊɛrɛ dam ma.

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: A bɔkarɛ, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: ‹Kʊ wɔɔ ɩ nyɩnta ʊ yaabɔɔ dɔmɩm ʊ, wɔɔ b'ʊ zɛm kʊ ra kan ŋ kɩ, ʊ wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ y.›

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Naa minto bɩ, awɔɔ mɩŋŋɔɔ nɩ kasɛtɩ karɛ a ʊ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ wosolɛsinnɩsorazannɔ wɔɔ zɩnzɛ rɔ wɔɔ nyɩnɔ n'awɔɔ m.

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: Mɩnnɔ, cɩrkarɔ do n'awɔɔ m! Awɔɔ y'a hɔɔn dam lɔ, k'a a da ma, a a si Woso k'awɔɔ dʊdɔ yar, an awɔɔ da sɛhar ʊ bɩ lɛɛ?

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, hʊnhʊrlɛ k'a a ga m gʊɔɔ zɩnzɛrɛ naa do minto bɩ, her zamaana naa do n nɩ hʊnhʊrlɛ bɩ do yɩm.» Yeezuu heerzarlɛ Zerizalɛm minto (Likki

ref: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: an a sɩŋŋɩda sa naa ma, awɔɔ b'a yarɛ a mɔɔ yɩ y, k'awɔɔ m b'a hɩ, a ʊ: ‹Barka ɩ gʊaa k'a nɩ zɛm Zuuba tɔ ma bɩ mim ʊ!› »

ref: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
text: Yeezuu k'a bɔ Wosocɛ b'ʊ k'a a hʊr bɩ, a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ a zɔ ma ŋn'ɩ Wosocɛ bɩ dɔrɛ mim hɩm nɩ.

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: A n a nyɩ bɔ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «Naa k'awɔɔ n'a yɩm haay naa, sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: jaa deem bɩ gɔɔtarɛ a mim burə ʊ y. Ŋ haay zɛm bɩ wurgə.» (Markɩ

ref: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
text: Bala, gʊɔɔ zɛm gʊta, ŋnɩ meer ba mɔɔ tɔ m, ŋ ʊ: ‹Mɔɔ m Krista,› bɩ ŋ gʊɔɔ nyʊnnyɔɔm gʊta.

ref: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
text: Awɔɔ yarbarɛ mim mam, kan yarbarɛ k'a n'a dam ma an bɩ wuti bɩ mim kɩ, a bɩ dabɔʊ ba y. Bala, pakra m, kʊ naa haay n ba, bɩ durnya bɩ nyarɛ bɛɛ m cɩna y.

ref: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
text: Yeezuu k'a nɩ nyɩntam Oliviyee ci mim ʊ bɩ, a karɩndanyɩnɔ a zɔ ma a minto, ŋn'a lar, ŋ ʊ: «A hɩ wɔɔ m, ba naa do nawʊm brɔ? Makra kara n n'ɩbɩɩ zɛrɛ hɩnkarɛ kan durnya nyarɛ kɩ?»

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Do deem gʊɔɔ wutire ŋnɩ yar ba kan do vanta kɩ, tara deem zannɔ wutire ŋnɩ yar ba kan tara vanta zannɔ kɩ, nɔ nyɩntam kʊrɔ ʊ, bɩncɛrɔ niŋŋoo nyiŋŋəm.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Dɔmɩnnɔ wɔɔ duro ʊ, a yɩm kʊsɩ lannɔ jɩsɩzannɔ wɔɔ ma, kan lannɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ nyɔ karɛ a nyɩnɔ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Naa do haay bɩ, bɔ a ʊ kʊ rɛ kan lʊ nyɩ yɩrɛ fɩryɩrɛ sɩŋŋɩda kɩ.

ref: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
text: Bɩ, n y'awɔɔ gʊrm, ŋnɩ fɩr ka awɔɔ ma, ŋn'a zɩnzɛ. Doro haay sɔrɛ awɔɔ m mɔɔ minto.

ref: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
text: Dɔmɩnnɔ wɔɔ duro ʊ gʊɔɔ gʊta sɩrakarɛ tom, ŋnɩ ŋʊaar ba kʊ m, ŋnɩ sɔ kʊ m.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ŋʊaarlɛsinnɩsorazannɔ zɛm gʊta ŋnɩ gʊɔɔ gʊta nyʊnnyɔɔ.

ref: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
text: Kʊ gʊaa a hɩ awɔɔ m, a ʊ: Krista ɩ naa ʊ, gɛɛ, a ɩ bra ʊ, a b'a si y.

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: K'a yaa da Woso m k'awɔɔ sire bɩ bɩ nyɩnta la barɛ dɔmɩnnɔ ʊ gɛɛ, *sabaa hinni ʊ y.

ref: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
text: «K'awɔɔ bɩ ‹Gʊaa kʊ hɔzaarbarɛzaa bʊnyaa m bɩ yɩ›, wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa Danɩyɛl n a hɩ ma bɩ, an nɩ nyɩntam lɛɛ mɩŋŋa kʊ Woso bɩncɛ m, b'a n a ga nɩ y, gʊaa k'a nɩ karɩnda bam bɩ, k'a n'a jɩ ma mɩŋŋa.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ Zidee ʊ bɩ, kʊ ŋ n'a si, ŋnɩ ta ciro ʊ.

ref: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
text: Gʊaa k'a n'a hɔsɩra ʊ sɔ bɩ, a bɩ doo k'a yaa a huu sa y.

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Kʊ Woso bɛɛ dɔmɩnnɔ nɔɔn duro bɔrɛ, gɔsɩ bɩ larɛ y. Bɩ, a ŋ bɔrɛ a gʊɔɔ k'a ŋ nyʊkɔ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ minto.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: A maam sɔ, k'awɔɔ bɩ minno nɔɔn duro yɩ, k'a dɔ a ʊ Gʊaanyɩ bɩ zɛrɛ a zɔ, bɩ, yɩ an nawʊm maam sɔ.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Hɔ k'a ba Nowee dɔmɩm ʊ bɩ nyɩntaŋ ʊ sɔ Gʊaanyɩ bɩ zɛrɛ dɔmɩm ʊ.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: «A n ta ʊ bɩ, gɔsɩ n dɔmɩm bɩ do kan a wakatɩ kɩ dɔ y. Baa, malɛɛkarɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ brama rɔ wɔɔ n a dɔ y, Nyɩ bɩ n a dɔ sɔ y. Zɩ bɩ y'a dɔ a deem.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Ŋ n a zu a ʊ, ŋ n a dɔ ŋ ʊ hɔsɩ ɩ barɛ y, hi bɩ n tolle ba an bɩ cem ŋ nɩ haay. A yɩrɛ ncɩnaaʊ Gʊaanyɩ bɩ zɛrɛ dɔmɩm ʊ.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Kʊ gʊaa hɩɩya ɩ hɔsɩra ʊ: n deem gaam, n nɩ deem to.

ref: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
text: Naa minto bɩ, a b'a zu a ʊ y, bala, awɔɔ n dɔmɩm k'awɔɔ Zuuba n nɩ zɛŋ ʊ bɩ dɔ y.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: K'a mim naa do jɩ ma mɩŋŋa. Kʊ harzaa b'a dɔ a ʊ, koor bɩ zɛm gunuu wakatɩ naa do ma, a bɩ hinceem barɛ y, a bɩ koor bɩ tore an gasʊ a cɛ b'ʊ y.

ref: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
text: Bɩ y'a ka, awɔɔ sɔ k'a a banka, bala, Gʊaanyɩ bɩ zɛm wakatɩ k'awɔɔ bɩr a hɔɔn dam ma b'ʊ y.» Zibəənyɩ mɩŋŋa mim (Likki

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Bɩ, kʊ zibəənyɩ bʊnyaa m, a y'a hɩrɛ a heer ʊ, a ʊ, mɔɔ Zuuba maasɩrɛ ba a bɩr zɛrɛ y.

ref: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.

ref: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.

text: B'a Zuuba bɩ zɛm dɔmɩm kʊ zibəənyɩ bɩ bɩr dɔm ma b'ʊ, kan wakatɩ k'a n a dɔ bɩ kɩ ʊ.

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.

ref: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
text: Ŋ hɔɔn ba ʊ rɔ wɔɔ n a fɩtɩrɛrɔ gʊr, bɩ ŋ n nyɔɔ tɔ mɔr ʊ, ŋn'a sa y.

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: Bɩ, ŋ hɔɔn n ta ʊ rɔ wɔɔ, brɔɔ nyɔɔ tɔ mɔdrɔ ʊ ŋn'a na a fɩtɩrɛrɔ wɔɔ ma.

ref: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
text: Gunuu wakatɩ bueerehɩɩya k'a b'a ku bɩ, zee n bɔ, a ʊ: ‹Zim bɩ ba n nɩ! K'a bɔ a yaa a zɛ ʊ!›

ref: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
text: A m naa, nyɩmbɔnnɔ bu rɔ wɔɔ n gu, ŋn'a fɩtɩrɛrɔ bankarɛ.

ref: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
text: Ŋ hɔɔn ba ʊ rɔ wɔɔ n'a hɩm, ŋ ʊ: ‹K'a nyɔɔ bɩ ka wɔɔ ʊ poore, bala, wɔɔ fɩtɩrɛrɔ wɔɔ ɩ lihim.›

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Yeezuu n a ya, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «A bɩ hinceem ba y, bala, awɔɔ n dɔmɩm bɩ kan wakatɩ bɩ kɩ dɔ y.» Zibəənyɩnɔ kaakʊrɔ hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom (Likki

ref: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
text: A dɔ k'ɩbɩɩ ʊ maam bɩ, mɔɔ busoo bɩ cɩnta ɩbɩɩ nawʊm busoocɩntarɛcɛ ʊ, mɔɔ wusigəre ma, mɔɔ yɩ m busoo bɩ sam kan nyɔɔdʊ kɩ.

ref: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
text: «Woso cirbəə b'a ya, an bɔ kʊ rɛ kan gʊaa k'a n'a ŋʊam k'a ta kʊ ʊ, an a zibəənyɩnɔ bir, an a arzaka ka ŋ ʊ kʊ ŋ nɩ dɔ ma bɩ kɩ.

ref: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
text: Gʊaa k'a talan soor bɩ yɩ bɩ n ta an ter ba m, an talan soor vanta yɩ an a na ma.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: A zuuba bɩ n a hɩ m, a ʊ: ‹Zibəənyɩ mɩŋŋa k'an tɩrga n'ɩbɩɩ m, a mɩŋŋa, ɩbɩɩ sɩra zi ba hɔ poore nɔ ʊ, mɔɔ hɔ gʊta zibəə karɛ ɩ ʊ, bʊr ɩ heernyɔɔ ba kan mɔɔ kɩ.›

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: A zuuba bɩ n a hɩ m, a ʊ: ‹Zibəənyɩ mɩŋŋa k'an tɩrga n'ɩbɩɩ m, ɩbɩɩ sɩra zi ba hɔ poore nɔ ʊ, mɔɔ hɔ gʊta zibəə karɛ ɩ ʊ, bʊr ɩ heernyɔɔ ba kan mɔɔ kɩ.›

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Nyibəə mɔɔ nyasʊ, mɔɔ n ta m aa ɩ busoo bɩ biitə tara ʊ, dɩga, ɩ busoo bɩ ba n nɩ.›

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: A zuuba bɩ n a nyɩ bɔ nʊ, a ʊ: ‹Zibəənyɩ bʊnyaa yakʊzaa ra! Ɩbɩɩ dɔ mɩŋŋa ɩ ʊ, mɔɔ ɩ hɔ dam lɛɛ kʊ mɔɔ n hɔ duu ʊ, m nɩ hɔ sam lɛɛ kʊ mɔɔ n hɔ sɔɔn ʊ.

ref: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
text: Naa minto bɩ, k'a busoo talan deem k'an ta m bɩ yɔm, a ka talan bu zaa b'ʊ.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Bala, gʊaa k'an ta hɔ m bɩ, n y'a kam bɩ zaa ʊ, an a na ma, a hɔ m yɩ gʊta. Bɩ gʊaa k'a bam bɩ, baa hɔ k'an ta m bɩ, n bɩ yɔm nɩ.

ref: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
text: Bɩ, k'a zibəənyɩ hɔ vam naa do zo garga ra monsigə ʊ. Lɛɛ bɩ do an nɩ baa daŋ ʊ, an a sɔ sʊ.› » Durnya gʊɔɔ haay dʊdɔkarɛ

ref: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
text: «Kʊ Gʊaanyɩ bʊr a cirbəə nɔ ʊ kan malɛɛkarɔ wɔɔ kɩ haay, a nyɩntam a cirbəəduu paŋŋazaa bɩ ra.

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Durnya kʊrɔ haay gʊɔɔ y'a som kʊ ma a lɛɛ, an gʊɔɔ gʊngʊ a hɔ, amba hɔbɩsɩrɔ dundor tɩr seero gʊ biiro ma bɩ m.

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text: A seero wɔɔ nyɔɔrɛ a bɩsɩ, an biiro wɔɔ nyɔɔ a jɩsa.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: Cir bɩ y'a hɩrɛ a bɩsɩ ra zannɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: ‹Awɔɔ kʊ mɔɔ Zɩ barka n nɩ kan a kɩ bɩ, k'a bʊr, a cirbəə kʊ Woso a banka awɔɔ minto durnya darɛ sɩŋŋɩda ʊ bɩ si.

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Bala, nɔ mɔɔ zɛ, awɔɔ n hɔbɩrɛ ka mɔɔ ʊ, mɩsɩ mɔɔ zɛ, awɔɔ n hi ka mɔɔ ʊ, mɔɔ bʊr awɔɔ zi saana, awɔɔ mɔɔ si a har ʊ,

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: mɔɔ nyɩnta dɩgɩ, awɔɔ n huu du mɔɔ ma, mɔɔ n nyɩnta laafɩɩ y, awɔɔ bɩ dɩga mɔɔ ma, mɔɔ nyɩnta lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ, awɔɔ m bʊr mɔɔ zi b'ʊ.›

ref: Then the ones who pleased the Lord will ask, “When did we give you something to eat or drink?
text: Meerbaa naa do jɛ ʊ, gʊɔɔ tɩrgɔɔ wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: ‹Zuuba, wɔɔ ɩbɩɩ yɩ brɔɔ nɔ n'ɩbɩɩ zɛrɛ, ʊ hɔbɩrɛ ka ɩbɩɩ ʊ, gɛɛ mɩsɩ n'ɩ zɛrɛ, ʊ hi ka ɩ ʊ?

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
text: Wɔɔ ɩbɩɩ yɩ brɔɔ saana, ʊ ɩbɩɩ si ʊ har ʊ, gɛɛ ɩbɩɩ n dɩgɩ, ʊ huu du ɩ ma?

ref: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
text: Wɔɔ ɩbɩɩ yɩ brɔɔ ɩ nɩ yaaba m, gɛɛ ɩ nɩ lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ, ʊ ta, ʊ aa dɩga ɩ ma?›

ref: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
text: Cir bɩ y'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: ‹Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, hɔɔ nɔɔn duro k'awɔɔ n n'a bam gʊɔɔ poorero nɔɔn gʊaa deem m bɩ, kʊ ŋ nɩ mɔɔ danyɩnɔ wɔɔ bɩ, a ba awɔɔ nawʊm mɔɔ m.›

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: Naa jɛ ʊ, a y'a hɩm jɩsa ra zannɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: ‹K'a jɩ bɔ mɔɔ ma a ta laatʊ, awɔɔ kʊ Woso lerbɔrɛ bʊnyaa n'a jɛ ʊ, k'a ta sɛ k'a lihire ba ʊ, n n a banka Sʊtaana kan a malɛɛkarɔ kɩ minto b'ʊ.

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.

ref: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
text: Brɔɔ y'a hɩm nɩ, ŋ ʊ: ‹Zuuba, wɔɔ ɩbɩɩ yɩ brɔɔ nɔ n'ɩ zɛrɛ, mɩsɩ n'ɩ zɛrɛ, gɛɛ saana, ɩ n dɩgɩ, ɩ nɩ yaaba m, ɩ nɩ lɔnnɔcɛ ʊ, wɔɔ n dɩga ɩbɩɩ ma, k'ʊ da ɩ han?›

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: Cir bɩ y'a hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: ‹Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, k'awɔɔ n a ba gʊɔɔ poorero nɔɔn duro gʊaa deem m bɩ, awɔɔ n a ba mɔɔ m sɔ y.›

ref: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
text: Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, ŋ y'a hʊrlɛ ŋnɩ ta hʊnhʊrlɛ k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ, bɩ gʊɔɔ tɩrgɔɔ wɔɔ tam mɩsɩrbaa k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ.»

ref: Corinthians
text: Korɛntɩ zannɔ

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: A n a bɔ an bʊr karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ zi, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ gɔɔta an nɩ hinceem bam, an nɩ hoon sim na? K'a dɩga a wakatɩ a ku, n Gʊaanyɩ bɩ nyasʊrɛ, n n a ka mimbʊnyaabənno ʊ.

ref: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.

text: Ŋ n'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Ʊ b'a nyasʊ cibsa bɩ bire ʊ y, kʊ m bɛɛ zɛkʊma nyɩntam zamaa bɩ bire ʊ.» Lʊ tidəər zɛ Yeezuu ma Betanii ʊ (Markɩ
