ref: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
text: Kʊ ŋ cibsa bɩ laarɔ lɔ ŋn'a nya bɩ, ŋ y'a hʊr ŋnɩ ta Oliviyee ci ra. Yeezuu ɩ Pɩyɛɛr bɩsɩkarɛ mim hɩm (Markɩ

ref: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
text: Dɔmɩm bɩ do tɛkka ʊ, Zidas y'a kam toore mɩŋŋa ma k'a Yeezuu nyasʊ a ka ŋ ʊ. Yeezuu ɩ pakkɩ hɔbɩrɛ bɩm kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ kɩ (Markɩ

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Hɔbɩrɛ bɩ kʊ ŋ n'a bɩm bɩ, Yeezuu n a hɩ, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, awɔɔ gʊaa deem mɔɔ nyasʊrɛ, an mɔɔ ka.»

ref: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
text: Bur kʊ n nɩ nyʊnyɔɔ da ʊ cibsa lɛɛkarɛ dɔmɩm ma bɩ, karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ bʊr Yeezuu zi, ŋn'a lar, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ lɛ n taa kʊ aa pakkɩ hɔbɩrɛ banka lɛɛ kara ʊ ɩbɩɩ minto?»

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Hɔbɩrɛ bɩ kʊ ŋ n'a bɩm bɩ, Yeezuu bur sa an barka da Woso m, an a zɩnzɛ ra, an bɔkarɛ an a ka a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ ʊ, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a si a bɩ, naa do naa mɔɔ mɛ m.»

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
text: Bala, mɔɔ ma n naa, gʊaasɩbabaa ma m, an a lo gʊɔɔ ciiro minto, mimbʊnyaarɔ sugur yɩrɛ minto.

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: A hɩ mɔɔ nawʊrɛ awɔɔ m, mɔɔ bɩ m yarɛ m dɩvɛn daa mi dʊntɔ y, an ta an dɔmɩm kʊ mɔɔ n a mim kan awɔɔ kɩ m Zɩ cirbəə nɔ ʊ bɩ ku.»

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
text: Bɩ kur bɩ, Yeezuu a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Gum naa do ʊ bala, awɔɔ haay y'a sɩrakarɛ tom mɔɔ minto, bala, n y'a gʊrsɩra ba Wosoci nɔ ʊ, n ʊ: ‹Mɔɔ seero dundor bɩ zɛm, seero wɔɔ n hɩsɩga da.›

ref: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ ɩbɩɩ m: Gum naa do ʊ bala, an lɛɛ ka koo baadarɛ m, ɩbɩɩ bɩsɩ kam mɔɔ ma gallɛm kaakʊ ɩ ʊ, ɩ n mɔɔ dɔ y.»

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text: Bɩ kur bɩ, Yeezuu ta kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ lɛɛ kʊ n n'a birm Gɛtsemanee ʊ b'ʊ, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a nyɩnta naa ʊ, kʊ m aa yaa da lɛɛ naa do ʊ kʊ m bʊr.»

ref: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
text: A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ heer a zar an a zɔ zɛ ma, k'a gɔɔta naa ʊ, a bɩ hinceem ba y.»

ref: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n meerbaa bɩ sa, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Baa kʊ nawɔnnɔ haay a sɩrakarɛ to, mɔɔ bɩ m sɩrakarɛ tore y.»

ref: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Baa kʊ ga mɔɔ nawʊm kan ɩbɩɩ kɩ, bɩ m. Mɔɔ bɩ bɩsɩ karɛ ɩbɩɩ ma y.» A muno wɔɔ haay n a hɩ maam sɔ. Yeezuu yaadarɛ Gɛtsemanee ʊ (Markɩ

ref: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
text: A n a ya, an a bɔ a hɩɩya hɔ ma, an yaa da, a ʊ: «M Zɩ, kʊ pakra m kʊ fɩryɩrɛ naa do n mɔɔ yɩ, k'an yɩ maam, k'ɩ hɔɔnhɔ n kʊ yɩ.»

ref: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
text: A n ta lɛɛ poore, an a do an a meer ku tara ma, an nɩ yaa darɛ, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «M Zɩ, kʊ fɩryɩrɛ naa do y'a dam ma an a gʊ mɔɔ ma, k'ɩ gʊ m ma. Kan naa kɩ haay ɩ bɩ mɔɔ hɔɔnhɔ ba y, ɩ hɔɔnhɔ ba.»

ref: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
text: K'a wuti! K'ʊ ʊ hʊr! Gʊaa k'a nɩ mɔɔ nyasʊm k'a mɔɔ ka bɩ lee ku.» Yeezuu nyasʊrɛ (Markɩ

ref: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
text: A n Pɩyɛɛr gaa kan Zebedee nyɩnɔ hɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ. Heerzar kan dabɔʊ kɩ gasʊ ʊ.

ref: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
text: A n a wusigə, an bʊr karɩndanyɩnɔ kaakʊrɔ wɔɔ zi, a bɩ dɩga a ŋ hinceem ba. A n a hɩ Pɩyɛɛr m, a ʊ: «Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, awɔɔ n a da a mɩŋŋa ma a jɩm wakatɩ deem nɔ ʊ tɔkkɛ kan mɔɔ kɩ y!

text: K'a yaa da, a bɩ hinceem ba y, a yaa n a do makra nɔ ʊ. Gʊaa heer lɛ taa, b'a mɛsim paŋŋa ba ʊ y.»

ref: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
text: Yeezuu n a ya, an a gʊ ŋ ma a kaakʊ hɔ ma, an aa so a yaadarɛ bɩ ra.

ref: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «M gʊaasɩba, hɔ k'ɩ bʊr, kɩ b'a ba bɩ ba.» A m naa, gʊɔɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ Yeezuu ma, ŋn'a nyasʊ.

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: K'a gɔɔta an nɩ meer bam bɩ, karɩndanyɩnɔ bueerehɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ gʊaa deem Zidas bɩ lee ku. Bʊr a ʊ kan zamaa cir gʊta kɩ, ŋn'ɩ kargʊrɔ m kan bənkənno kɩ. Wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ kan zɩɩfʊrɔ gʊɔɔgusinno wɔɔ kɩ yɩ ŋ nyɔɔ.

ref: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
text: Zidas n a zɔ Yeezuu ma lɛɛm, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, mɔɔ ɩ yaa darɛ ɩbɩɩ m!» A n bɔkarɛ, an yaa da m.

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ kan Yeezuu kɩ rɔ wɔɔ gʊaa deem a kargʊ gaa ʊ, an wosocɛmannɩbər jɩtaarɛ bɩ zibəənyɩ deem sakʊ, an a tʊr gʊ ma.

ref: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɩ kargʊ bɩ du a caan ʊ, bala, gʊaa niŋŋə k'a nɩ yar bam kargʊ m bɩ, kargʊ n n'a zɛm gər.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: A m naa, wosocɛmannɩbər jɩtaarɛ bɩ nɔ n ma ʊ, an a huuro kɩnkaansʊ, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «Woso sʊnsɔ a a wʊ, kasɛtɩzannɔ zibəə a ya an ta wɔɔ ma ra? Awɔɔ Woso sʊnsɔ k'a wʊ bɩ ma.

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu bɩr hɔsɩ hɩrɛ y. Wosocɛmannɩbər jɩtaarɛ bɩ n a ya, an lɛ ka ʊ, a ʊ: «Ɩ wer si Woso k'an mɩsɩr bɩ tɔ ma, sɩra hɩ wɔɔ m, ɩbɩɩ m Krista Woso Nyɩ bɩ gɛ?»

ref: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
text: A n a hʊr b'ʊ, an nɩ tam har bɩ lɛ ma, zibəənyɩlʊ vanta n a yɩ, an a hɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ b'ʊ rɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Gʊaa naa do nyɩnta kan Nazarɛtɩ Yeezuu bɩ kɩ.»

ref: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
text: Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ Yeezuu nyasʊ rɔ wɔɔ ta m wosocɛmannɩbər jɩtaarɛ *Kayiifʊ har ʊ. Lɛɛ bɩ do wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ a so kʊ ma ʊ, kan gʊɔɔgusinno wɔɔ kɩ.

ref: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
text: Pɩyɛɛr n zu Yeezuu jɛ ʊ laatʊ, an ta an aa lee ku wosocɛmannɩbər jɩtaarɛ bɩ har ʊ. A n gasʊ har b'ʊ, an aa nyɩnta kan hallɛdɔmazannɔ wɔɔ kɩ, k'a dɩga mim bɩ nyambʊrlɛ ma.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ wɔɔ kan dʊdɔkənno wɔɔ haay kɩ y'a kam mim ma k'a ka Yeezuu ra, k'a ku a dʊdɔ yar, a ka kʊ n n a zɛ gər.

ref: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
text: Bɩ, ŋ bɩr mim yɩrɛ y, baa kʊ gʊɔɔ bʊr gʊta ŋnɩ ŋʊaar ba k'a ka ra bɩ, ŋ n a da ma, ŋnɩ hɔsɩ yɩ k'a ka ra y. Gʊɔɔ hɩɩyɔɔ ɩ bɔkarɛ ŋnɩ bʊr.

ref: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
text: Ŋ n a hɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Gʊaa naa do a hɩ, a ʊ: Mɔɔ m da ma, m Wosocɛ bɩ wurgə, m m ya, m a dɔ dɔmɩm kaakʊ nɔ ʊ.»

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ nʊ, a ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ n'a hɩrɛ, bɩ mɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m, an a sɩŋŋɩda sa naa ma kɛɛrɛ bɩ, awɔɔ Gʊaanyɩ bɩ yɩrɛ an nyɩnta Woso paŋŋazaa bɩ bɩsɩ ra, awɔɔ y'a yɩm sɔ, an bɩr zɛm warkʊ mim ʊ.»

ref: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
text: Ŋ n lihi da a meer ma, ŋn'a wɔrɔ hunhuŋŋu ŋn'a zɛrɛ, gɔsɩnnɔ n n'a wɔ tɩntam lɛ, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ:

ref: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
text: «Wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa zi ba, ɩ hɩ wɔɔ m, Krista, nka y'ɩ zɛ?» Pɩyɛɛr bɩsɩ karɛ (Markɩ

ref: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a ya, an bɩsɩ ka Yeezuu ma, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ m wer si, mɔɔ n gʊaa naa do dɔ y.»

ref: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
text: Kʊ Pɩyɛɛr nɩ nyɩntam tɩnsara b'ʊ bɩ, wosocɛmannɩbər jɩtaarɛ bɩ zibəənyɩlʊrɔ wɔɔ gʊaa deem a zɔ ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ ɩ kan Galilee gʊaa Yeezuu bɩ kɩ sɔ!»

ref: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
text: Pɩyɛɛr n bɩsɩ ka gʊaa haay lɛɛ, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ n hɔ k'ɩbɩɩ ʊ k'ɩ hɩ naa jɩ dɔ y.»

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Kʊ maasɩrɛ ba poore bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ b'ʊ rɔ wɔɔ a zɔ Pɩyɛɛr ma, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Kʊ sɩra m, ŋ gʊaa n'ɩbɩɩ m sɔ, ɩbɩɩ meerbaa y'ɩ tojɩ hɩnkarɛ.»

ref: Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't know that man!” Right then a rooster crowed,
text: Pɩyɛɛr n a sɩŋŋɩda ba, an n'a wer sim, an n'a tarɛ a rɛ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ n gʊaa naa do dɔ y!» Lɛɛm bɩ, koo n baa da.

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Pɩyɛɛr n mim kʊ Yeezuu a hɩ m bɩ tɔ da a nɔ ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ bɩsɩ kam mɔɔ ma gallɛm kaakʊ koo n bɔkarɛ, an baa da.» A n bɔ, an nɩ nyi kam kan heer zar gʊta kɩ.

ref: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
text: Bala, Pɩlatɩ a dɔ mɩŋŋa a ʊ Yeezuu nyasʊ ŋ y'a ʊ ŋn'a ka a ʊ jɩmbaa minto.

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Bɔ kʊ ŋ y'a ʊ kʊ b'ʊ bɩ, ŋ Sirɛnnɩ gʊaa kʊ n n'a birm Sɩmɔɔn bɩ zɛ ʊ, sʊrdaarɔ wɔɔ n paŋŋa ba ma, an Yeezuu gɔdarkʊra bɩ busu.

ref: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
text: A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Gʊaa k'a n hɔsɩ ba naa kʊ mɔɔ ka ŋn'a zɛ naa, mimbʊnyaa mɔɔ ba.» Bɩ brɔɔ a nyɩ bɔ nʊ, ŋ ʊ: «Kara ɩ pakra ba wɔɔ ma? Ɩbɩɩ mim m!»

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Ŋ nɩ kʊ ma, ŋnɩ busoo bɩ sa ŋnɩ mɔrdor hɔsɩra si m, k'an yɩ saanɔɔ gəənoyaa.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Bɩ y'a ka, n n hɔsɩra bɩ do tɔ ka, n ʊ: ‹Ma sɩra,› an ta, an her dɔmɩm ku.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu yaa jɩm lɛɛzaa Pɩlatɩ lɛɛ. Bɩ n a lar, a ʊ: «Zɩɩfʊrɔ cir n'ɩbɩɩ m gɛ?» Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ hɩ ɩ zim.»

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Bɩ, wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ wɔɔ kan gʊɔɔgusinno wɔɔ kɩ ɩ mim kam Yeezuu ra, b'a n hɔsɩ nyɩ bɔ ʊ y.

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu n deem taan… nyɩ bɔ ʊ y, naa do a ka cir bɩ n zɛ kədə. (Markɩ

ref: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
text: Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ cenim b'ʊ rɔ wɔɔ n'a mim zukə zɛrɛ, ŋn'a sʊnsɔ wʊrɛ.

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: Pɩlatɩ k'a n'a yɩrɛ, a ʊ, awɔɔ b'a dam mim bɩ bankarɛ ma bɩ, bɩ zamaa bɩ y'a ŋʊam k'a lɛɛ zɛ kʊ ma bɩ, a hi kusi, an a wɔ hir m zamaa bɩ lɛɛ, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ ba gʊaa naa do zɛrɛ barla y, awɔɔ mim m!»

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Zamaa bɩ haay n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ: «K'a zɛrɛ mim n wɔɔ yɩ, kan ʊ nyɩnɔ kɩ.»

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: ŋnɩ laa waa ŋn'a ku a mim ma, ŋ ʊ, cinno firgə m, ŋnɩ bənkə da a bɩsɩ wɔ ʊ, ŋnɩ kukurə zɛ a taa ŋn'ɩ yaa wʊm ma, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Wɔɔ ɩ yaa darɛ ɩbɩɩ m zɩɩfʊrɔ cir!»

ref: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
text: A sɩra ka Woso ʊ, an a hɩ a ʊ, Woso Nyɩ n'a mɩ, kɛɛrɛ bɩ, kʊ Woso y'a ŋʊarɛ, k'a n a yɔ wa.»

ref: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
text: Kʊ ŋ yaa wʊ ma ŋn'a nya bɩ, ŋ zaba bɩ bɔ ma, ŋn'a huuro wɔɔ du ma, ŋn'a hʊr m k'a yaa wɔ gɔdarkʊra ma. Ŋ Yeezuu wɔ gɔdarkʊra ma (Markɩ

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ŋ n dɩvɛn war kʊ ma kan hɔ hɛɛnr kɩ, ŋn'a ka ʊ, k'an a mi. K'a da a lɛ ma bɩ, a n a ŋʊa an a mi y.

ref: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
text: Ŋ n a wɔ gɔdarkʊra ma, ŋnɩ pʊɛɛga da a huuro wɔɔ ma ŋnɩ ŋ jir.

ref: Then they sat down to guard him.
text: Ŋ n a gaa, ŋnɩ nyɩnta b'ʊ, ŋn'ɩ dɔm ma.

ref: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text: Ŋ n Yeezuu zɛ bɩ jɩ gʊrsɩra ba warga ma, Yeezuu mim la gɔdarkʊra bɩ ma, ŋ ʊ: ‹Yeezuu m naa, zɩɩfʊrɔ cir m.›

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
text: Ŋ n'a hɩm Yeezuu m, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ k'ɩ n'ɩ dam ma, ɩ Wosocɛ bɩ wurgə, ɩ ya, ɩ dɔ dɔmɩm kaakʊ nɔ ʊ bɩ, ɩ bʊmbɔ ɩ zim kʊ Woso Nyɩ n'ɩbɩɩ m, k'ɩ zer gɔdarkʊra bɩ ma!»

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Bɩ, a muno wɔɔ y'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «Jɩm cɩna, k'ʊ dɩga a Elii zɛm b'a bʊmbɔ gɛ?»

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Sinsebir k'a da bɩ, monsigə gʊta zɛ tara burə ʊ, an ta an wakatɩ kaakʊ ku yirbəəre m.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: Yirbəə wakatɩ kaakʊ zeezi bɩ, Yeezuu zee gʊta da, a ʊ: «Eloyii, Eloyii, lama sabaktaani?» A lɛ taa k'a hɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ Woso, mɔɔ Woso, bɔ m, ɩ ɩ bɔ mɔɔ ʊ?»

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Ŋ gʊaa deem n bra si, an aa hɔ mafʊ-mafʊda sa, an a nyɔɔ hɔ hɛɛnr ma an a da bənkə ma, an a ka Yeezuu ʊ k'an a mi.

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, Wosocɛ bɩ pɛɛra bɩ n zɛ kumbir, an a sa mim ʊ an b'a zɛ tara ma. Tara n nyiŋŋə, ciro n wunwu,

ref: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
text: gəənoyaarɔ n a gʊngʊ. Wosogʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ gɩnga rɔ wɔɔ gɔsɩnnɔ n a mɩm bʊmbɔ ʊ gʊta.

ref: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
text: Ŋ n bɔ yaarɔ wɔɔ ʊ, Yeezuu k'a b'a mɩm bɔ ʊ bɩ hinni ʊ, ŋnɩ gasʊ Zerizalɛm ʊ, kʊ mɩŋŋa b'ʊ, gʊɔɔ gʊta nɩ ŋ yɩ.

ref: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
text: Sʊrdaa zɔrɔ cir, kan sʊrdaarɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ dɔm Yeezuu ma rɔ wɔɔ tara bɩ nyiŋŋəre yɩ, kan hɔ k'a nyɩnta an ba haay bɩ kɩ, ŋ da n bɔ ʊ gʊta, ŋn'a hɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Sɩra m, Woso Nyɩ m paan…»

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Lannɔ nyɩnta b'ʊ guta, ŋn'a to laatʊ ŋn'ɩ dɩgam, zu ŋ y'a ʊ Yeezuu jɛ ʊ Galilee ʊ tee…, ŋn'a dam han.

ref: That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea
text: Woso lɛ k'a b'a zo bɩ, Arimatee kʊ gʊaa arzakazaa deem k'a tɔ m Zozɛf bʊr, Yeezuu karɩndanyɩ deem m sɔ.

ref: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
text: A n ta Pɩlatɩ zi, an aa Yeezuu gər bɩ yɛ han. Pɩlatɩ n lɛ ka, n n a ka ʊ.

ref: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
text: A n gər bɩ sa, an pɛɛra fɛʊda burgu ma,

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: an a da a mɩŋŋa yaa daa k'a hɔ ci ʊ b'ʊ, an jaa gʊta gərgu an a ta rɛ, an a hʊr.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Magdalaa kʊ Maarii kan Maarii vanta bɩ k'ɩ nyɩntam b'ʊ, ŋn'a meer nya yaa bɩ rɛ. N yɩ dɔm yaa bɩ ma

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: *Sabaa bankarɛ hinni k'a cem dɔ n mɛ bɩ, wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ wɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kɩ zu kʊ ʊ ŋnɩ ta Pɩlatɩ zi.

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: Ŋ n a hɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Wɔɔ lɛɛzaa, ŋʊaarzaa naa do k'a nyɩnta an a hɩ, a ʊ: Dɔmɩm kaakʊ hinni ʊ, mɔɔ yɩ m mɩm bɔŋ ʊ bɩ, a tɔ ɩ wɔɔ nɔ ʊ.

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Lɛ ka kʊ n n dɔ yaa bɩ ma, k'an ta an dɔmɩm kaakʊ bɩ ku, k'a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ b'a da ma, ŋnɩ bʊr, ŋn'a gər bɩ kʊna, ŋn'a hɩ zamaa bɩ m, ŋ ʊ: A a mɩm bɔ ʊ gəəno bire ʊ y. Kʊ m bɛɛ m, ŋʊaar naa do lɛ dam lɛɛkarɛ hɔ bɩ m.»

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Pɩlatɩ n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ ɩ sʊrdaarɔ m, k'a ta a ka ŋnɩ dɔ yaa bɩ ma amba awɔɔ a da ma bɩ m.»

ref: So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.
text: Ŋ n a hʊr, ŋnɩ ta, ŋn'aa jaa k'a ta yaa bɩ lɛ bɩ kʊsɩ, ŋnɩ gʊɔɔ da a dɔma ʊ.

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Karɩndanyɩnɔ bueeredeem nɔ wɔɔ n a hʊr, ŋnɩ ta Galilee ʊ, ci kʊ Yeezuu a hɩ ŋ nɩ a ʊ, ŋnɩ ta ra bɩ burə ʊ.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: Brɔɔ n a so kʊ ma, kan gʊɔɔgusinno wɔɔ kɩ, ŋnɩ kʊ ma, ŋnɩ busoo ka sʊrdaarɔ wɔɔ ʊ gʊta.

ref: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
text: A hɔ kʊ mɔɔ dɩnda awɔɔ m haay bɩ dɩnda ŋ nɩ, kʊ ŋ nɩ zu ʊ. Bɩ mɔɔ ba n bɩr kan awɔɔ kɩ dɔmɩm haay nɔ ʊ, k'an ta an durnya nyarɛ ku.»

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «A da bɩ bɔ ʊ y. K'a ta a yaa hɩ mɔɔ danyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, ŋnɩ ta Galilee ʊ, ŋ mɔɔ yɩm b'ʊ.»

ref: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
text: Tara n nyiŋŋə lɛɛm, Zuuba malɛɛka n a to brama an zer, an bɩ jaa bɩ gərgu an a gʊ yaa bɩ rɛ, an nyɩnta ra.

ref: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
text: A mɛ n bɔ kʊ ʊ kan brama nyakɩ kɩ, a huu bɩ fu am lajaa hɔ bɩ m.

ref: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
text: Sʊrdaarɔ wɔɔ da n bɔ ʊ, ŋ mɛ nɩ hinkəm, ŋn'a ba ɩ ʊ, gɩnga ŋ y'a ʊ.

ref: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
text: Bɩ, malɛɛka bɩ meer ba kan lannɔ wɔɔ kɩ, a ʊ: «A da bɩ bɔ ʊ y. Mɔɔ dɔ mɔɔ ʊ Yeezuu kʊ n y'a wɔ gɔdarkʊra ma bɩ awɔɔ n'a kam ma.

ref: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
text: Bɩ, k'a ta zɔɔ a yaa hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: A a mɩm bɔ ʊ, bɩ, a ɩ lɛɛkarɛ awɔɔ lɛɛ Galilee ʊ, lɛɛ bɩ do awɔɔ naa yɩ ʊ, naa do mɔɔ kʊ m a hɩ awɔɔ m.»

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu bʊr an ŋ zɛ ʊ, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ ɩ yaa darɛ awɔɔ m.» Lannɔ wɔɔ n a zɔ ma ŋn'a gannɔ nyɩnnyasʊ, ŋnɩ kukurə zɛ a taa.

ref: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
text: Kʊ ŋn'ɩ tam bɩ, sʊrdaarɔ gɔsɩnnɔ bʊr kʊnɔ ʊ b'ʊ, ŋnɩ bɩ hɔ k'a ba haay bɩ sa, ŋn'a dɩnda wosocɛmannɩbənno jɩtannɔ wɔɔ m.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: Ŋ nɩ lɛ ka ŋ ʊ, ŋ ʊ: «K'a hɩ a ʊ, a karɩndanyɩnɔ ɩ bʊr gunuu ʊ, ŋnɩ b'a gər bɩ kʊna, bɩ ma bɩ, wɔɔ hinceem ba.

ref: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
text: Sʊrdaarɔ wɔɔ n busoo bɩ si, ŋnɩ hɔ kʊ ŋ y'a dɩnda ŋ nɩ bɩ ba, mim naa do n bɔ lɛɛ haay ʊ zɩɩfʊrɔ wɔɔ bire ʊ, an ta an lee ku her dɔmɩm ʊ. Yeezuu hɩnkarɛ (Markɩ

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Yeezuu n a zɔ ŋ ma, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «N paŋŋa do haay ka mɔɔ ʊ, brama kan tara kɩ burə ʊ.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Bɩ, k'a ta doro haay zi, a yaa gʊɔɔ wɔɔ ba mɔɔ karɩndanyɩnɔ, a batɛm ka ŋ ʊ, Zɩ kan Nyɩ kɩ Sɛnt-Ɛspri kɩ tɔ ma.

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: Kʊ ŋ yaa yɩ bɩ, ŋnɩ kukurə zɛ, bɩ gɔsɩnnɔ sɩnsɩm ka.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, ŋ zaŋŋarɔ wɔɔ to, ŋn'ɩ zum kʊ ʊ kan kɩ.

ref: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
text: K'a ta lɛɛ poore bɩ, a Zebedee nyɩnɔ yɩ, Zakkɩ kan a danzaa Zaan kɩ, ŋn'ɩ zaŋŋarɔ bankarɛ a kɔɔlʊʊ nɔ ʊ.

ref: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
text: Yeezuu tɔ bɔ lɛɛ haay ʊ, Galilee kʊ ʊ. Yaabazannɔ waarɛ (Matiyee

ref: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
text: Mim bɩ, a sɩŋŋɩda a ba, amba wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa Ezayii a gʊrsɩra ba biisi bɩ m, a ʊ: Mɔɔ nɩ m zibəəzaa nyɔɔrɛ ɩbɩɩ lɛɛ, k'an b'ɩ zaa banka.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Zaan bɩ, yoonde ka huu a du a ma, an kʊmbar so a ʊ. Bɩsɩnɔ kan zɔɔn kɩ a n'a bɩrɛ.

ref: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
text: A hɔdɩndarɛ nɔ ʊ bɩ, a y'a hɩm gʊɔɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Gʊaa nɩ mɔɔ jɛ ʊ, an nɩ zɛm, bɩ, a zaa paŋŋa cem mɔɔ han. Mɔɔ n m ga m, m sʊnta a taa, m a kʊsɔrɔ bar tɔkkɛ hʊnhʊr y.

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
text: Ŋ gasʊ Kapɛrnawum kʊ nɔ ʊ, *sabaa hinni ʊ, Yeezuu n gasʊ zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ ʊ, an hɔ dɩnda.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ n zɛ kədə a hɔdɩndarɛ bɩ minto. Bala Woso paŋŋa an nɩ hɔ bɩ dɩndam nɩ, a bɩ hɔdeem kan wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ hɔ kɩ y.

ref: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
text: Dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu a to Nazarɛtɩ ʊ, Galilee kʊ nɔ ʊ, an bʊr Zʊrdɛn kɔ ʊ k'a bɩ batɛm si Zaan zi.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Bɩ, k'a bɩ batɛm si an a nya, an nɩ bɔm hi b'ʊ bɩ, brama a gʊ, Sɛnt-Ɛspri n zer a mim ʊ, am cirpʊʊrɛ hɔ bɩ m.

ref: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
text: Kusimə deem n b'a zɔ Yeezuu ma, an sʊnta, an a zuutə a taa, an n'a yɛm han, a ʊ: «K'ɩbɩɩ lɛ taa, ɩbɩɩ ɩ da ma, ɩ mɔɔ waa.»

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: an nɩ Woso lemim nyɩnta pa dam gʊɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Dɔmɩm b'a ku, Woso cirbəə b'a zɔ. K'a bɔ a taam, k'a lemim nyɩnta bɩ si.»

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Yeezuu n ŋ bir lɛɛm, ŋn'a zɩ Zebedee to kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ kan zibənno wɔɔ kɩ, ŋn'a hʊr, ŋn'ɩ zum kʊ ʊ kan kɩ. Yeezuu y'a paŋŋa hɩnkam (Likki

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Yeezuu a zɔ, an a wɔ nyasʊ, an a ka an wuti. Mɛmarɛ bɩ n bɔ a mɛ ʊ, an wuti, an nyasʊ ŋ nɩ.

ref: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
text: ŋn'aa yɩ, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ haay y'a karɛ ɩbɩɩ ma!»

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ bɩ, gʊaa kʊ zinəro n ta m nɩ ŋ sokʊmacɛ b'ʊ, an nɩ zee darɛ, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ:

ref: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
text: «Bɔ ɩbɩɩ lɛ n taa k'ɩ ba wɔɔ nɩ, Nazarɛtɩ Yeezuu? Bʊr ɩbɩɩ ʊ k'ɩ bɩ wɔɔ zibəə zar gɛ? Kʊ nka n'ɩbɩɩ m, mɔɔ dɔ: Woso gʊaa mɩŋŋa n'ɩbɩɩ m.»

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Yeezuu n meer ba ma, a ʊ: «Ɩ tʊr ka, k'ɩ bɔ gʊaa bɩ mɛ ʊ!»

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Zinə bɩ n gʊaa bɩ hinkə kan paŋŋa kɩ, an bɔ a mɛ ʊ kan zee kɩ.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ haay zɛ kədə, ŋn'ɩ laaka dɩndam kʊ han, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ m naa? Hɔdɩndarɛ daa kan paŋŋa gʊta kɩ bɩ ba n nɩ! A m lɛ da bɩ, Yeezuu ɩ həən sim zinəro wɔɔ ma, ŋn'a lɛ sire.»

ref: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Ʊ ta kʊrɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ lezima rɔ wɔɔ ʊ sɔ, kʊ m aa Woso lemim nyɩnta dɩnda ŋ nɩ. Bala, bʊr mɔɔ ʊ bɩ minto.»

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Yeezuu a cicir zɛ, an a wɔ hʊr, an a da ma, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ lɛ taa ma k'ɩ laafɩɩ yɩ.»

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Kʊ ŋ bɔ sokʊmacɛ ʊ lɛɛm bɩ, ŋ ta Sɩmɔɔn kan Andɩr kɩ har ʊ, kan Zakkɩ kɩ Zaan kɩ.

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Bɩ ma bɩ, Sɩmɔɔn dində ɩ wʊtam kan mɛmarɛ kɩ. Lɛɛm bɩ, n yaabazaa bɩ mim hɩ Yeezuu m.

ref: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
text: Yirbəəre m bɩ, woso lɛzore dɔmɩm ʊ bɩ, n bʊr yaabazannɔ m kan hɔɔzannɔ kɩ m haay Yeezuu zi.

ref: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
text: Kʊ bɩ zannɔ haay a so kʊ ma har bɩ lɛ ma.

ref: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
text: A yaabazannɔ kʊ ŋ nɩ cicir bam yaaba do haay m nɔ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ waa gʊta, an la zinəro ra gʊta gʊɔɔ mɛ ʊ. Yeezuu n zinəro wɔɔ to ŋn'ɩ meer barɛ y, kʊ ŋ y'a dɔ bɩ minto. Yeezuu ɩ lemim nyɩnta pa dam Galilee ʊ (Likki

ref: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
text: Dɔgta mɩŋŋa m, Yeezuu wuti, an bɔ, an ta poohɔɔn ʊ, an nɩ yaa dam b'ʊ.

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Sɩmɔɔn wuti kan a muno kɩ, ŋn'a dɩndaŋ ʊ,

ref: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
text: A a da Galilee kʊrɔ wɔɔ zi haay, an nɩ hɔ dɩndarɛ ŋ nɩ ŋ sokʊmacɛrɔ wɔɔ ʊ, an nɩ lam zinəro ra. Yeezuu laafɩɩ ka kusimə ʊ (Matiyee

ref: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, yaaba bɩ bɔ kusimə bɩ mɛ ʊ, an a ba mɩŋŋa.

ref: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
text: Bɩ jɛ ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu a nyɔɔ, an lɛ ka ʊ,

ref: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
text: an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «Dundo ɩ zi, ɩ b'a ka ɩ hɩ gɔsɩ nɩ y. Ta ɩ yaa ɩ hɩnka wosocɛmannɩbər bɩ m, *hubɔrɛ mannɩ kʊ Moyiisi lɛ ka awɔɔ ʊ k'a ba bɩ, k'ɩ bɩ ba, kʊ gʊaa biyəə n a dɔ, a ʊ, ɩbɩɩ laafɩɩ yɩ.»

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Bɩ, gʊaa bɩ bɔ, an a hʊr, an a sɩŋŋɩda sa, an nɩ mim bɩ dɩndam gʊɔɔ m lɛɛ haay ʊ. Naa do minto ma, Yeezuu b'a dam ma, an a hɩnka, an gasʊ kʊnɔ ʊ y. Gɔɔta a tɩr a ʊ bɔlɛɛmarɔ ʊ. Bɩ, gʊɔɔ a to lɛɛ haay ʊ, ŋn'ɩ zɛm Yeezuu zi.

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: gasʊ k'a ʊ Wosocɛ ʊ kan a gʊɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ, wosocɛmannɩbər jɩtaarɛ Abɩyataar dɔmɩm ʊ, an bur kʊ ŋ y'a cɩnta Woso m bɩ gʊr, an a bɩ kan a gʊɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ, awɔɔ n a karɩnda ba ra? Bɩ, ŋ n a ga m, ŋn'a bɩ, kʊ wosocɛmannɩbənno wɔɔ bɛɛ m bala y.»

ref: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
text: Gʊɔɔ cir gʊta bʊr, ŋnɩ b'a so kʊ ma b'ʊ, lɛ ba b'ʊ y, baa cɛlɛ ma bɩ, lɛ ba ʊ sɔ y. Yeezuu nɩ Woso lemim dɩndam gʊɔɔ wɔɔ m.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Gʊɔɔ gɔsɩnnɔ bʊr zi mɛgarɛzaa m, ŋn'a busu gʊaa si.

ref: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
text: Kʊ ŋ n a da ma, ŋn'ɩ tam nɩ Yeezuu zi bɩ, zamaa bɩ minto m, ŋ n cɛ mim bɩ hɔn ra, bɩncɛ kʊ Yeezuu n ta ʊ b'ʊ, ŋnɩ mɛgarɛzaa bɩ busu kan a wʊtarahɔ bɩ kɩ, ŋn'a zer cɛ bɩ yaa bɩ m.

ref: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
text: «Bɔ m, gʊaa naa do nɩ meer naa do barɛ? Woso sʊnsɔ a n'a wʊm! Nka y'a da ma, an gʊaa mimbʊnyaarɔ sugur ka ʊ, kʊ Woso deem bɛɛ?»

ref: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
text: Bɩ, awɔɔ y'a dɔrɛ, a ʊ: Gʊaanyɩ b'a da ma, an gʊɔɔ mimbʊnyaarɔ sugur ka ŋ ʊ, tara burə ʊ naa ʊ.»

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Yeezuu a ya, an ta Galilee higʊta lɛ ra. Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ haay bʊr zi, an nɩ Woso lemim dɩndarɛ ŋ nɩ.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Yeezuu a ya, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Hoonsire dɔmɩm bɩ ba Woso ʊ, gʊaa minto, bɩ, a n gʊaa ba hoonsire dɔmɩm minto y.

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
text: Kʊ Yeezuu nɩ nyɩntam Levii har ʊ b'ʊ k'a hɔ bɩ bɩ, yawʊrsinno kan mimbʊnyaabənno kɩ gʊta nyɩnta kan kɩ, kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ. Bala, gʊɔɔ gʊta zu kʊ ʊ kan Yeezuu kɩ.

ref: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
text: Zɛkʊrazannɔ n ta ʊ, ŋn'ɩ ŋ birm farizɛɛnrɔ. Kʊ farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ kan wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ a yɩ, an nɩ hɔbɩrɛ bɩm kan yawʊrsinno wɔɔ kɩ, mimbʊnyaabənno wɔɔ kɩ bɩ, ŋ y'a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, ŋ ʊ: «A lɔ, an nɩ hɔbɩrɛ bɩm kan yawʊrsinno wɔɔ kɩ, mimbʊnyaabənno wɔɔ kɩ?»

ref: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ bɩ, Zaan karɩndanyɩnɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ karɩndanyɩnɔ kɩ y'a lɛ kʊsɩm. Ŋ bʊr, ŋnɩ bɩ Yeezuu lar, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ minto nɩ, Zaan karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kan farizɛɛnrɔ hɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ n'a lɛ kʊsɩm, b'ɩbɩɩ hɔɔ wɔɔ bɩr a lɛ kʊsɩrɛ?»

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Dɔmɩnnɔ niŋŋoo zɛm bɩ nyɩnta ʊ, kʊ n bɩ lʊ bɩ zim bɩ gʊ ŋ ma, ŋ y'a lɛ kʊsɩm dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ.»

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: A n a ya, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «Gʊaa ba ʊ, an nɩ pɛɛra daa kər sam, an a sɩnsar kan huu gusire kɩ y. Pɛɛra daa kər bɩ n nɩ huu gusire hɔ bɩ gaam, an n'a kaansʊm, an nɩ cenim biisi han.»

ref: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
text: A n a ya, an a vanta hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Gʊaa ba ʊ, an nɩ *dɩvɛn daa sam k'a tɔ hibɔɔnrɔ gusinno ʊ y. Kʊ n bɛɛ m, dɩvɛn bɩ hibɔɔnrɔ wɔɔ hɩnhaanm. Dɩvɛn bɩ kan hibɔɔnrɔ wɔɔ kɩ, ŋ y'a zaar bam. Bɩ, k'a dɩvɛn daa tɔ hibɔɔnrɔ daarɔ ʊ.» Sabaa hinni bɩsɩkarɛ (Matiyee

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Hindeem ʊ, *sabaa hinni ʊ, Yeezuu bɔ hɔsɩrarɔ ʊ. A karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ zaa sa ŋn'ɩ tam bɩ, ŋ y'a sɩŋŋɩda ba, ŋn'ɩ hɔyaa gɔyir yɩnyarm.

ref: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ wɔɔ n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Dɩga, hɔ kʊ ŋ n'a bam sabaa hinni ʊ! A n a ga m, ŋn'a ba a hinni ʊ y.»

ref: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Mim k'a pakra ba Davɩd m kan a gʊɔɔ kɩ bɩ dɔmɩm ʊ, hɔ niŋŋə k'a a ba, nɔ k'a ŋ nyasʊ bɩ,

ref: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
text: Bɩ y'a ka, Gʊaanyɩ bɩ m sabaa hinni bɩ minzaa.»

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: A a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ, k'a a zɔ kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ m mɔɔ jɩr ʊ, kʊ zamaa bɩ b'a da ma, ŋn'a so mɔɔ ʊ y.

ref: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
text: Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ bʊr b'ʊ rɔ wɔɔ ɩ dɩgam Yeezuu ma, hɔ k'a n'a bam *sabaa hinni ʊ, k'a gʊaa bɩ waam k'a yɩ. A ka brɔɔ nawʊm ma, k'a mim ka Yeezuu ra, mim naa do jɛ ʊ.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ wɔgərzaa bɩ m, a ʊ: «Wuti, ɩ bʊr bireyaa ʊ naa ʊ!»

ref: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
text: A ɩ dɩgarɛ ŋ ma a jɩr ʊ kan nɔmaa kɩ, a heer n a zar ŋ heerkarɛ bɩ minto. A n a hɩ gʊaa bɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɩ wɔ bɩ hʊr!» B'a a hʊr, a wɔ bɩ n a banka.

ref: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
text: Yeezuu ta kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ Galilee higʊta lɛ ra. Zamaa gʊta n zu a jɛ ʊ. Zamaa bɩ, a to ŋ y'a ʊ Galilee ʊ, Zidee ʊ,

ref: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
text: Bueerehɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ kʊ Yeezuu ŋ bir lɔ wɔɔ ba n nɩ: Sɩmɔɔn kʊ n n'a hɩm ma Pɩyɛɛr ta ʊ,

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Yeezuu der ci ra, an nɩ gʊɔɔ k'a lɛ n taa ŋ ma rɔ wɔɔ birm. Brɔɔ ŋ bʊr zi.

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: Zebedee nyɩ Zakkɩ ta ʊ, kan a danzaa Zaan kɩ. Yeezuu tɔ vanta ka brɔɔ ma, a ʊ: Bʊanɛrgɛs, a lɛ taa k'a hɩ: bramahəənsire nyɩnɔ.

ref: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
text: Andɩr ta ʊ sɔ, kan Filipi kɩ, Bartelemii kɩ, Matiyee kɩ, Toma kɩ, kan Alfee nyɩ Zakkɩ kɩ, Tade kɩ, Sɩmɔɔn *Zelɔtɩ kɩ sɔ,

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a to Zerizalɛm nɔ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ, ŋ y'a hɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Bɛlzebul nɩ kan kɩ!» Ŋ y'a hɩ sɔ, ŋ ʊ: «Zinəro cir daahan minto y'a ka, an wɔ ʊ, an nɩ lam zinəro ra.»

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Zamaa bɩ nyɩnta, n nɩ bɩr ma, n n'a hɩm nɩ, n ʊ: «Gaafra, ɩbɩɩ da kan ɩbɩɩ danyɩnɔ kɩ y'ɩbɩɩ birm garga ra.»

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Yeezuu n ŋ bir, an hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom da ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Sʊtaana y'a bam lɔ, an zoo ba kan a mɩŋŋa kɩ?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Yeezuu a wusigə, an bʊr har ʊ, zamaa bɩ n a ya, an a so kʊ ma. Bɩ minto nɩ, Yeezuu kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ ba toore m k'a hɔ bɩ y.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Kʊ k'a gʊɔɔ bɩr kʊ mam bɩ y'a darɛ.

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Kʊ har gʊɔɔ a gʊngʊ, har bɩ do b'a dam ma, an ta lɛɛ y.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: Gʊaa ba ʊ, an b'a dam ma, an gasʊ gʊaa paŋŋazaa har ʊ, k'a mɔrlɔ gʊr, k'a n ser ka, an bʊr la an a sʊr y. K'a sʊr an a nya, b'a bɔkarɛ an gasʊ, an mɔrlɔ wɔɔ gʊr.

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Sɩra mɔɔ n'a hɩrɛ awɔɔ m: Woso mimbʊnyaarɔ haay kan sʊnsɔrɔ haay kɩ sugur kam gʊɔɔ ʊ.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Bɩ gʊaa biyəə k'a nɩ *Sɛnt-Ɛspri sʊnsɔ wʊm bɩ, a b'a sugur yɩm fɩɩga y. A mimbʊnyaa bɩ gɔɔtarɛ a mim ʊ dɔmɩm k'a nyarɛ ba ʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ.»

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Yeezuu naa do hɩ, kʊ ŋ ʊ: Sʊtaana nɩ kan kɩ bɩ minto. Yeezuu kan a har gʊɔɔ kɩ mim (Matiyee

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Bɩ jɛ ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu da kan Yeezuu danyɩnɔ kɩ bʊr, ŋnɩ jɩm garga ra, ŋ n nyɔɔ da kʊ ŋ n Yeezuu bir.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «Nka m mɔɔ da? Nkɔɔ m mɔɔ danyɩnɔ?»

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: A n bɔkarɛ, an a bɔ, an nɩ dɩgam gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nyɩnta ŋnɩ bɩr ma rɔ wɔɔ ma, an n'a hɩrɛ, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ da bɩ ba n nɩ, kan mɔɔ danyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ!

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Gʊaa niŋŋə k'a nɩ Woso hɔɔnhɔ bam bɩ, mɔɔ danyɩ m, mɔɔ danlʊ m, mɔɔ da m.»

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Yeezuu a ya, an nɩ hɔ dɩndam Galilee higʊta lɛ ra. Zamaa a so kʊ ma gʊta ŋnɩ bɩr ma, an wuti, an aa nyɩnta kɔɔlʊʊrɔ wɔɔ deem ʊ hi bɩ nɔ ʊ. Zamaa bɩ haay n nyɩnta tara ʊ hi bɩ lɛ ra.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: K'a n'a duum bɩ, gɔsɩnnɔ n a lo zaa gʊr ma. Bɛnnɔ n bʊr, ŋnɩ b'a bɩ.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: A hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom darɛ nɔ ʊ, a hɔ dɩnda ŋ nɩ gʊta. A hɔdɩndarɛ nɔ ʊ, a a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ:

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: «K'a tʊr ka: gʊaa ɩ bɔ k'a hɔ duu, a duure nɔ ʊ bɩ, a hɩsɩga an nawʊm.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Gɔsɩnnɔ n a lo jaarɔ bɩncɛ ʊ, taa n nyɩnta b'ʊ gʊta y. Hɔyaa bɩ n lɛɛ ka an bɔ, kʊ taa ba ʊ gʊta b'ʊ bɩ minto.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Woso k'a der bɩ, an na hɔka bɩ ma, an ga, kʊ ŋ məsi ba ʊ bɩ minto.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Gɔsɩnnɔ a zar laa bɩncɛ ʊ, laa bɩ n haan, an a ta ŋ mee, ŋ n nyɩ da y.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yeezuu a ya, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «K'ʊ Woso cirbəə makra ba lʊnlɔ kan bɔ kɩ?

ref: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
text: Kʊ zamaa b'a hʊr bɩ, gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ gɔɔta kan Yeezuu kɩ, a karɩndanyɩnɔ bueerehɩɩyɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ bɩ, ŋ nyɩnta ŋn'a larm a hɔbɔkʊrɛnoonno wɔɔ jɩ ma.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: Bɩ, ŋ mɩŋŋɔɔ məsi ba ʊ y, hindeem gʊɔɔ m. Kʊ cicir gɛɛ fɩryɩrɛ bʊr Woso lemim bɩ minto, bɩ ŋ y'a to.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: Bɩ minto y'a ka gʊɔɔ wɔɔ nɩ dɩgam, bɩ ŋ bɩr lɛɛ yɩrɛ y, ŋn'a tʊr kam, bɩ ŋ bɩr hɔsɩ jɩ marɛ kʊ ŋ b'a da ma ŋn'a bɔ a taam kʊ Woso n sugur ka ŋ ʊ y.» (Matiyee

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Hɔyaa k'a a lo zaa lezi bɩ, bɔ ŋ y'a ʊ kʊ ʊ kan gʊɔɔ kɩ. Brɔɔ Woso lemim bɩ ma, lɛɛm bɩ Sʊtaana lemim kʊ n y'a duu ŋ heer ʊ bɩ si ŋ han.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Hɔduurezaa bɩ, Woso lemim a n n'a duum.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: A a ya, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «N bɩr sɛ dam fɩtɩrɛ ʊ, n n'a cɩntam kasɩ gɛɛ gargʊ taa y, gɛɛ lɔ? A cɩnta n nawʊm hɔ burə ʊ.

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: A a ya, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Woso cirbəə bɩ, bɔ a kʊ ʊ kan gʊaa k'a nɩ hɔ duum tara ʊ bɩ kɩ.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Tara bɩ n'a zi barɛ, hɔyaa bɩ n nɩ lɛɛkarɛ an nɩ haanm, an nɩ gɔ dam, an nɩ jɩsɩ sim, a nyambʊrlɛ ma bɩ, an nɩ nyɩ dam.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Hɔyaa bɩ k'a ma bɩ, a da n nawʊm, k'a dɔmɩm a ku bɩ minto.» Mutardɩ yaa hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom (Matiyee

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: Bɔ a kʊ ʊ kan hɔ kʊ n n'a hɩm ma mutardɩ bɩ yaa kɩ; A m hɔyaa haay k'a nɩ durnya nɔ ʊ rɔ wɔɔ poore, n n'a duum.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: Kʊ n y'a duu, ma a nawʊm, an nɩ gɔ dam an lɛ da hɔyaa haay ra, an nɩ haanm gʊta, a vəŋŋəro n ta ʊ, bɛnnɔ n b'a dam ma ŋnɩ cɛ kɩnka ma.» Woso lemim dɩndarɛ hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: Hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom an n'a dam gʊɔɔ wɔɔ m bala, bɩ k'a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ nɩ, a y'a jɩ hɩrɛ ŋ nɩ haay. Yeezuu hɩnhɛr gʊta da jɩm ʊ (Matiyee

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Hinni bɩ do yirbəə m bɩ, Yeezuu a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Ʊ bra gəm deem bɩ ra.»

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Yeezuu ɩ Woso lemim nyɩnta dɩndam gʊɔɔ wɔɔ m kan makrarɔ kʊ ŋ bɔ kʊ ʊ kɩ gʊta, kʊ ŋ n'a da ma ŋn'a jɩ ma.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Kʊ Yeezuu a gʊ zamaa bɩ ma bɩ, a zu kʊ ʊ kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ, ŋn'aa gasʊ kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ. Kɔɔlʊʊrɔ vantɔɔ nyɩnta b'ʊ sɔ kan kɩ.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, hɩnhɛr gʊta wuti an nɩ hɔnm. Hi kurə bɩ n'a tɔm kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ, an a han lɛɛm.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Bɩ ma bɩ, Yeezuu a mim ka minkarahɔ ra an nɩ hinceem bam kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ nɔ ʊ jɛ ʊ, ŋn'a gu, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Karɩnsaamba, wɔɔ gɩngarɛ bɩr hɔsɩ barɛ ɩbɩɩ m na?»

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: K'a gu bɩ, an həən si hɩnhɛr bɩ ma, an a hɩ higʊta bɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɩ tʊr ka ɩ zɛ sɩɩ…» Hɩnhɛr bɩ n jɩm, lɛɛ bɩ n zɛ sɩɩ….

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «Bɔ m, awɔɔ nɩ nyi barɛ ncɩnaaʊ? Awɔɔ ba sɩrakarɛ nɩ?»

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: Nyibəə gʊta n gasʊ ŋ ʊ, ŋn'a hɩm kʊ m, ŋ ʊ: «Bɩ, gʊaa bɩ kara n naa, hɩnhɛr kan hi kɩ n'a lɛ sire?»

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: Bala, lʊ b'a hɩ a mɩŋŋa m, a ʊ: «Kʊ mɔɔ m wɔ da a huu tɔkkɛ ma, mɔɔ yɩ m waam.»

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ nyɩnnɩ, ɩ sɩrakarɛ ɩ bʊmbɔ, ta kan laafɩɩ kɩ, bɩ, ɩ ɩ waa ɩ yaaba bɩ minto.»

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: A a yɔ gʊta nyɩsɩbənno wɔ ʊ, a nyɩsɩbarɛ nɔ ʊ. A a wɔhɔ zar haay a tar la, b'a n a waa y. Yaaba bɩ n'a dam la.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: A n gasʊ, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Bɔ tatʊr kan baadarɛ kɩ nɩ? Nyɩ bɩ n ga y, hinceem a ba.»

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Ŋ yaa lee ku higʊta tʊr ʊ, Gerazaa zannɔ kʊ ʊ.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: A wʊtajɩ m gəənoyaa ra bɩ m. Baa kan pɔyaabar kɩ, gʊaa paŋŋazaa ba ʊ, an b'a dam ma, an a nyasʊ y.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Bala, dɔmɩm biyəə bɩ, ŋ pɔyaa da a gam ʊ, ŋn'a wɔ kʊsɩ pɔyaabar m, an a cencer haay. Gʊaa ba ʊ an nɩ bʊrm ma y.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: Sinuu ʊ kan a gunuu ʊ kɩ, gəənoyaarɔ an nɩ wʊtaŋ ʊ kan ciro wɔɔ burə kɩ, zee an n'a dam, an nɩ yaaba darɛ a mɩŋŋa ma jaarɔ m.

ref: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
text: K'a a to an Yeezuu yɩ laatʊ bɩ, a bra si, an aa sʊnta a taa,

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: an nɩ zee darɛ, a ʊ: «Yeezuu, Woso k'a lɛ da haay Nyɩ, bɔ ɩbɩɩ lɛ n taa k'ɩ ba mɔɔ nɩ? Mɔɔ y'a yɛrɛ ɩbɩɩ han Woso tɔ ma gʊta: ɩ bɩ zibʊnyaa ba mɔɔ nɩ y!»

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Dɔmɩm bɩ do ʊ bɩ, kurkuuro cir gʊta nɩ ci bɩ zeezi, ŋn'ɩ hɔ bɩm.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Zinəro wɔɔ n n'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «A ka k'ʊ gasʊ kurkuuro wɔɔ mɛ ʊ!»

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Kurkuuro dundonno wɔɔ n bra si, ŋn'aa hɔ k'a ba bɩ hɩ taranɔzannɔ wɔɔ m, kan lezimazannɔ wɔɔ kɩ m. Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ n bʊr, ŋnɩ bɩ dɩga hɔ k'a nyɩnta an ba bɩ ma,

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: ŋnɩ bʊr Yeezuu jɩr ʊ, ŋnɩ bɩ gʊaa kʊ zinəro Leziyɔn n ta m bɩ yɩ, an huu du a ma, an nɩ nyɩntam kan laafɩɩ kɩ. Dabɔʊ n ŋ nyasʊ.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Ŋ n a da a ra, ŋn'a yɛm Yeezuu han k'an a hʊr a tara bɩ ra.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Bɩ Yeezuu n zaa ka ʊ y, b'a a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Doo ɩ yaa a hɩ ɩ har zannɔ m. K'ɩ hɔ kʊ Zuuba a ba ɩbɩɩ m a cicirzɛrɛ minto bɩ hɩ ŋ nɩ.»

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: Gʊaa bɩ n a hʊr, an aa hɔ kʊ Yeezuu a ba a m haay bɩ dɩnda Taraburo wɔɔ gʊɔɔ wɔɔ m. Gʊaa haay n zɛ kədə. Zayɩɩr nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ waarɛ kan lʊ k'a wɔ da Yeezuu huu ma kɩ (Matiyee

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ nɩ yaa wʊm Yeezuu ma. A n la gʊɔɔ wɔɔ ra, ŋnɩ bɔ, an nyɩ bɩ zɩ kan a da kɩ to, a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ, ŋnɩ gasʊ nyɩ bɩ bɩncɛ ʊ b'ʊ.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Kʊ Yeezuu a wusigə kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ m an higʊta bɩ zɛ ra an lee ku tʊr deem bɩ ra b'ʊ bɩ, zamaa cir gʊta a zɛ kʊ ra, ŋnɩ bʊr zi. Nyɩnta Yeezuu b'ʊ higʊta bɩ lɛ ra.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ cir deem n bʊr, a tɔ m Zayɩɩr. K'a Yeezuu yɩ bɩ, a kukurə zɛ a taa,

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: an a yɛ han kan a heer kɩ haay, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ poore a zɔ zɛ ma, bʊr ɩ bɩ ɩ wɔ ka nyɩ bɩ mim la, k'an a waa, k'an la sɔ.»

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Yeezuu n cem kan kɩ, zamaa gʊta n zu kʊ ʊ kan kɩ, ŋn'a so ʊ, ŋn'ɩ tʊntɔm kʊ ra.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Lʊ n ta ʊ, ma n'a dam zi a dɔɔraa bueerehɩɩya hɔ m naa.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Lʊ bɩ k'a Yeezuu mim ma bɩ, a bʊr a kur zamaa gaa ʊ b'ʊ, an a wɔ da a huu ma.

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, lʊ bɩ yaaba b'a da jɩm ʊ. A n a dɔ a ʊ, yaaba bɩ bɔ a mɛ ʊ, b'a a waa.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, Yeezuu a dɔ a ʊ, paŋŋa bɔ a mɛ ʊ poore, an a wusigə zamaa bɩ bire ʊ, an laaka da, a ʊ: «Nka y'a wɔ da mɔɔ huu ma?»

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: A karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ n a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ yɩ zamaa bɩ n'ɩbɩɩ nyɔɔtam, ɩ ɩ ya, ɩ nɩ laaka darɛ, ɩ ʊ, nka y'a wɔ da ɩbɩɩ ma gɛ?»

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Yeezuu nɩ dɩndɩgarɛ a jɩr ʊ, k'a zaa yɩ.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Kɛɛrɛ bɩ, lʊ b'ɩ nyi bam a mɛ n nɩ hinkəm, k'a hɔ k'a ba a m bɩ dɔ bɩ, a bɩ kukurə zɛ Yeezuu taa, an a sɩra hɩ m haay.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: K'a nɩ meer bam bɩ, gʊɔɔ a to zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ cir bɩ har ʊ b'ʊ, ŋnɩ b'a hɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ ga. K'ɩ ɩ ya, ɩ Zuuba da kɔrɛ ma lʊnlɔ gɛ?»

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Bɩ, Yeezuu n a tʊr ka mim bɩ ma y. A n a hɩ zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ cir bɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɩ da bɩ bɔ ʊ y, sɩra ka bala.»

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: A n yɛ kʊ gɔsɩ n zu kʊ ʊ kan a kɩ y. Pɩyɛɛr a gaa bala, Zakkɩ kan a danzaa Zaan kɩ.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Kʊ ŋ yaa lee ku zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ cir bɩ har ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu gʊɔɔ yɩ, ŋn'ɩ tatʊr barɛ, ŋn'ɩ baa darɛ, ŋn'ɩ zee dam.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ wuti, an nɩ tam. Bala, nyɩ bɩ dɔɔraa bueerehɩɩya. A m bɩ, zamaa bɩ n zɛ kədə.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Yeezuu lɛ ka gʊɔɔ wɔɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Ŋ b'a hɩ gɔsɩ nɩ y.» A n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a hɔbɩrɛ ka nyɩ b'ʊ, k'an a bɩ!»

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Kʊ ŋ higʊta bɩ zɛ ra bɩ, ŋ yaa lee ku Zenezarɛtɩ gəm la, ŋn'a kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ kʊsɩ.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Wosolɛsinnɩsorazaa bɩ daʊrɛ yɩrɛ a zɩ kʊ ʊ kan a yɩrɛrɔ kɩ, a har gʊɔɔ kɩ bire ʊ y.»

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ k'a hɔ kʊ Yeezuu a ba bɩ ma bɩ, a a hɩ, a ʊ: «Zaan-Batiisi m! Mɔɔ n a ka, n n a mim gʊ ma bɩ y'a a ya, an a mɩm bɔ ʊ!»

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: *Sabaa hinni k'a a ku bɩ, Yeezuu hɔ dɩnda gʊɔɔ m zɩɩfʊrɔ sokʊmacɛ ʊ. Zamaa bɩ n zɛ kədə a hɔdɩndarɛ bɩ tɔ ma. Gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ nyɩnta b'ʊ rɔ wɔɔ gʊta y'a hɩm kʊ m, ŋ ʊ: «Awɔɔ a to mindɔrɛ naa do m ka? A to awɔɔ ʊ hɔɔn naa do m ka, an a da ma, an nɩ hoserlo barɛ a wɔ nɩ?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: Awɔɔ bɛɛ m Maarii nyɩ, an nɩ gɔ wum bɩ ra? Zakkɩ, Zozɛs, Ziidi kan Sɩmɔɔn kɩ danyɩ bɛɛ ra? Awɔɔ danlʊrɔ ba wɔɔ bire ʊ naa ʊ ra?» Naa do a ka ŋ n kaanm Yeezuu m.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: A a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Ŋ bɩ hɔsɩ sa a wɔ ʊ zaa sarɛ minto y. Bur, hɛra, busoo, a b'a sa y, k'a maam bara m.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: K'awɔɔ ta kʊ ʊ, ŋ nɩ kaanm awɔɔ m, kʊ ŋ n a tʊr ka awɔɔ mim ma, k'a ɩ doom, k'a kʊ bɩ taanyisi gʊngʊ a gam ma, k'an nyɩnta kasɛtɩ.»

ref: Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!” So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
text: Bala, Zaan a hɩ Hɩrɔɔdɩ m, a ʊ: «A n a ga m, k'ɩbɩɩ m ɩ dam lʊ yɔm y!»

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Hɩrɔdɩyaadɩ nyɩnta an sɔ Zaan m, a lɛ n taa k'a ka kʊ n n a zɛ, b'a b'a dam ma y.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ ɩ nyi bam Zaan m, an nɩ kum la, bala, a a dɔ, a ʊ: Zaan bɩ, gʊaa tɩrga nɩ, gʊaa mɩŋŋa m sɔ. K'a a tʊr ka Zaan meerbaa bɩ ma bɩ, a n a dɔ kʊ bɔ an n'a hɩm Zaan nɩ y, b'a a meerbaa bɩ ŋʊa, an n'a tʊr kam ma.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Hɩrɔɔdɩ n a wer si bɩ m, a ʊ: «Hɔ k'ɩbɩɩ lɛ n taa bɩ, mɔɔ y'a karɛ ʊ, baa kʊ mɔɔ cirbəə tara bɩ zɛkumbir m.»

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ n bɔ, an aa a hɩ a da m, a ʊ: «M bɔ yɛ?» A n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «Zaan-Batiisi mim yɛ.»

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ n ta zɔɔ-zɔɔ, an aa a hɩ cir Hɩrɔɔdɩ m, a ʊ: «Mɔɔ lɛ n taa dɩɩtaa, k'ɩ Zaan-Batiisi mim da laaga ʊ, ɩ ka m ʊ.»

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Cir Hɩrɔɔdɩ heer a zar, bɩ, a b'a dam ma, an a gu ma y, k'a a wer si hɔbɩrɛbinno wɔɔ bire ʊ bɩ minto.

ref: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
text: an bʊr m laaga ʊ, an b'a ka nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ b'ʊ, an a si, an a ka a da ʊ.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: Zaan karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kʊ ŋ naa ma bɩ, ŋ bʊr, ŋnɩ bɩ Zaan gər bɩ sa, ŋn'aa bir. Yeezuu gʊaa tisi soor tʊrlɛ (Matiyee

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Yeezuu kan a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ kɩ n toore yɩ k'a hɔ bɩ gʊɔɔ gʊta kʊ ŋ nɩ zɛm, ŋn'a wusigəm bɩ minto. Bɩ minto y'a ka, a ʊ: «K'a bʊr k'ʊ bɔ toore ma, bɩncɛ kʊ gʊɔɔ ba ʊ b'ʊ, k'a hoon si poore.»

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ŋ yaa da kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ, ŋnɩ ta toore ma bɩncɛ kʊ gɔsɩ ba ʊ b'ʊ.

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Gum k'a bʊr an nɩ dam bɩ, Yeezuu karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ a zɔ ma, ŋn'a hɩ m, ŋ ʊ: «Gum da, bɩ pooyaa m naa,

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Yeezuu n a nyɩ bɔ ʊ, a ʊ: «K'a hɔbɩrɛ ka ŋ ʊ, ŋn'a bɩ!» Ŋ n a lar, ŋ ʊ: «Ɩbɩɩ lɛ n taa k'ʊ aa bur si *deniyee zɔɔhɩra k'ʊ b'a ka ŋ ʊ gɛ?»

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a yaa dɩga a, bur karɔ n ta awɔɔ ʊ?» Kʊ ŋ yaa dɩga a bɩ, ŋ b'a hɩ, ŋ ʊ: «Bur soor n ta ʊ kan zɔ kɩ hɩɩya.»

ref: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
text: A n lɛ ka ŋ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n zamaa bɩ nyɩnta bur butur bɩ ra.

ref: They sat down in groups of
text: Ŋ nɩ nyɩnyɩnta zɔrɔ-zɔrɔ, busoor-busoor.

ref: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
text: Bala, ŋ haay a yɩ, nyibəə n ŋ nyasʊ. Lɛɛm bɩ, Yeezuu meer ba kan ŋ kɩ, a ʊ: «K'a sɩra ka, mɔɔ m, a da bɩ bɔ ʊ y!»

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Yeezuu bur soor bɩ si kan zɔ hɩɩya bɩ kɩ a wɔ ʊ, an dɩga brama, an a barka da ʊ, an ŋ zɩnzɛra, an a ka a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ ʊ, kʊ ŋ n a ka zamaa b'ʊ. A zɔ hɩɩya bɩ jir zamaa bɩ m sɔ.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ haay a bɩ, an ŋ ka.

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Ŋ bur kan zɔ kɩ kuuro gʊr kasɩ lɛ ma bueerehɩɩya.

ref: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
text: Lɛɛm bɩ, Yeezuu a hɩ a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ m, a ʊ: «K'a da kɔɔlʊʊ ʊ, k'a bra gəm la Bɛtsayɩdaa zeezi.» A mɩŋŋa n gɔɔta k'a zamaa bɩ da zaa ra.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: K'a ŋ da zaa ra, an a nya bɩ, a a hʊr, an ta ci ra k'a yaa da.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: Yirbəə k'a da bɩ, kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ hi bireyaa ku, a mɩŋŋa nɩ gəm la.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: K'a yɩ hɩnhɛr n a nya ŋ lɛ, ŋn'a yɔm kan kɔɔlʊʊ bɩ bɔrɛ kɩ bɩ, dɔ k'a bʊr an nɩ mɛm bɩ, a wuti an tɔ hi ra, an nɩ tarɛ ŋ zi, an a yo k'a cem ŋ han.

ref: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
text: Kʊ ŋ y'a yɩ an nɩ tam hi ra bɩ, ŋ yɩ dɩgam ma, ŋ ʊ: «Toor m,» ŋn'ɩ zee dam.

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: A n da kɔɔlʊʊ b'ʊ kan ŋ kɩ, hɩnhɛr bɩ n jɩm. Ŋ n zɛ kədə, ŋ n a so a ʊ y.

ref: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
text: Baa kʊ ŋ bur bɩ jirle yɩ bɩ, ŋ n hɔsɩ jɩ ma ŋn'a dɔ ŋ ʊ, Yeezuu a da hɔ haay ma y. Zenezarɛtɩ yaabazannɔ waarɛ (Matiyee

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Kʊ ŋ zer lɛɛm bɩ, gʊɔɔ wɔɔ Yeezuu dɔ.

ref: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
text: Ŋ n a si, ŋnɩ ta kʊrɔ haay ʊ, ŋn'aa yaabazannɔ busu a gʊraŋŋarɔ m, ŋnɩ bʊr ŋ nɩ bɩncɛ kʊ Yeezuu n ta ʊ b'ʊ.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Bɩncɛ k'a nɩ taŋ ʊ, kʊrɔ gʊtɔɔ ʊ, kʊrɔ poorero ʊ, bɔlɛɛmarɔ ʊ, zɛ n nawʊm yaabazannɔ m daasɩrɔ ʊ, ŋn'a yɛm han, k'an yɛ kʊ ŋ n'a wɔ da a huu lɛ ma bala. Bɩ gʊɔɔ kʊ ŋ y'a wɔ da ma rɔ wɔɔ waarɛ yɩ haay.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ kan wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ gɔsɩnnɔ kɩ y'a to Zerizalɛm ʊ, ŋn'a zɛ kʊ ra, ŋnɩ bʊr Yeezuu zi,

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Woso lɛrɔ wɔɔ bɔ awɔɔ ʊ a jɛ m, an nɩ zum a yɩnnɩbayɩ ʊ.

ref: They noticed that some of his disciples ate without first washing their hands.
text: ŋnɩ Yeezuu karɩndanyɩnɔ gɔsɩnnɔ yɩ, ŋn'ɩ hɔ bɩm kan dintim kɩ, a lɛ taa k'a hɩ, a ʊ: ŋ n a wɔ hir y.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Kʊ sɩra nɩ, farizɛɛnrɔ kan zɩɩfʊrɔ kɩ haay hɔbɩrɛ bɩrɛ bɩ, a wɔ ŋn'a hirm mɩŋŋa, amba ŋ yaabɔɔ yɩnnɩbayɩ m.

ref: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
text: Kʊ ŋ y'a to daasɩ ʊ, ŋnɩ bʊr, ŋ bɩr hɔ bɩm kʊ ŋ n dintim gʊ a ma y. Ŋ yɩnnɩbayɩrɔ vantɔɔ ta ʊ gʊta, amba hɛrɔ sʊrgɔrɛ, gərlo kan hɔbɩrɛ mɔdrɔ kɩ.

ref: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
text: Farizɛɛnrɔ kan wosocikarɩnsaambɔɔ wɔɔ kɩ laaka da Yeezuu han, ŋ ʊ: «Bɔ m, ɩbɩɩ karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ bɩr zum wɔɔ yaabɔɔ yɩnnɩbayɩ b'ʊ, ŋnɩ bɔkarɛ ŋn'ɩ hɔ bɩm, bɩ ŋ n hi nyasʊ?»

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «Zambʊzannɔ, Ezayii gumə ta ʊ, Woso lɛ k'a a si an so ra, an awɔɔ mim hɩ bɩ nɔ ʊ, bala, a a gʊrsɩra ba ncɩnaaʊ, a ʊ: Gʊɔɔ nɔɔn duro, mɔɔ da ŋ nawʊŋ ʊ a lɛ m, bɩ ŋ heer laatʊ mɔɔ ma,

ref: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
text: bɩ mɔɔ daʊrɛ bɩ vam m. Durnya mim ŋ n'a dɩndam gʊɔɔ m, Woso hɔ bɛɛ y.

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Woso lɛrɔ wɔɔ awɔɔ to, an nɩ zum gʊɔɔ yɩnnɩbayɩrɔ ʊ.»

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: B'awɔɔ, awɔɔ y'a hɩm, a ʊ: Kʊ gʊaa a hɩ a zɩ m gɛɛ a da m, a ʊ: hɔ kʊ mɔɔ m ga m, m m dam ɩbɩɩ han bɩ ‹kɔrbaan m› a lɛ taa k'a hɩ, a ʊ: ‹Woso mannɩ hɔ m.›

ref: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
text: A ba awɔɔ nawʊm ncɩnaaʊ an nɩ Woso lemim bɩ zaar bam a yɩnnɩbayɩrɔ wɔɔ m. Ziro ncɩnaaʊ rɔ wɔɔ lɔɔrɔ awɔɔ nɩ ŋ bam gʊta.» Hɔ k'a nɩ dintim kam gʊaa ma (Matiyee

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: A a ya, an a hɩ, a ʊ: «Bɔ nɩ bɔm gʊaa nɔ ʊ bɩ nɩ dintim kam ma.

ref: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
text: Bɩ jɛ ʊ bɩ, Yeezuu zamaa bɩ bir, an a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a tʊr ka mɔɔ ma, k'a da a heer ʊ.

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: K'a a gʊ zamaa bɩ ma, an gasʊ har ʊ bɩ, a karɩndanyɩnɔ wɔɔ a lar hɔbɔkʊrɛnoom naa do jɩ ma.

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: A n a nyɩ bɔ ŋ nʊ, a ʊ: «Awɔɔ sɔ, awɔɔ n naa do jɩ dɔ ra? Awɔɔ n a dɔ, a ʊ: hɔ k'a n'a tore, an nɩ gasʊm gʊaa nɔ ʊ bɩ b'a dam ma, an dintim ka ma ra?

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: Bala, a nɔ an nɩ gasʊŋ ʊ, an ta, an nɩ bɔm garga ra, a bɩr gasʊrɛ a heer ʊ y.» Ncɩnaaʊ bɩ, Yeezuu y'a hɩnkarɛ, a ʊ: «hɔbɩrɛ haay mɩŋŋa.»

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Hɔɔ bʊnyaarɔ nɔɔn duro haay, bɔ ŋ nawʊm gʊaa heer ʊ, ŋn'ɩ dintim kam ma.» Grɛkkɩ kʊ lʊ sɩrakarɛ mim (Matiyee

ref: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
text: Yeezuu k'a cem b'ʊ bɩ, a ta Tiir kʊ ʊ, an aa gasʊ har ʊ, a lɛ baa kʊ ŋ n a yɩ y. Bɩ, a n a da ma, an a nyaakʊm da y.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Bala, lʊ nɩ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ m, zinə nɩ kan kɩ. Bɩ, lʊ bɩ Yeezuu mim ma, an bra si, an bɩ kukurə zɛ Yeezuu taa.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: Lʊ bɩ, grɛkkɩ do m, yɩ a ʊ Siirii kʊ Fenisii ʊ. A n a yɛ Yeezuu han k'an la zinə bɩ ra nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ mɛ ʊ.

ref: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ m, a ʊ: «A to kʊ nyɩnɔ wɔɔ ŋ hɔ bɩ, an ŋ ka. A n a ga m, ŋnɩ nyɩnɔ hɔbɩrɛ sa, ŋn'a ka jinnyɩnɔ ʊ y.»

ref: Jesus answered, “That's true! You may go now. The demon has left your daughter.”
text: «Meerbaa naa do minto, doo, zinə bɩ bɔ ɩ nyɩmbʊɛɛrɛ bɩ mɛ ʊ.»

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Lʊ bɩ n a hʊr, an doo, an aa a nyɩ bɩ yɩ, an nɩ wʊtam gargʊ burə ʊ. Zinə bɩ n bɔ a mɛ ʊ. Tikki waarɛ

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Yeezuu a hʊr Tiir kʊ ʊ, an a bɔ, an bɔ Sidɔn ʊ kan Taraburo wɔɔ kɩ nɔ ʊ, an ta Galilee higʊta zeezi.

ref: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
text: N bʊr tikki m Yeezuu zi. Tikki bɩ, bɛkka m sɔ. N n a yɛ han k'an a wɔ ka a mim la, k'an a waa.

ref: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
text: Yeezuu n a gaa zamaa bɩ jɩr ʊ, an bɔ m toore ma, an a nyʊkʊnnyɩnɔ dundu tikki bɩ tʊr ʊ, an lihi da, a nyʊkʊr ma, an a nɔ a nɛɛm ma,

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: an a bɔɔta an dɩga brama, an hoonsi bɔ a rɛ, an a hɩ m, a ʊ: «Ɛfata!»

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Yeezuu n a hɩ ŋ nɩ, a ʊ: «K'a b'a hɩ gɔsɩ nɩ y.» Yeezuu k'a lɛ ka ŋ ʊ, a ʊ: ŋ b'a hɩ bɩ, ŋ y'a pa da, an lɛ da biisi ra.

ref: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
text: Gʊɔɔ wɔɔ zɛ kədə, ŋn'a hɩrɛ, ŋ ʊ: «A ɩ hɔ haay bam mɩŋŋa; A ɩ tikkiro tʊr gʊm, a a ka bəətinno nɩ meer bam!»

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Yeezuu lɛ ka ŋ ʊ, a ʊ: «K'a dundo a zi, k'a ba laatʊ farizɛɛnrɔ nyʊnyɔɔ bɩ ma, kan Hɩrɔɔdɩ hɔ bɩ kɩ ma sɔ.»

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: A n minno nɔɔn duro nyaakʊm da an n'a hɩrɛ y. Pɩyɛɛr n a gaa an bɔ m toore ma, an nɩ nyaa bam kan Yeezuu kɩ, a ʊ: a n a ga m, an naa hɩ y.
